"No, no, look! The clothes are moving. He’s invisible!"

"Or maybe he is the clothes!"

Atlas sighed, muttering under his breath, "So we’ve summoned a ghost in beachwear?"

The invisible figure laughed. "Not a ghost, my lord! Everburn! But wait. Hehe. I have an idea to show myself!"

Atlas pinched the bridge of his nose. "Why not do that from the start?!"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 298 - Luminveil Curse
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Now, Everburn, could you make it easier for us to recognize you and show yourself ," Atlas said

The invisible figure in front of him hadn’t done anything yet. His clothes just floated there.

"Ah, haha. Yes, so... hahaha," the voice stammered.

"Come on, Burny," someone from the crowd shouted. "Show yourself! What’s wrong, are you too ugly to appear?"

Laughter broke out across the plaza.

"Yeah, maybe he doesn’t even have a face!" another added.

Atlas couldn’t help but smirk, though he folded his arms, deciding to wait and see what this strange being would do. He was just as curious as everyone else.

What kind of ability did an invisible, laughing fire spirit even have?

"Oh yes, sorry again!" the voice replied cheerfully. "I forgot to properly introduce myself, my lord!"

He continued, "My name is Everburn Legionnaire, and. Uh... I got cursed!"

Atlas raised an eyebrow. "Cursed?"

"Yes! Totally cursed!" Everburn said brightly. "My body can’t be seen by anyone anymore. Bit troublesome, isn’t it? Hahaha!"

"So you were really naked earlier?" one of the soldiers called out.

"No way. He was naked!"

"You’re shameless, Burny!" someone else teased.

"No, no, no!" Everburn quickly protested. The floating beach shorts and shirt spun around as if he’d turned to face the crowd. "Okay, yes, I’m not wearing clothes. But that doesn’t mean I’m naked! Don’t get the wrong idea, it’s not what you think! I can explain!"

"Don’t even think about taking off your clothes again, Burny."

"No, no. It’s not as dirty as it sounds!" the invisible man insisted, clearly trying to defend himself.

"How does that even make sense?"

"You literally said your body’s invisible! You’re only wearing clothes so we can tell where you are!"

"So... yeah. You were naked."

"Hahaha! How do I even explain this?!" Everburn replied, clearly flustered. "Yes, I don’t wear clothes. But I’m not naked!"

Atlas exhaled heavily, rubbing the bridge of his nose again. "Right. Alright, Everburn. How about this: instead of explaining... show us what you can do?"

Everburn straightened instantly, the air around him heating faintly. "Yes, my lord!" he said with sudden enthusiasm.

Faint embers began to flicker inside the empty floating outfit. Small tongues of flame dancing in the hollow where a body should be.

Atlas narrowed his eyes. "Everburn, what are you doing?"

A sheepish laugh came from the flickering void. "Ah. Sorry, my lord. The clothes might burn a little, but trust me. I’m not naked!"

The flames suddenly roared to life , swallowing the shirt and shorts in an instant. The crowd gasped and flinched as the fire grew brighter, hotter, until, through the blazing inferno. A shape began to emerge.

The outline of a skeleton, burning from within, stood where the invisible figure had been.

"So, hahaha!" the fiery skeleton said. "I am an undead skeleton, blessed with Fire, and master of Blaze!"

"Blaze?" Atlas muttered. That was the advanced form of Fire Element.

Everburn tilted his flaming skull slightly. "Yeah, had a bit of trouble in the past, hehe... let’s just say my skeletal frame stopped being visible. But hey, I can do something interesting!"

Someone in the crowd shouted. "Yikes, he’s actually naked!"

The skeleton chuckled. "Relax! It’s all bone and glory!"

Then, before anyone could retort, he stretched his arms wide. And the flames around his limbs shifted. The skeletal structure morphed and elongated, bones reshaping themselves into the form of fiery wings.

A heartbeat later. FWOOSH!

A wave of scorching heat exploded outward as Everburn took off, soaring upward in a burst of fire, his skeletal wings beating furiously. Trails of molten light carved through the air as the flaming skeleton spiraled high above the plaza.

"He can reshape his own bones?"

Everburn then seemed to show off in the air, spewing a massive burst of fire that blazed above them, as if trying to cover the entire sky with flames. The crowd flinched, even though they were used to dealing with fire by now.

The figure dropped down in the midst of the scattering people, landing with a laugh that echoed through the chaos. Its skeletal frame shifted again, twisting into the form of a four-legged beast. Something like a cheetah, or maybe a large cat?

Everburn dashed through the crowd, still laughing, weaving between the panicked people.

While watching this undead skeleton stir up chaos, Atlas took a moment to check the status of this new summon.

[Name: Everburn Legionnaire]

[Level: 125]

[Strength: 300 | Agility: 320 | Intelligence: 320 | Constitution: 210 | Animus: 311]

[Job: Specialized Fighter]

[Luminveil Curse (SSS) - Morphic Reconstruction (SS) - Cheerful Undeath (B) - Bone Cracker Symphony (B) - Eternal Storyteller (C) - Blaze Pressure Control (S) - Ember Reforging (S)]

Level 125? That was even higher than Orren. And Everburn had fairly balanced stats, plus he was classified as a Specialized Fighter. Interesting.

And just like Dullorak, Everburn also had the Animus stat instead of Stamina. And yes, there were now two elite undead subordinates among them. Not a bad thing at all, since people had grown quite used to creatures like them by now.

One thing Atlas noticed about his talents was the presence of one SSS-rated talent, and another rated SS, while the rest were a mix of S, B, and C. Truly unique and exceptional. Atlas let out a quiet chuckle as he read through it.

[Luminveil Curse: Body remains eternally invisible under natural light. Only heat shimmer and flame outline reveal presence. Grants passive evasion.]

[Morphic Reconstruction: Skeletal structure can be freely reconfigured into any anatomical shape. Strength, mobility, and mass adapt to current form.]

[Cheerful Undeath: Maintains stable emotional aura that counteracts fear effects around other undead or mortals.]

[Bone Cracker Symphony: Produces rhythmic clacking noises by snapping its own joints. Entirely cosmetic.]

[Eternal Storyteller: Capable of recounting thousands of deaths and rebirths with comedic timing.]

[Blaze Pressure Control: Compresses inner flame to detonate in controlled bursts, enhancing strikes or jumps with explosive propulsion.]

[Ember Reforging: Upon destruction, remaining flames coalesce into a new skeletal form after a short delay. Limited to once per day.]

So, Everburn really could go completely invisible, and had full control over both Fire and Blaze. On top of that, he could reshape his skeletal frame into any form he desired.

That might be exactly why he was placed under the Specialized Fighter classification. He could adapt his form to suit all kinds of combat situations. He could fly, shift into a beast for fast attacks, or maybe even take on a tank-like form. Truly, a highly versatile unit.

But perhaps the most remarkable one of his talents was Ember Reforging. It was similar to Phoenix Mockery, like Serenith’s. A talent that essentially allowed him to rise again if he was completely destroyed. Though, in this case, it was clearly stated that he could only do it once per day.

Well... not bad at all. Everburn should also make an excellent assassin and scout, especially with his ability to become completely invisible. Though that did mean he had to remove his clothes to do it.

But really, who cares if he’s not wearing anything? He’s an undead skeleton. No one should be seeing a naked skeleton as something indecent. Right?

With this undead skeleton’s ability to fly, Atlas now had another unit capable of engaging in aerial combat. It was a truly remarkable power boost, and something that definitely needed more focus moving forward in Gacha Haven.

A truly unique summon, and one that would be a perfect match for Brigid in Team Inferno. Both of them were fire-aligned and would complement each other well.

Everburn had now returned to the stage, this time wearing another set of relaxed beachwear, and his flames had died down once more.

"My lord, I really love this place! They’re so enthusiastic," he said. "Just look at their faces. They love fire."

"Shut up, Burny! You burned my clothes!" someone shouted.

"Stay away from me with that bare body of yours!"

Atlas gave a small nod before turning back to the others. "Let’s give another warm welcome to our new friends tonight. Orren and Everburn. Now, please join the others."

"Burny!" Everburn said cheerfully. "I’m okay with that name, haha. New life, new name, that’s fine."

"Shut up, Burny! You’re undead, you don’t even have a life, hahaha!"

"Ha! Yes, you’re spot on! Perks of being dead. I never have to pay taxes again!" The invisible skeleton burst out laughing, then leapt down from the stage and joined the rest.

So, he didn’t mind being called Burny. That would definitely make things easier, since Everburn was a bit too long and dramatic to say every time.

"Burny," Atlas added once more. "Over there in the corner is Brigid and Team Inferno. I’m sure you’ll fit right in with them. And I’m confident you can bring something valuable to that team."

"Alright, my lord!" the skeleton replied loudly.

It was truly a fiery night. Two fire summons and one fire item. Could the next Rank-A ticket really bring out another fire-based reward? No way, right?

Without wasting another moment, Atlas reached out and touched the purple ticket floating in the air.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 299 - Silver Hawk Umbra
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Atlas took a step back as a swirl of green energy burst from the purple ticket floating in the air. Then... several vines began to sprout from the ticket, their roots twisting and curling until the ticket itself crumbled apart.

Oh... could this be a new character?

Usually, when the effect was this elaborate, it was either a new character or an important item.

The vines kept growing, stretching out even farther. Until finally, something appeared in the center. A cuboid-shaped object, brownish in color, like a wooden box. And just like that, the vines vanished.

So, it wasn’t a character after all. Atlas gave a faint smile. Guess it would’ve been a bit much to expect three characters to drop in a single gacha session.

[You have received Rank-A Item: Guardian Seed Cuboid]

[An artifact rumored to hold four variant seeds once used to grow living fortresses.]

Atlas reached out and took hold of the cuboid, which turned out to be a wooden box. He opened the lid and frowned slightly when he saw four compartments inside, each filled with small, bean-shaped seeds, dozens in each container.

He lifted his gaze and turned toward someone. "Mira, come."

The bunny girl leapt swiftly, landing on the stage in an instant.

"The description says these can grow into living fortresses," Atlas said, still eyeing the seeds, "though I’m sure it’ll take some time for us to actually grow them. So you’ll hold on to part of them, and I’ll give the rest to Gwen to see what she can figure out."

"Alright, my lord..." she replied eagerly.

Atlas handed the wooden box to Mira, planning to visit the elf girl Gwen after the Lord Battle season was over.

Seeds that could grow into living fortresses? That already sounded intriguing just from the description alone. And even though the box held quite a lot of seeds, they probably wouldn’t be anywhere near as extraordinary as the Luminaria Grove.

Mira hopped back down as the night continued. There were still twenty tickets left for Atlas to open. Next up, he called forward someone else, a man walking toward the stage with a nervous, almost panicked expression.

Milo was already on stage when Atlas handed him ten silver-colored tickets.

Milo held the tickets in both hands and turned to face the crowd with a wide grin, shaking them dramatically like a deck of cards.

"Milo, just throw the tickets, no need for theatrics!" someone shouted.

"Haha, yeah Milo, we’re not here for a magic show. We can’t wait!"

"Oh, yes, sorry!" Milo grinned sheepishly, then tossed the tickets up into the air.

BROWN. BROWN... one after another, the tickets flashed and turned brown, until the ninth. Finally, one turned purple. Only a single Rank-A ticket.

"My lord... I’m sorry, only one Rank-A," Milo said, his face tight with anxiety.

"No, it’s alright. We’re here to enjoy the night. Have fun," Atlas replied with a calm smile.

Milo gave a grateful nod and stepped down from the stage.

Atlas quickly opened the nine Rank-B tickets, storing everything directly into his inventory. Then, he turned his attention to the one purple ticket floating before him.

"Let’s see what other surprises tonight has in store," Atlas said, smiling.

"FIRE!!!" the crowd shouted in unison.

Atlas reached out and touched the purple ticket, then stepped back a bit as a sudden gust of wind swept out from it. The ticket vanished into a swirl of dark energy, disappearing in an instant.

Narrowing his eyes, Atlas watched closely as the transformation began. A sound of wings flapping echoed through the air, followed by a stronger gust of wind.

Then, something emerged from the shadows. A large bird, easily two or three times the size of a typical eagle.

[You have received Rank-A Beast: Silver Hawk Umbra]

[A spirit beast born of wind and shadow, its metallic feathers blur into transparency as it soars, leaving violet trails of mist in its wake. Silent and swift, it hunts unseen, striking with precision before fading back into the darkness of the sky.]

The bird’s feathers shimmered in silver, though the energy that cloaked it gave off a darker hue. It flew high into the air, circling above the crowd with grace and power.

Wind and shadow? This one looked like the perfect candidate for a scout or a stealth-based tracker. The bird gave a sharp cry overhead, then dove downward, slicing through the air before landing gently on Atlas’s outstretched right arm.

"Hey, welcome to Gacha Haven. I already know who you’ll be perfect for," he said, glancing toward Mira among the others. With a small motion, he let the bird take off again, gliding toward the bunny girl.

"Mira, take the bird with you," he said.

Mira lit up, joy sparkling in her eyes as she hugged the spirit beast gently. It was an ideal match. Mira’s team already had plenty of beastkin and was made up of fast-moving fighters and assassins. A spirit beast like this would be right at home among them.

Nice. The night was still young. Time for one more round.

Atlas called the next person to the stage, a girl with crimson hair and a tight-fitting red outfit. Serenith.

Serenith eagerly accepted the 10 standard tickets from Atlas, her eyes sparkling with excitement as she cheerfully tossed them into the air. All eyes turned upward, watching with anticipation as the tickets began to shift color one by one.

PURPLE!

"Fire!"

Wow, the very first ticket was already purple. Could this be something amazing?

PURPLE.

"FIRE!"

Another one? No way.

BROWN. BROWN. BROWN.

Two Rank-A tickets already. That was well above average. Atlas kept his expectations in check.

But then...

BROWN. PURPLE.

"FIRE!"

A third Rank-A ticket? Atlas couldn’t help but feel a surge of excitement.

BROWN. BROWN.

One last ticket remained, trembling slightly in the air as if holding back its surprise. No way... could it really be?

Then, the ticket flashed RED!

"FIRE!!!!" the crowd roared, their cheers deafening with excitement and disbelief.

Three Rank-A tickets and one Rank-S! This was a true jackpot. A Rank-S pull coming this early, right at the 40th draw. Far earlier than the guaranteed Rank-S milestone.

Serenith looked overjoyed, practically bouncing with excitement at her unbelievable pull. Lyrassa had gotten something incredible too before, and now Serenith had followed it up with her own stroke of luck.

The red-haired girl stepped off the stage with a huge grin, and Atlas swiftly opened the six Rank-B tickets, storing their contents straight into his inventory.

This was shaping up to be an absolutely legendary session. No doubt about it.

Atlas reached out and touched the first purple ticket, curiosity buzzing in his chest. Seriously, three Rank-A pulls and one Rank-S? This had to lead to something extraordinary.

The ticket trembled, and a burst of blinding light exploded from its core.

Light? That was new. Most summons so far had been aligned with shadow or dark elements. This felt different and unusual.

Then, something began to take shape within the light. The glowing figure started to move, bouncing lightly in midair, its body still wrapped in a radiant aura. But as it shifted and landed, its outline became clearer.

It had four legs.

Another beast?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 300 - Aether Fox Lumis
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The glowing figure slowly settled down in front of Atlas, and as the light began to fade, its true form came into view. It was a fox. Sleek, graceful, with immaculate white fur that shimmered faintly, bathed in a bluish aura that looked like condensed mana.

[You have received Rank-A Beast: Aether Fox Lumis]

[A radiant spirit fox born from pure mana streams. It channels ambient mana into its allies through its Aether Bloom Aura, steadily restoring their energy and soothing their spirits during prolonged battles.]

The fox looked almost ethereal, with three elegant tails swaying gently behind it. Atlas crouched down and gently stroked the creature’s head.

"Hi. Welcome to Gacha Haven," he said softly. "So you can buff allies with mana regeneration?"

He glanced toward the crowd, already knowing who this spirit beast would suit best. His mind went to Team Blaze, with Kaida and Lyrassa. But also to Team Hydros, led by Dullorak and Selvara. Between the two, Team Hydros hadn’t received anything from the gacha pulls yet. So the choice was clear.

Atlas turned to the Hydros team, where Dullorak was currently trying on another set of bizarre heads Atlas couldn’t quite identify. With a quiet smile, he decided to call Selvara up to the stage instead.

The fox wasted no time, leaping gracefully toward the woman. Selvara knelt down in awe, utterly mesmerized by the fox’s radiant beauty. Even those standing nearby looked enchanted, their eyes wide with admiration.

The more spirit beasts they gathered, the more valuable it became. Especially since they could form bound contracts with them. That would only enhance the synchronicity between allies during battles.

Nice. The first Rank-A pull turned out to be something truly exceptional. With how many magic users were in Team Hydros, this would no doubt boost their overall performance by a lot.

Atlas didn’t waste a moment and moved right on. This was going to be a long night. He touched the next purple ticket, and all eyes turned toward him, eager to see what would appear next.

Suddenly, the ticket began to grow in size.

Huh... that was new.

Then, it started to roll up on itself, forming into a neat scroll with a shimmer of magical energy swirling around it.

A skill scroll?

A Rank-A skill scroll?

Skill scrolls were usually part of Rank-B pulls. So if this one was showing up in a Rank-A ticket, then it had to be something special.

[You have received Rank-A Skill: Eternal Glacier Domain]

[Unique-grade (Ice Element Mage) – Eternal Glacier Domain: Creates a massive frozen biome for 30 seconds. All enemy abilities are slowed, while allies gain immunity to heat and fire.]

Atlas took the scroll into his hand and read through the description carefully. Unique-grade. Now that was impressive. The only downside was the strict requirement: the user had to be a Mage subclass and possess an ice element affinity.

He looked around the crowd, mentally going over the people he knew had Mage subclass potential. A few names came to mind. But none of them currently had strong ice affinity. That narrowed his choices quickly.

Turning back to the group, Atlas explained the nature of the skill and how powerful it could be in battle, especially with its large area-of-effect and dual offensive-defensive use. It was ideal for turning the tide during drawn-out fights.

Dullorak could probably make good use of this skill. But Atlas knew the man already had more abilities than he realistically needed. Giving this one to him would only bury it beneath a pile of others.

Selvara came to mind once again. She already had a strong affinity with water, and with some focused training, she might be able to develop her connection to ice. She hadn’t trained with ice yet, but it was definitely within reach.

This scroll might be better saved for her, once she was ready.

Next Rank-A ticket!

The tension in the air grew with each step closer to the Rank-S revealing. Atlas reached out and touched the purple ticket floating before him.

A vortex suddenly appeared, pulling the ticket inward. Until a small, pitch-black cube formed in its place.

[You have received Rank-A Item: Void Storage Cube]

A Void Storage Cube? Atlas grabbed the item and quickly scanned the description:

[100 cubic dimensional inventory container.]

100 cubic? That was enormous. Ten times larger than the inventory storage Atlas currently has.

On the island, dimensional storage was something most people could access easily. But a portable version, this size? That was a different level entirely. Having this much mobile storage would be incredibly practical, especially for expeditions into the Lower Lands. They could carry full-sized vehicles, maybe even a bus inside it.

Without hesitation, Atlas signaled toward Edrik and tossed the cube to him.

"Dimensional storage. Size: 100 cubic," he explained.

Gasps rippled through the crowd. Of course, they were stunned. Even most lords only had 10 cubic storage. And for normal awakeners, owning dimensional storage at all was rare. The few portable items that did exist were typically just 1 cubic, and even those came with a hefty price.

So the value of this item? It wasn’t just high. It was absolutely immense.

Alright, finally, the moment had come.

Atlas stood before the glowing red ticket. His pulse steady, his mind ready. This was the Rank-S pull.

He knew it could be anything: a signature weapon, maybe something specifically made for Lyrassa, Raze, or even Dullorak. That alone would be a massive upgrade for their combat capabilities.

Or it could be a legendary-grade weapon, something universal, usable by anyone. That would be just as valuable.

Then there was the chance it could be a spirit beast, and if so, it would almost certainly be something of immense power, like Zefyros had been.

And of course, the ultimate jackpot, a new character. Rank-S characters had always proven to be powerful, essential pieces with incredible growth potential and impact.

Atlas took a slow, measured breath and reached out, placing his hand on the radiant red ticket. It shimmered under his touch. Then, he stepped back, giving room for whatever might appear.

Then... the ticket began to tremble violently, a powerful gust of wind suddenly blasting out from its center. Atlas took another step back. It was clear this was far from over.

A loud bzzzz tore through the air, high-pitched and resonant, the wind now whipping around the area with increasing force. The sound vibrated in every direction, sharp enough to sting in the ears.

Could it really be... a new character?

Suddenly, blades of wind slashed outward, tearing through the leaves and cutting them clean in half. One streak shot toward the crowd, slicing clean through the front row’s table and sending people scrambling to their feet in surprise.

And then... a swirling vortex of green energy gathered in the center of the stage. Within it, a transparent figure began to materialize. Tall, nearly the same height as Karian.

The figure gradually became solid. The first feature that stood out was the armor: dark, hardened plating with glowing green. Parts of the body were exposed beneath the armor, pulsing with a vibrant emerald energy.

Its eyes glowed the same green, and its mouth, also lit with that eerie glow—opened slightly, giving off an unsettling hum. Two antennae protruded from its head, and on its back... something buzzed violently.

Insect-like wings.

The figure fully formed and stood tall before Atlas, its wings vibrating with a chilling rhythm.

Another aerial unit!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 301 - The Green Tempest
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[You have received Rank-S Character: Krythalis - The Green Tempest]

Atlas observed the figure standing before him, quietly studying the details. He couldn’t immediately tell whether the man was a beastkin or simply someone clad in intricate armor. But the more he looked, the clearer it became. His eyes were too natural, and the mouth, if that was a mouth, glowed faintly with life, not machinery.

Still, if this was an insect-type beastkin, it didn’t answer the biggest question: what kind of insect? He had wings. Two pairs, like a bee or wasp. Two antennae curled from his head. Yet his form, heavily armored and imposing, didn’t quite match anything familiar. Maybe a mantis type? Or a hornet?

Before Atlas could dwell further, the figure seemed to register what had just happened, realizing he’d been summoned. The area around them remained quiet, the crowd holding their breath, curiosity hanging heavy in the air. Unlike the chaos that had come with the last two summons, this one felt composed and measured.

The figure dropped to one knee before Atlas. And even kneeling, he was still tall. One hand pressed against his chest, and his voice rang out, strong and unwavering, with a tone of deep respect:

"I once stood as a lord. Failed in battle, defeated, brought down by my own misjudgment. In that fall, I lost everything: my people, my floating islands, my name. I should have vanished with them.

"Yet here I stand, granted a second chance. I know my past has ended... but I am not yet finished.

"Though the strength I now hold is different from before, I pledge it all to you. Every breath, every strike, every wingbeat, will serve your command.

"I am of the Wasp-type, an Aerial Ravager. My domain is the sky. I am bound to Air, Wind, Storm, and Lightning. I specialize in aerial warfare. The skies belong to me, and under my wings, I can summon an aerial legion and dominate the battlefield above."

Gasps rippled through the crowd. The way he spoke, it was like Edrik’s polish, but rawer, more honest. Where Edrik’s words often felt too smooth, too well-rehearsed, this man spoke with conviction, and it struck deep.

Atlas gave a faint smile and nodded once.

"Welcome. I hope Gacha Haven becomes a place that meets your expectations. And I look forward to what we can build together, with you, and everyone who calls this island home.

"This is a Rank-2 floating island for now... but with the strength of our elite subordinates, our summons, and our growing forces. We are chasing something far greater.

"Now rise, Krythalis."

The beastkin stood tall once more, his form solid, unwavering, and striking in its intensity. Around them, not a single voice rose to mock or jest, none dared. Not like they had with the last two summons.

Atlas turned to face the crowd, raising his voice just enough to carry across the gathering. He introduced the new summon, and the response was immediate. Thunderous applause and loud cheers rang out.

It was rare. Even the last Rank-S summon hadn’t been welcomed like this. In fact, from the moment Dullorak had arrived, the jokes had started flying.

After the applause settled, Atlas asked Krythalis to demonstrate his abilities. The beastkin nodded once, then beat his dual pairs of wings in a rapid blur. With a sharp bzzzt, he shot into the air. So fast it left a ripple in the air behind him.

His speed was stunning, possibly on par with Serenith’s flight. Maybe even faster?

In a straight race, Krythalis might edge her out. But if Serenith kicked in her boosters, she’d probably still win in sheer acceleration. Still, his agility in the sky was unmatched. And now, with his lightning affinity, he would synergize well with Atlas himself.

The skies above darkened without warning. The wind began swirling violently. A crack of thunder echoed overhead, followed by spiraling air currents forming into mini tornadoes that tore across the sky.

Whoa...

Everyone watched, wide-eyed, as Krythalis unleashed his power.

Then, with a flick of his wings, he launched a volley of wind-like needles. Sharp and fast, slicing through the air with terrifying precision. It was clear now: this man could absolutely dominate the aerial battlefield against large forces.

Just then, Serenith hovered nearby, landing gracefully beside Atlas.

"The buffs he provides... They boost aerial fighters. Speed, and stamina," she said.

Next, Krythalis raised his arm, and the air shimmered.

A buzzing, coordinated hum filled the sky as he summoned an insect-type aerial legion. Smaller units swarming in formation. There were dozens: sleek Mantis-Hornet hybrids, bulky Rhinoceros Beetle variants, and deadly Wasp-Bee hybrids. Each group looked like it had its own combat specialty.

It was clear these weren’t just showpieces, they were a real tactical force.

Atlas took a moment to inspect Krythalis’s detailed status, and what he saw only deepened his impression.

[Name: Krythalis]

[Level: 132]

[Strength: 302 | Agility: 530 | Intelligence: 298 | Constitution: 298 | Stamina: 360]

[Job: Specialized Fighter]

[Aether-Linked Hive Consciousness (S) - Empyrean Rebirth Instinct (SS) - Bladewing Anatomy (S) - Storm-Reading Reflex (SS) - War Scholar Mindset (S) - Observation Discipline (S) - Loyal Construct Code (A)]

The level? 132.

Higher than any other summon currently on the island. Far above the average.

Even more telling was the stat allocation. It was a clear, deliberate build, full agility. Everything about him was optimized for speed, movement, and swift, decisive attacks in the air. He was built to be a skyborne assassin, meant to tear through large groups of enemies with precision and overwhelming speed.

As for talents, while there were no SSS-rated ones, almost every single one sat firmly above S. That alone made it a solid, high-tier combination.

[Aether-Linked Hive Consciousness: Shares perception and sensory feedback across all nearby drones and constructs simultaneously.]

[Empyrean Rebirth Instinct: When critically damaged, the body sheds its outer layer and detonates in radiant wind, leaving behind a cocoon that reforms once ambient mana stabilizes.]

[Bladewing Anatomy: Wing edges harden into mono-molecular blades capable of slashing through reinforced materials when charged.]

[Storm-Reading Reflex: Nervous system detects micro-changes in pressure, predicting enemy movement before visual confirmation.]

[War Scholar Mindset: Catalogs every opponent’s movement style for future encounters, building predictive combat models.]

[Observation Discipline: Rarely engages without full data; studies weather, terrain, and enemy rhythm before committing.]

[Loyal Construct Code: Programmed with immutable loyalty; prioritizes protection of the summoner’s aerial domain above self-preservation.]

And there was one particular talent that caught Atlas’s attention: a revival mechanic. It wasn’t quite as miraculous as Phoenix Mockery, like the one Serenith possessed, but it gave Krythalis a second chance. A failsafe, letting him recover from devastating damage. Another layer of survivability to an already elite aerial warrior.

40 tickets were spent, and one Rank-S successfully pulled early . A jackpot. The celebration that night slowly drew to a close with laughter, music, and the kind of electric energy that only a legendary pull could stir.

But for Atlas, the night was far from over.

After the festivities, he gathered all of his elite subordinates, without exception. Alongside them, the squad leaders and other key figures within Gacha Haven, all were summoned to a council.

Two challenges had already been issued to Atlas.

And with only one day left in the Lord Battle Season, the enemy islands were already drawing close. If those challenges were accepted, there wouldn’t be long before battle would begin.

Preparations had to be made. Now!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 302 - Fengyun Terrace
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Since three new characters had arrived at the same time, Atlas ended up with more Elite Subordinates than he needed to lead the existing combatant squads in Gacha Haven.

At that time, there were a total of 454 combatants divided into eight squads, each with its own squad leader and supervisor. Every squad had around fifty or more members, with well-balanced roles. Especially between tankers and support healers.

Although, some teams had their own unique specialties. For example, Team Eclipse, led by Veylor and Mira, focused more on assassins and close-range fighters.

Existing Squads:

[Team Aether – Zara and Milo]

[Team Blaze – Kaida and Lyrassa]

[Team Cauldron – Garen and Karian]

[Team Dusk – Ronan and Orren]

[Team Eclipse – Veylor and Mira]

[Team Flame – Kazan and Serenith]

[Team Hydros – Selvara and Dullorak]

[Team Inferno – Brigid and Everburn]

Atlas had also made several personnel changes and rebranded Team Cinder into Team Cauldron, where Garen would now work together with Karian.

The other elite subordinates, Edrik and Kurogasa, as usual, were assigned as dependable guards to stay close to Atlas. Meanwhile, Baldric would serve as backup support. Krythalis was also set to remain on standby, handling the battle in his own way for the time being. He was more than capable of managing things independently.

As for Morganna, as always, it was best to let her carry out her role however she wished. Even though she tended to do everything in unpredictable ways, the fact that she still followed Atlas’s directions showed something deeper about her loyalty and understanding of his intent. Moreover, she was also keeping Vienne safe by her side, which, in truth, added a touch of pressure on Atlas’s mind.

Later, he planned to form new teams under other elite subordinates once they gained more combatants. His short-term goal was to expand the number of total combatants to one thousand.

Among everyone in Gacha Haven, only a few elite subordinates were capable of aerial battle. Morganna and Serenith could fight in the air, while Lyrassa was able to do so but mainly in a support role.

Dullorak could also fly by using one of his heads that granted him this ability. Everburn was able to transform into a flying creature, and Krythalis, one of the new elite subordinates, was a true aerial combat specialist.

Kurogasa, although unable to fly, was agile enough to use his surroundings to launch himself into the air, leaping toward his opponents and combining his skills to survive midair. He was still a strong contender despite lacking wings.

Mira could also engage in aerial combat thanks to her incredible jumping power, which allowed her to stay airborne by using anything around her as a foothold. Even her enemy’s body.

As for those who could actually fly, there were seventeen combatants at that point.

Atlas did not yet have a fully established aerial unit, but with the forces he already had, it was more than enough to handle any airborne battle for the time being.

In addition, he had already set up defensive structures across the jagged terrain surrounding the outer edges of Gacha Haven. These fortifications helped deliver attacks more effectively against aerial units that came within range. Although he still needed more time to build additional structures, the current setup was already a great improvement compared to before.

As the floating island grew larger, maintaining a proper defense became increasingly complex since enemies could approach from any direction. This presented a weakness of its own, yet also offered a strategic advantage because Atlas had a substantial number of ground combatants capable of holding their positions when opponents descended to engage in ground battles.

That very night, Atlas gathered everyone for a detailed discussion about their next defense battle. They began by choosing which challenger they would accept, carefully considering the compatibility of each team and elite subordinate involved.

During that meeting, Orren, Everburn, and Krythalis openly declared that they were ready and willing to fight immediately if necessary. It was no surprise, since all three had far more experience in lord battles than Atlas did in their previous lives. Battle was their way of living, something they had endured dozens, hundreds, or even thousands of times, especially those who had reached higher ranks.

And so, that night, Atlas accepted the battle challenge.

[Atlas of Gacha Haven, Scout Division 1, has accepted the defensive challenge from Bai Yun of Fengyun Terrace, Scout Division 1.]

[The battle will begin in: 06:00:00]

**

They had a few hours to rest, for when the morning sun began to shine over the entire floating island, the battle would soon begin.

Everyone only had a light breakfast. Just enough to give them the energy they needed for the fight ahead, which was set to take place shortly after sunrise.

Atlas had already summoned his floating throne, with Edrik and Kurogasa standing behind him. But that morning, a new presence joined them, Krythalis. His dark shell gleamed under the sunlight, reflecting a faint metallic glow as he stood beside Atlas.

The floating throne rose swiftly into the sky, soaring higher and higher before speeding across the jagged terrain that formed the island’s outer boundary. Along those cliffs, several combatants were already stationed, ready to defend their posts.

Before long, the throne reached the island’s outermost edge. In the distance, the opponent’s floating island came into clear view, its form gliding steadily closer, as if to announce the arrival of its master.

Atlas waited there for a moment, seated on his throne with one leg crossed over the other, clad in his full armor and ready to face the approaching enemy. He knew they would appear soon. Most likely with their usual loud declarations and taunts meant to provoke before the real battle began.

Then, from the direction of the opposing island, something emerged swiftly into the air. Atlas narrowed his eyes as the enemy troops, dressed in light armor and blue robes, shot forward, each of them standing upon their flying blades.

There were many of them. Dozens, then hundreds, appeared in quick succession, rising higher and soaring toward the upper area of Gacha Haven, right above its protective dome.

Edrik spoke first. "So, they’re confident enough to reveal their forces even before the battle begins."

Atlas responded with a faint smile.

Krythalis then stepped forward. "Allow me to handle them all on my own, my lord," he said calmly.

Atlas glanced at him. "You’re confident you can take them all by yourself, Kryth? Remember, there are others here who can fly as well."

Krythalis bowed deeply, placing a hand over his chest. "Forgive me if I appear arrogant, my lord. That is not my intention. I merely wish to demonstrate my full strength before you. If I fail to subdue them, I will retreat and leave the rest in your command."

Atlas’s expression softened. "No, it’s alright. You may handle them, all of them. How many are there?"

"Two hundred and forty visible, my lord," Krythalis replied with confidence.

Atlas nodded slightly and kept his gaze fixed on the opposing island, waiting for another presence to emerge. Soon enough, someone shot out from the enemy’s floating island, moving swiftly through the air. Unlike the others, this one did not ride a flying blade. Could it be their Lord?

The figure drew closer. A young man with long black hair tied neatly at the back, clad in light silver armor layered with a flowing blue robe.

Yes, this had to be their Lord.

Bai Yun.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 303 - The Sky Burns First
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Bai Yun, the opposing lord, could fly without using those flying blades. That made him a difficult opponent if Atlas had to face him alone, especially since Atlas did not have any special ability to fly. There were, of course, a few ways Atlas could try to fight an aerial opponent like this, but none of them were as simple as fighting an opponent on the ground.

The long black-haired man crossed his arms and lifted his chin. "I am rather impressed you had the nerve to answer this challenge, Atlas," he said.

Atlas shrugged and replied, "I am also impressed you kept pressing this challenge even after I had won two all-out confrontations and one defense. Do you honestly think those wins were only because of support from higher ranks?"

The man smirked at Atlas’s words. "I will admit you have good potential. You climbed the ranks fast, and your victories were striking. But I think that streak has closed your eyes to caution. You are up against an alliance far stronger than you. I suppose you have already lost the chance to step back. You could ignore my challenge and leave with only the shame of your people. But I suspect you would rather choose destruction than shame."

"You have no right to lecture me about shame, Bai Yun, when you yourself admit you have support from your higher ranks. Can you not fight on your own?"

The man’s smile tightened. "Did you expect to meet a lord who was naive enough to rush forward without thinking? Power, backing, alliances, do you think they exist for no reason? They are there to crush the naive like you. Those who think that winning once against weak opponents makes them untouchable fall right into the trap. You have walked into that trap. You will never repay them, because this will be your last breath, ATLAS. You will be destroyed. Mark my words: you will be obliterated, and you will not even have the chance to surrender."

"Your threats really scare me," Atlas answered in a flat tone.

"I will destroy you completely!" Bai Yun continued. "I will leave none of your people alive. I will wipe everything out. Just let me take your islands. I will make this battle a punishment for the wrong choices of everyone who stood under your banner. You are clearly too early for these lord battles. War is not won by brute force alone, you need brains."

Atlas gave a small, dry laugh. "How ironic to hear that from someone who admits he has reinforcements from his higher ranks. You try to scare me and all you make me want to do is laugh until I cry."

The man narrowed his gaze. "Well, at least you’re good at believing in yourself. I’ll give you that."

The official battle was still ten minutes away, but it was clear they had gone to extra lengths to provoke this fight. Their aerial troops hovering above Gacha Haven showed how confident they were in destroying the floating island before them.

Bai Yun then flew back toward his island, and Atlas’s side finished their final preparations. They were ready to face the real battle in a few minutes.

But when the countdown reached thirty seconds something unusual happened. Or maybe it was not unusual at all. Edrik had warned them about this possibility.

About thirty of the flying blade riders gathered in one spot. They moved into a ring formation in the air and a magic pattern quickly formed between them. The formation flared with bright light as mana concentrated at its center.

"They are charging their attack to release it when the barrier opens," Edrik said.

"How cunning," Atlas said. "They do not even hide this shameful trick."

The magic formation burned brighter as mana pooled, growing more intense with every second of the countdown.

It was clear the attack was aimed straight at Atlas, who sat on his floating island.

Fifteen seconds left, and Atlas stayed where he was.

"They are using all their mana and stamina," Edrik said.

"No wonder. It is a group of thirty. The attack will be devastating," Atlas replied.

Edrik nodded slowly. "We have a few options to respond."

"Use the safest one first. Save the bigger ones to counter what comes after," Atlas answered.

As the countdown ticked down, the formation shone ever brighter and a massive sphere of magic took shape, spinning violently at its center.

Then, just as the countdown reached zero and the protective barrier opened, the great sphere readied to launch toward its target.

But before the sphere could fire from its source, something streaked in from behind Atlas. A red energy like a cannon shot flew toward the enemy with terrible speed. Time seemed to slow, then a huge blast hit.

The explosion tore at the air itself. The sound was deafening and the shockwave rolled across the island. The blast obliterated the thirty who had been charging the spell.

Serenith could always be relied on for moments like this. Her strike had become the opening drumbeat of the battle. The fight had begun in earnest now. Enemy flying troops hurried to fill the sky.

They should have used all three hundred for that same attack. Why only thirty? Had they underestimated Atlas?

Magic portals then formed from several directions, circling the entire outer edge of Gacha Haven. Besides the enemy’s ability to strike from the sky, they were now ready to send ground troops as well.

That was a good sign, because Atlas’s ground forces could join the fight. It would have been far more difficult if every enemy had stayed in the air.

Edrik, Kurogasa, and Krythalis still stood behind Atlas, waiting for the enemy to make the first move. They were meant to handle anything that appeared in the sky.

The enemy forces remained spread along the outer ring and had not fully entered Gacha Haven.

Edrik gave a small, knowing smile. "They are very cunning," he said. "They deliberately distracted us with those airborne troops."

Krythalis bowed slightly. "Should I face them as well?" he asked.

Edrik had meant that, even if the enemy was not visible, a team of assassins was flying straight toward Atlas in invisible mode, ready to strike him down.

At that moment, someone stepped forward beside Atlas and bowed. It was Kurogasa, the ninja rat. "Let me face these, my lord," he said.

Atlas nodded slowly. "Yes, I think assassins should be met by an assassin," he replied. "Let Krythalis handle the others over there."

Kurogasa straightened and stepped forward to the front of the floating throne. In his right hand, a chained blade appeared, its edge glimmering faintly as dark energy began to wrap around his entire body.

It seemed this was the first time Kurogasa had truly looked serious in the middle of a battle.

After all, today Atlas fully intended to let all of his elite subordinates show their true abilities without restraint.

Moments later, several figures appeared from every direction surrounding Atlas and the floating throne. Enemies clad in black robes and light armor, wielding swords and various weapons. There were dozens of them, attacking from above, below, left, right, front, and back.

However, at that same moment, something astonishing happened.

Kurogasa, or rather, multiple Kurogasas... burst forth from his position, each one darting toward the attackers in perfect synchronization, matching their number exactly.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 304 - The Dragon Lord Descends
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Atlas remained seated calmly on his throne as the flying assassins revealed themselves, rushing toward him all at once. He could admit that this opposing lord was strategic, he wasted no time destroying enemy structures but went straight for the lord himself, knowing that taking out the leader could quickly end the battle.

However, Kurogasa moved even faster. His shadow clones shot into the air, each one hurling the blade of his kusarigama toward the enemies. And he didn’t stop there, he kept creating more and more clones, until their numbers doubled those of the incoming assassins.

But damn, how could he control that many shadow clones at once? The precision and technique of this ninja rat were truly exceptional.

The assassins tried to defend themselves atop their flying blades, weaving and spinning to evade the incoming strikes. Yet Kurogasa was relentless. He cut through them with swift, deadly motions. One head fell, and its owner tumbled from his flying sword, then another, and another, as the assassins scattered, desperate to find which of the shadow figures was the real one.

Atlas guided his floating throne farther from the clash. Kurogasa could clearly handle this alone. To doubt even for a moment the ability of this ninja rat would have been an insult to him.

On the other side, the enemy troops hovering atop their flying swords began to move, casting their magic spells from a distance.

"I suppose this is your turn, Kryth?" Atlas murmured.

Krythalis stepped forward and gave a respectful bow toward him. "I will take on the three hundred of them, my lord."

"Alright," Atlas replied. "Let the others handle the rest."

At that moment, the carapace on Krythalis’s back opened wide, revealing two pairs of radiant, blade-like wings that shimmered with a metallic gleam. They quivered once, then beat rapidly as he launched himself from before Atlas. In the blink of an eye, he had already soared high into the air, ready to confront the enemy forces head-on.

"Facing three hundred at once?" Atlas muttered under his breath.

Edrik bowed slightly and answered, "Kryth’s power was designed for large-scale combat, it seems. This kind of battle suits him perfectly."

Atlas nodded slowly, his eyes following as Krythalis began his assault in the sky, displaying his true capability in the open heights above.

Krythalis instantly filled the area around him with a violent storm. Fierce winds howled as the sky darkened, and countless small tornadoes spun outward in every direction. Seeing this, the enemy troops quickly scattered and charged toward him from all sides.

Krythalis darted through the air, effortlessly avoiding the rain of long-range attacks aimed his way. From both of his forearms extended sharp, natural blades. Organic weapons that were part of his own body, much like the deadly forelimbs of a mantis. With each slash of his twin arm-blades, enemies were cut down, their bodies falling helplessly from their flying swords.

One of the most remarkable things about Krythalis was his ability to wield the Lightning element. As he moved, every beat of his wings sent out wind blades that sliced toward his enemies, while arcs of crackling lightning surged across the storm, striking those caught in his path.

From a distance, all that could be seen was a streak of green and gold light weaving through the dark sky. Dancing, twisting, and cleaving through opponents with impossible speed.

"This isn’t even Kryth using his insect legion yet," Atlas murmured quietly.

Right after Atlas spoke those words, more insects burst into the sky, their numbers rapidly spreading across the battlefield. The dark, shadow-colored creatures swarmed with astonishing speed, darting in every direction as they attacked the enemy forces. They were nearly impossible to track, striking from all angles and quickly breaking the enemies’ formation by forcing them to divide their attention.

Atlas shifted his floating throne once more, moving away from the area littered with falling bodies. He guided it swiftly to a higher vantage point where he could observe the battle more freely from a distance.

One thing he noticed immediately, Bai Yun, the enemy lord, still hadn’t shown himself. He was clearly calculating his every move, letting his troops fall first before making any personal appearance.

Or perhaps he had no intention of showing himself at all? If that were the case, then this massacre would continue until the enemy forces were completely annihilated by Krythalis, Kurogasa, and Serenith, who were already wreaking havoc in the distance.

Atlas suddenly noticed two figures darting through the air and landing upon his floating throne. He turned his head, recognizing Morganna and Lyrassa standing there. In Morganna’s arms was someone much smaller, Vienne, who reached out toward him with a bright, innocent smile.

"Papa!" Vienne called out cheerfully.

Atlas drew in a sharp breath, his chest tightening every time he saw Vienne brought into a battlefield like this. He had already asked Morganna countless times to leave her safely inside the fortress, under protection.

But Morganna merely loosened the wrap holding the little girl and handed her over to him.

"Why?" Atlas asked quietly.

"To join the battle," Morganna replied. "What else do you think I came here for?"

And just as she said that, several figures suddenly appeared as if emerging from the void itself, floating in the air around them and encircling the throne from a distance.

One, two, three, until there were thirty of them, spreading out in every direction to surround Atlas’s position.

Holding Vienne close against his chest, Atlas shielded her with his arms as the enemy figures began forming rapid hand seals. A moment later, they raised their hands, and glowing magic formations flared to life in their palms all at once.

"They really don’t know when to give up," Edrik said after a short breath.

"Let’s see if you can withstand this, Atlas!!" they shouted as their spells reached completion.

In the next instant, they thrust both hands forward, forming dense magic spheres that appeared and solidified in midair. Within a heartbeat, those spheres shot toward Atlas at tremendous speed.

Lyrassa reacted immediately, raising both of her hands as a protective barrier of shimmering green light appeared. Interlocking octagonal patterns spread outward in perfect symmetry, forming a shield that wrapped tightly around the floating throne.

A series of deafening explosions erupted as the enemy’s magic slammed into the barrier, each impact shaking the air. Yet, it was as if the attacks struck an unbreakable wall. Every blast simply dispersed, bursting apart upon contact with the shield.

Still, the attackers did not stop. They kept firing one spell after another, endless waves of magic raining down on the green barrier, determined to tear it apart through sheer persistence.

Morganna stepped forward to the front of the floating throne, her body now wrapped in swirling energy of dark crimson and deep shadow. In her hands, her signature weapon appeared, the Bloodkissed Scythe.

But before Morganna could launch herself forward, Atlas’s attention was drawn elsewhere, far beyond the explosions lighting up the sky. Through the smoke and chaos, he saw it: a massive azure dragon avatar twisting and coiling through the air. From its gaping maw, a lone figure floated calmly, standing at its center as though commanding the beast itself.

"I thought he’d hide until the battle was over," Atlas murmured quietly.

"Do you want the quick way, or will you face this lord yourself, my lord?" Edrik asked evenly.

Before Atlas could respond, Morganna had already dashed forward, piercing through the protective barrier like a streak of red lightning, heading straight toward the chaos outside.

Yet Edrik’s question lingered in Atlas’s mind.

Would he end this quickly... or face the flying lord, Bai Yun, with his own strength alone?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 305 - Cannonfire and Calm Eyes
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Atlas, Edrik, and Lyrassa remained sheltered behind the protective barrier cast by Lyrassa, yet even from within its safety, they could clearly see Morganna’s battle outside.

Atlas gently turned Vienne’s head away, not wanting the little girl to witness what her mother was doing.

"Mama... Mama..." Vienne called softly, struggling to look past his arm.

Atlas swallowed hard as his eyes followed the terrifying sight beyond the shield. Morganna was completely cloaked in a swirling aura of black and blood-red energy. A pair of dark wings spread wide behind her, and her body was now barely visible beneath the writhing power surrounding her.

She moved like a crimson storm, flying at incredible speed. From her body, tendrils of living darkness burst forth, rushing toward fleeing enemies and dragging them back into her reach.

More opponents appeared, casting spells from afar, desperate to stop her advance. Yet even when their magic struck her, the impact dissolved into her shadowy aura, the energy absorbed as though feeding the darkness that wrapped around her.

Atlas guided the floating throne farther away once again, giving Morganna more space to fight freely.

At the same time, Atlas received an update that the enemy’s ground forces had begun to appear as well. And their numbers were massive. They were emerging from twelve different portals scattered along the outer regions of Gacha Haven.

This would be troublesome. The ground troops of Gacha Haven consisted of only eight squads, which meant they wouldn’t be able to intercept every assault launched by the enemy.

"How many total troops are we dealing with, Edrik?" Atlas asked.

Edrik paused briefly before replying. "Roughly fifty per portal on average."

"Fifty?" Atlas frowned. "That means their total numbers are nearing the system’s maximum limit for this battle, one thousand combatants."

Edrik nodded slightly. "Considering their levels are mostly above this rank range, they should already be affected by the system’s debuff. Slower stamina and mana regeneration."

Atlas gave a faint smirk at that. "And yet he said not to approach battle with brute force alone? Then why send such a massive army if not to crush us with sheer numbers?"

Edrik didn’t comment further. Instead, he immediately began relaying orders, directing all eight squads to intercept the enemy forces and focus their defense near the routes leading toward the island’s central area.

If all went according to plan, nearly half of the invading army wouldn’t be able to advance easily, as the highlands on the far side of the island would slow their movements. That delay would give Gacha Haven’s squads enough time to confront and engage the others before the enemy could reach deeper into their territory.

Atlas turned his attention once more toward the distance, where the massive azure dragon twisted gracefully through the sky. The opposing lord, Bai Yun, floated calmly within the beast’s gaping maw, observing the battle as though it were a mere spectacle.

"Now... let’s test the fortress’ cannon," Atlas said quietly.

Edrik nodded in acknowledgment and immediately summoned the control console. His fingers moved swiftly, ensuring the targeting system locked precisely onto the enemy’s position in the distance. As long as the target remained within the range of the floating islands, it would be a safe and reachable distance for the cannon’s blast to strike.

It always felt like the system itself imposed an artificial restriction. Despite the islands being able to grow endlessly, the cannon’s attack range was still confined strictly to the battlefield zones around them. In theory, though, the shot should still reach even from far away.

A loud, rising hum echoed from the direction of the central island, building in intensity until it turned into a deep, resonating vibration that filled the air. Then, with a thunderous boom, the fortress cannon fired.

A blazing streak of energy roared through the sky, passing just beneath Atlas’s throne. Everything in its path, the jagged terrain, the floating rocks, the air itself, seemed to melt and disintegrate from the sheer force of the blast.

The shot moved faster than sound, unstoppable, and it was aimed directly at Bai Yun in the distance.

There was no way he could dodge it in time!

In the distance, a deafening roar echoed through the skies as the dragon beast bellowed, shaking the air itself. A massive magic formation materialized before Bai Yun, glowing with an intense blue light that expanded outward like a barrier.

"No way... is he really going to take that hit head-on?" Atlas muttered in disbelief.

The fortress cannon blast struck violently, detonating upon impact with a chain of massive explosions. The shockwaves rippled through the clouds, lighting the horizon in blinding white flashes. But when the blast faded, barely a second after the cannon ceased firing, Bai Yun still floated there, calm and untouched, alongside his dragon.

"Did he just withstand that attack?" Atlas spoke quietly.

"More like..." Edrik replied. "That’s his system ability, my lord."

"I see," Atlas responded.

"Most likely an ability that nullifies or negates certain forms of damage. Perhaps magic-based attacks or long-range blasts," Edrik continued. "From what I observed, the explosion we saw wasn’t from the cannon itself, but from the impact against his magic formation. The blast was completely nullified upon contact."

Atlas exhaled slowly, his gaze narrowing. "Then firing again would be a waste of resources," he said. "Though I’m certain he won’t be able to nullify an attack of that scale multiple times in a row."

Edrik bowed slightly before speaking again. "Should I handle him, my lord?"

Atlas lifted Vienne into his arms, gazing at the bright, eager expression on the little girl’s face. "No," he said softly. "Let me use this as a way to progress myself."

He smiled at her then, brushing a strand of her hair aside. "You’ll stay here with Uncle Edrik, alright?"

"No..." Vienne protested immediately at the mention of Edrik’s name.

"There’s also Mama Lyra here," Atlas said reassuringly. "You’ll be safe with her, okay?"

"Mama Leela..." Vienne repeated, giggling afterward.

Atlas chuckled under his breath, then gently set the little girl down on the throne. She looked delighted, her tiny legs bouncing slightly as if she wanted to leap even though she was still seated.

With that, Atlas summoned his Wavebreaker Spear, and the figure of Zefyros appeared above, descending swiftly to land on the platform of the floating throne with a resounding impact.

"Are you ready, Zefyros?" Atlas asked.

KREEEAAAKKKK!

The creature’s cry echoed in answer.

Atlas gave a firm nod, then turned to Lyrassa. "I’ll be needing your support."

Lyrassa smiled warmly and bowed gracefully. "I will not let you lose your footing even for a moment, my lord."

Atlas looked back at Vienne one last time, gently patting her head. "I’ll be back soon."

Then, facing the distant enemy once more, his body became shrouded in a swirling aura of water and golden lightning. In the next instant, he merged with Zefyros, the fusion transforming the outward appearance of his armor.

Atlas gritted his teeth and tightened his grip around his spear. At the same time, a series of glowing octagonal platforms materialized in the air before him, stretching far into the distance like stepping stones of green light.

A small, confident smile crossed his face. He bent his knees slightly, then leapt forward, landing firmly on the first platform. The moment his foot touched it, he propelled himself to the next, and then the next, moving faster each time until his figure became a blur darting through the air.

"Let’s see," he murmured under his breath, his eyes fixed on the distant figure of Bai Yun. "Just how much of my magic can you really nullify?"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 306 - The Dragon and the Storm
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Atlas shot through the air at incredible speed, closing the distance toward his enemy in the far distance. He combined the explosive power of his Mountain Leaper Bands with the green stepping platforms Lyrassa created beneath him, using both in perfect rhythm to propel himself forward.

He needed to finish this battle quickly, before more lives were lost on either side.

The massive azure dragon that had been coiling around Bai Yun suddenly broke away, its immense body twisting as it began to gather energy in its gaping jaws. A brilliant light flared inside its mouth, signaling an energy blast about to be unleashed straight at Atlas.

Atlas pushed himself faster, but the dragon was quicker. Several glowing magic spheres fired from its mouth in rapid succession, streaking toward him like shooting stars.

"Damn it!" Atlas cursed, veering sharply to the side. Explosions erupted in the air around him, shockwaves rippling through his armor. He had only a split second each time to react.

More and more magic spheres followed, converging on him from all angles. He could only rely on Lyrassa’s support from afar, her nature energy shaping footholds beneath him just in time to keep him from losing momentum in his mad dash through the storm of blasts.

Atlas darted right, then left, another explosion bursting close enough to rattle his balance. He rose higher into the sky, hovering for a brief moment before a new volley came. Without hesitation, he dove straight down, slamming his foot onto another green platform, and launched himself forward again with explosive speed.

The air split around him as he surged ahead, lightning trailing in golden arcs around his armor.

The distance between him and Bai Yun was shrinking fast. He was almost there!

[Unique – Stormcall Domain (Active Skill) activated.]

[Epic – Storm Chain: Judgment Spear (Conditional Active Skill) activated.]

The skies above darkened in an instant, thunder roaring as lightning split the heavens. Bolts of pure energy rained down in rapid succession, and in that same heartbeat, they converged into Atlas’s spear.

The weapon blazed with divine light, crackling with the power of a raging storm. In mere fractions of a second, he would be within striking distance. His first blow ready to land.

But Bai Yun only smiled arrogantly. The man’s expression was calm, confident. Then, with fluid precision, he darted backward, his body twisting through the air as he weaved between the crashing bolts of lightning that shattered the sky around him.

The azure dragon roared again, its massive body gliding through the storm as it spiraled away from the danger zone, energy coursing along its scales.

Atlas pressed forward, chasing him relentlessly. Lightning burst from his armor, propelling him faster as he aimed to strike, but Bai Yun moved even faster. His speed exploded in a surge of blue energy as he shot upward, leaving Atlas trailing behind.

Within moments, Atlas realized he had left the range of his Stormcall Domain, and as he crossed that invisible boundary, the storm faded. The power surrounding his body dissipated, and his skill deactivated.

"Hah!" Bai Yun’s mocking laughter echoed through the air. "Did you really think I would stay still and let you strike me so easily? I’m not an opponent you can defeat with such foolish tricks, Atlas!"

The azure dragon’s roar thundered once again, its maw glowing as it charged another attack aimed directly at Atlas.

If ranged assaults were useless, then Atlas would take him down in close combat!

Lightning erupted around him once more, golden arcs snapping across his armor as he pushed off from the air itself. In a single burst, he vanished from his position. Then reappeared right before Bai Yun, his spear sweeping upward in a blinding motion.

But Bai Yun was ready. Though he held no weapon, his stance shifted sharply. His feet moved as if stepping on invisible platforms, his body steady and perfectly balanced in midair, like a master who could walk the wind itself.

Atlas swung his spear in a sharp horizontal arc toward Bai Yun, but the man bent backward at the last instant, the blade missing him by mere inches. Atlas followed with a rapid series of thrusts, the tip of his spear flashing in streaks of golden light, yet Bai Yun twisted and wove between each strike, moving like water, smooth, fluid, and impossibly fast. His arrogant smirk never fading.

"Is that all, Atlas?" he taunted. "Did you think I was just another foolish lord relying on reinforcements? No... I am a master of my art!"

Atlas gritted his teeth and pushed harder, lightning crackling along his weapon. He spun the spear around his back and launched another strike from the front. With the reach of his weapon, Bai Yun would have little room to counterattack unless he resorted to using magic.

[Elite – Flurry of Spears (Active Skill) Lv. 9 activated.]

The spear danced like a storm, striking again and again with impossible speed.

But Bai Yun merely smirked, raising one hand as a glowing magic formation appeared before him. It absorbed the strikes with a loud metallic hum, nullifying the skill’s power in an instant. Then, with a flick of his wrist, Bai Yun deflected the shaft of Atlas’s spear to the side.

He moved in close, faster than expected, his feet sliding with perfect control as his fist shot forward. He aimed a precise blow toward Atlas’s chest, followed by another to his side, each strike aimed to keep Atlas from recovering his stance or countering with his weapon.

Atlas barely managed to retreat, leaping back just in time as the impact grazed his armor.

Then Bai Yun’s hands blurred again, forming complex seals in rapid motion. His entire body became enveloped in glowing blue energy that surged and pulsed like a living flame.

"Now," he shouted, his eyes gleaming, "let’s see how brave you truly are, Atlas. Let’s see how long you can stand against me in close combat!"

Atlas planted his feet firmly on the floating platform, gripping his spear with both hands. Lightning rippled along his armor as he steadied his breath, his eyes locked on his opponent. He was ready.

Bai Yun’s hands moved again, forming another rapid sequence of hand seals. In the blink of an eye, his body split apart, one after another, until twelve identical copies surrounded Atlas from every direction, each radiating the same sharp blue aura as the original. They floated in a loose circle around him, their movements perfectly synchronized.

Then, all at once, their voices overlapped, echoing through the air like a sinister chant.

"Let’s see how this foolish rat handles being hunted by twelve predators at once."

Atlas lowered his stance slightly, gripping his spear tightly. The air around him pulsed with surging energy, his lightning aura flickering wildly like a living storm that hungered to be unleashed.

His gaze swept across the circle of enemies, each one a perfect copy of Bai Yun. Yet his expression remained calm, unreadable, untouched by fear or doubt.

Bai Yun was clearly skilled in martial arts, his movements refined and deliberate, strengthened further by his mastery of spellcraft. But Atlas knew this was nothing compared to the martial prowess of Kurogasa.

A faint, confident smile curved at the corner of his lips.

"I haven’t even finished warming up," he said coolly.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 307 - The Real One Bleeds
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Atlas was surrounded from every direction by the enemy’s copies. Whether they were illusionary projections or physical clones, he couldn’t tell yet. He would only know once they struck simultaneously.

With foes circling him on all sides, it would have been nearly impossible to face them all at once. If he relied on his eyes alone. But Atlas had trained far beyond that. He could fight even without sight, relying on the rhythm of movement, the vibration of mana in the air, and the pressure that his enemies released with every motion.

One clone darted in from behind. Atlas clenched his teeth, spinning sharply as his spear cut through the air. The attack missed, his weapon slicing through nothing but mist, as the clone slipped past him effortlessly and its hand darted forward to strike.

Damn! Even the clone moves this fast!

Two more charged from his flanks, closing in from the right and left. Atlas reacted instantly, propelling himself upward with a burst of lightning. But as soon as he rose, two more clones met him midair, their feet whipping toward him in synchronized kicks.

Atlas twisted his spear and swung in a wide arc, meeting their attacks head-on. The blade slashed through them cleanly. Both clones shattered like glass, dissolving into blue light.

Yet more emerged, rushing him from every angle at once. Atlas tried to block and counter, but in that brief instant, a powerful strike landed squarely on his back.

"Ugh—!" The blow hit hard. The impact sent him flying upward, spinning through the air uncontrollably.

More clones surged after him, streaking toward his path with killing intent, their movements so precise it was as if they anticipated every motion he would make.

But Atlas wasn’t done. He twisted midair, forcing his body into alignment, and landed hard on a floating platform. His feet struck the surface, and in that same moment, lightning burst from beneath him.

Lightning Step!

His figure vanished in a flash of golden light, reappearing behind his pursuers as his spear tore through them in rapid succession. One, two, three. Each clone burst apart under the force of his attack.

How foolish of him, only now, in the middle of this chaos, did Atlas realize that his conditional passive skill, Crimson Tempest Guard, wasn’t triggering at all. The ability was supposed to activate whenever he successfully parried an enemy’s strike, yet no matter how many hits he deflected, it didn’t react.

So that was it, the skill only worked against weapon-based attacks. Against bare-handed assaults like Bai Yun’s, it was useless.

The fight raged on, and the endless flurry of Bai Yun’s clones overwhelmed him from every direction. Their fists and kicks struck in perfect unison, forcing Atlas onto the defensive.

He was thrown through the air again and again, his body spinning as he struggled to regain balance. Each time he found his footing, another blow would land, driving him back into the storm.

A pair of powerful hits smashed into his chest and back, sending him hurtling downward like a comet. Atlas gritted his teeth, forcing his focus into his core. A glowing platform of light formed beneath him just in time. He struck it hard, then pushed off with explosive force, propelling himself upward once more as several clones dove down from above to finish him off.

[Unique – Stormcall Domain (Active Skill) activated.]

The sky darkened instantly, and thunder cracked across the heavens. Bolts of lightning cascaded downward, striking furiously around the battlefield. Atlas surged through the air like a golden tempest, his armor blazing with arcs of power, every movement shaking with raw energy.

But the clones reacted just as fast. The moment the storm took form, they broke formation, diving and spinning through the sky, evading every bolt of lightning with fluid precision.

"HAHAHA! THE SAME TRICK WON’T WORK ON ME TWICE, ATLAS! TRY SOMETHING MORE CREATIVE!" they shouted in unison.

Atlas dove sharply, using the air itself as leverage as he launched another jump, propelling himself at incredible speed toward the fleeing clones. Yet, within seconds, they had already slipped beyond the range of his Stormcall Domain, the edge of the storm fading behind them.

At that moment, the twelve Bai Yun clones aligned themselves vertically in the air, forming a circular formation that spun like a spiral. Their hands blurred as they performed rapid hand seal movements, their bodies glowing with blue light that pulsed in unison.

Behind them, the massive azure dragon arched its neck, its jaws opening wide as it began charging another devastating blast.

[Epic – Storm Chain: Judgment Spear (Conditional Active Skill) activated.]

Lightning roared in answer. Atlas’s spear pulsed as he drew in the bolts from the raging sky above, the energy surging violently toward the weapon’s tip until it glowed blinding gold.

"YOU’RE A FOOL, ATLAS!" the clones shouted as one.

But Atlas’s focus never wavered. In that perfect instant, he stopped midair, twisting his body with explosive precision, and hurled his spear forward with all his strength. The weapon shot across the sky, pulling the storm with it. Bolts of lightning followed like a divine chain, all converging into a single colossal strike.

The impact came like the wrath of the heavens. The spear met the enemy’s energy blast head-on, and for a fraction of a second, the sky went white. Then came the explosion—massive, deafening, swallowing everything in a wave of fire, smoke, and ash. The shockwave rippled through the clouds, shaking the air for miles.

Atlas surged forward again, teeth clenched, lightning bursting behind him.

[Unique – Crimson Reaper’s Descent (Active Skill) activated.]

A surge of crimson lightning erupted at his back, twisting into the spectral form of a dark crimson reaper. The ghostly figure spread its wings wide, following him closely as if mirroring his every move.

Atlas clenched his fist tightly, and at his command, the Wavebreaker Spear reappeared, spinning through the smoke and flames, drawn back to him like a bolt of fate itself.

This was one of the exclusive abilities granted through his merge with Zefyros. The power to control the Wavebreaker Spear, to throw it at will and summon it back through sheer force of will and command.

The weapon spun into his grasp again, lightning sparking violently upon impact with his palm. Atlas narrowed his eyes toward the veil of smoke ahead.

The real battle was only just beginning.

Through the smoke and the fading light of the explosion, Atlas narrowed his eyes. His vision cut through the swirling ash until he saw them. Bai Yun and his remaining clones, still hovering defiantly in the air. They were already moving, preparing another synchronized assault.

But Atlas only smiled faintly.

[Elite – Flurry of Spears (Active Skill) Lv. 9 activated.]

His body blurred, lightning exploding beneath his feet as he launched himself forward. In an instant, he was right in front of one of the clones. And the man’s eyes widened in shock.

Atlas had found the real one!

The Crimson Reaper behind Atlas flared with violent energy, hurling a chain of blood-red lightning straight toward Bai Yun. The enemy lord hastily raised his hand, summoning a glowing protective magic formation before him.

The chain struck it, sparking with force. But the barrier held, nullifying the first impact.

It didn’t matter.

Atlas was already there. His spear thrust forward in a blur of golden arcs, blow after blow crashing against Bai Yun’s defense. Sparks scattered, the sound of impact shaking the air as Bai Yun was forced back, his expression twisting with frustration.

Then came the follow-up. The reaper, still hovering behind Atlas, raised its crimson chain once more. It tore through the remnants of the magic barrier and struck Bai Yun square in the chest.
