[ - Any enemy that dies within the zone is taken over by a crimson reaper and rises as a Crimson Husk.

[ - Crimson Husks retain their weapons and skills and will attack the nearest enemy with doubled aggression.]

[ - Crimson Husks persist until the domain ends, then collapse into ash.]

Atlas smiled faintly as he reread the detailed description of the skill. It was his very first time obtaining a Legendary-grade skill, and the realization alone made his chest tighten with a mix of pride and disbelief.

Edrik clapped softly. "You truly deserve this reward, my lord."

"Thank you, Edrik," Atlas replied. "After reading through its description, this skill really has an incredible effect. Still, it requires a lot of strategy to use it properly."

Edrik nodded. "The 25-meter radius could be an issue. That kind of area can either be small or wide, depending on how we manage to lure the enemies into the right position for maximum impact."

"And yes," Atlas continued, "the six-second duration as well. The timer increases by one second for every enemy that dies within that period, and it can keep increasing endlessly."

He paused for a moment, his tone lowering slightly. "And the fact that the Crimson Reapers are summoned based on the number of enemies, and they’re completely untargetable, that’s just insane. On top of that, anyone who dies within the skill’s duration becomes part of an undead army that attacks their own allies. Though, once the effect ends, the undead vanish as well."

Edrik responded thoughtfully, "The main difference between this skill and Stormcall Domain, my lord, is that in Stormcall Domain enemies can still escape the area. But once they enter the Crimson Reaper Dominion, they’re marked, and they’ll continue to suffer the effect even if they try to flee afterward."

"Yes." Atlas nodded slowly.

This truly was a skill worthy of being called Legendary-grade. Its potential impact was overwhelming. A technique designed for pure devastation. It was the kind of skill that could turn a battlefield into a massacre, as long as its setup and timing were executed with absolute precision.

However, the rewards Atlas received didn’t stop there. There was still one more surprise waiting for him.

[You have awakened a Legendary-grade ability. The storm and the crimson flow now recognize your dominion.]

[Title Acquired: Crimson Storm Sovereign]

[A sovereign who commands both storm and blood with absolute authority.]

[Lightning bends to your will, and the crimson reapers answer your call.]

[Title Effects:]

[Storm Sovereignty: Lightning damage increased by +15%, Blood damage increased by +15%.]

[Reaver’s Dominion: Enemies afflicted by Bleed or Stormbrand take +10% additional damage from all sources.]

[Reaper’s Call: When a nearby enemy dies, there is a 10% chance a minor crimson specter appears and strikes another enemy for 150% lightning-blood damage.]

Atlas took a slow breath as he read through the new title effects. This was his third title. Atlas Blackthorn, the Crimson Storm Sovereign, a name that carried a powerful, almost regal weight to it.

Every effect from the title was remarkable, and since they were all passive abilities, it gave him an enormous advantage. He wouldn’t even need to activate them manually.

The only drawback, however, was that Atlas could equip only one Title at a time. That meant he had to carefully choose which one would be the most effective to use in any given situation.

A perfect upgrade.

Then, his expression shifted as the conversation turned more serious.

"Do you have any update about the Shattered Ember Island boss raid we’ll be launching soon, Edrik?"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 348 - Storm Island Has Entered the Chat
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Atlas had returned to Shattered Ember, his second floating island, where Baldric was already waiting for them that afternoon. They stood together on a rocky cliff that circled a massive lava pool far below.

One of the island’s raid bosses dwell within that molten pit, and even from this distance, its presence was obvious. The lava roared and erupted in random bursts, jets of fire shooting skyward again and again despite the real monster still hiding beneath the surface.

"According to Lady Celestia’s recent prophecy, my lord," Edrik explained, "the island’s biome should start changing again within the next few days."

Baldric gave a short laugh before adding, "Yeah, hahaha, it’s normal for an island like this to shift two or three times a year." He raised one hand, rubbing his fingers together as if testing the air.

"Like... you can already feel how the moisture in the atmosphere has changed a little," he continued. "I’m certain this volcanic island will transform soon. Fighting a fire-type monster while the terrain stays volcanic would be a nightmare. It’d have infinite sources of flame and lava to draw its power from. Attacking it up close would be nearly impossible."

He paused, then added more seriously, "Sure, we could go for long-range attacks, but considering how wild this raid boss is supposed to be, I’d bet it’ll break free from its lair eventually. And once it does, it’ll attack everything on this island. That would be a disaster."

Atlas nodded slowly. "We’ll need to take down at least one of them before the Dominion Convergence begins. I’m certain the experience points and rewards we gain will give us a solid boost afterward."

He paused for a moment, his gaze fixed on the bubbling lava below. "We have plenty of water-element users among us, and Dullorak already confirmed he can use one of his abilities to contain the raid boss inside the pool so it won’t escape. But yes, the real problem is whether we’ll have enough damage power to bring it down before it breaks free and causes massive destruction."

Atlas fell silent again, thinking carefully before he spoke. "Let’s prepare everything," he said, turning to Edrik. "If the biome hasn’t changed within two days, we’ll launch the assault."

Edrik bowed his head slightly toward Atlas in acknowledgment.

The next thing they did was something Baldric had been doing regularly for the past few weeks. Down below, near the edge of the lava pool, several people were seen setting up a sequence of traps and charges.

Once the preparations were complete, they quickly withdrew to a safe distance. Just moments before a massive explosion erupted at the center of the lava pool.

"This will bring the monster out," Edrik said afterward.

Atlas had been told about this method several times before, but this was his first time witnessing it in person.

The ground beneath them began to shake violently. Bursts of molten rock exploded upward, sending columns of lava spiraling into the air, twisting and expanding with a deafening roar.

Then, from the heart of the pool, the monster’s enormous head emerged, bellowing with a thunderous cry that echoed across the island.

Edrik continued. "We need to keep the monster awake, my lord. So we can catch it in this state when the biome shifts."

That was the main strategy: to stall for time and hope that the next environmental change would favor them. Anything but fire-based terrain would do. Water or storm would be ideal, and even ice would still be far better than this volcanic nightmare.

What they witnessed was pure ferocity. The raid boss had first appeared when the island shifted from the Eternal Ice Island to this volcanic one, a time when the creature had only just awakened from its long slumber. Back then, it hadn’t even shown its full strength.

Yet even in that state, the monster had managed to incinerate the enemy forces trapped above the lava pool. Wiping out hundreds of lives in mere moments.

This was deadly serious. This raid would have to be executed flawlessly if Atlas wanted to secure the best possible outcome.

**

Two days later, the moment they had all been waiting for finally arrived.

Atlas stood once again in the same place, right at midday. Though here on the volcanic island, the sky was never visible beneath the thick clouds of smoke rising from below.

He wasn’t alone. All along the ridge encircling the lava pool, the entire Gacha Haven army stood ready, fully equipped in their battle gear with weapons drawn. Every single one of them had come prepared for what was about to happen.

Their target, the Lava Leviathan, had also emerged, letting out a roar so powerful it seemed to shake the entire island to its core. It was clear the creature knew it was surrounded. Hundreds of warriors were now closing in from every direction.

At that very moment, Atlas caught a faint change in the air. He inhaled slowly, sensing it. The temperature was dropping, and fast.

"The environment is about to shift," Baldric said beside him.

Almost immediately, the monster grew restless. Massive pillars of lava burst upward, spinning violently across the pool. It was the kind of attack that could annihilate anyone caught within its range.

Everyone watched in tense silence as the molten glow beneath them began to fade. The brilliant red of the island’s lava slowly dimmed, the radiance dying away. The island was transforming.

Atlas lifted his hand, feeling something cool touch his skin.

"Rain?" he murmured.

"Seems luck is on our side," Edrik replied with a faint smile.

The change came rapidly. The lava across the island hardened and cooled within moments, the once-blazing terrain darkening to shades of gray and black.

Then, the first few drops turned into a downpour. Rain poured heavily across the entire island, hissing as it struck the steaming rock.

A sudden flash tore through the clouds, followed by a deafening crack of thunder that rolled across the entire sky!

"I suppose this is your moment to shine, my lord," Edrik said with a grin, his voice nearly drowned out by the storm.

The biome had shifted into a raging storm island. A nightmare for workers who would have to endure it for months to come, but a blessing for Atlas. This was his element. The lightning that tore through the heavens could now be bent to his will, directed toward his target.

The rain poured even harder, hammering against the hardened ground, while the massive lava pillars still burned defiantly within the pool ahead. The monster refused to retreat, guarding its lava pool despite the violent shift in the environment, and that was exactly what Atlas wanted.

Everyone across the ridge lifted their long-range weapons at once. There would be no close combat this time. Even the usual melee fighters had switched to ranged gear, adapting to the new plan.

Dullurak rose into the air as ten heads spun around him in a slow circle.

A deep, echoing rumble followed. Then, suddenly, every drop of rain in the air froze in place. Suspended midfall. A heartbeat later, the water burst outward, spreading into a massive dome of liquid force that sealed the monster within.

Atlas raised his spear again, lightning crackling fiercely along its length.

"Let the storm answer our wrath! Strike with me!"

And at that cry, the sky itself seemed to split apart as a barrage of lightning spears rained down upon the imprisoned beast.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 349 - Let the Leviathan Sleep
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The monster roared violently, its massive jaws opening wide as streams of molten lava blasted out in every direction. The fiery torrents struck against the barrier Dullorak had created, but the dome held strong.

Then, the counterattack began. Arrows, mana guns, and magic spells filled the air. In this raid, everyone had to contribute, no matter how small their damage output might be. Even the workers had been ordered by Atlas to join the assault, lending every bit of force they could muster.

The reward from this raid boss would be shared among all participants, and even a small contribution meant valuable experience points.

The monster thrashed in frustration, its massive body shaking as blows struck from every direction. It was clearly feeling the pressure. Though not yet enough to truly harm it. Still, the sheer scale of the attack was staggering.

Atlas had brought nearly nine hundred people into this raid, combatants and workers combined. All united in one purpose, surrounding the colossal creature and raining destruction upon it from every possible angle.

[Elite - Spirit Infusion (Conditional Active Skill) activated.]

Atlas immediately merged with Zefyros, whose armor shifted in appearance as currents of water surged violently around his entire body. A heartbeat later, Atlas shot upward, propelled through the air as if the storm itself had given him wings.

With the torrential rain and dense water particles filling the air, his movement felt effortless. Almost like swimming through the sky, guided by Zefyros’s water element ability.

From above, he watched as the monster unleashed another wave of molten fury, spewing a massive stream of lava toward one side of the dome. Atlas’s eyes widened when he noticed dozens of people gathered at that very spot.

The impact came with a thunderous crack. The glowing barrier trembled violently.

Dullorak’s protective dome was giving way. It could no longer hold!

At that very moment, the dome finally cracked open. The raging blast of molten lava tore through the breach, surging straight toward the dozens of people clustered below.

Several tankers rushed forward instinctively, raising their shields and activating protective barriers. Ten of them stood in formation, bracing for impact. But even then, it was clear they wouldn’t be enough to withstand such power.

Then Selvara darted ahead. A massive wall of water burst forth in front of the defenders, colliding with the torrent of lava. Steam exploded in every direction, hissing violently as the two elements clashed.

Still, the wall began to falter.

Dullorak immediately flew toward the breach, his ten heads chanting in unison as he reinforced the barrier, adding another layer of water pressure to hold back the inferno.

Down below, a lone figure sprinted across the molten pool as if the surface were solid ground. Atlas’s eyes narrowed in recognition, it was Karian.

He moved with impossible speed, leaping high into the air and landing above the monster’s massive head. With a powerful spin, he struck downward, slamming into the creature’s skull and forcing the lava cannon attack to cease midstream.

A heartbeat later, a set of chain blades shot out and wrapped around the monster’s enormous jaws. Kurogasa had joined the fray, straining to hold the creature in place. But the monster’s strength was overwhelming. It jerked violently, lifting Kurogasa off the ground.

In midair, Kurogasa split into multiple shadow forms, each one darting across the monster’s body in a blur of motion, slashing in synchronized strikes that left glowing lines of damage along its molten hide.

A blazing figure suddenly shot through the air. Flames whirled wildly around it. Everburn, the invisible skeleton, had joined the battle. He unleashed a massive torrent of fire toward the monster, his inferno roaring loud enough to rival the creature’s own lava breath.

Chaos filled the shared mind chat almost immediately.

"Burny, you do realize your fire won’t hurt that thing, right?!"

"Get out of there, Burny, before you get roasted by the lava!"

"Hahaha!" Everburn’s laughter echoed back through the link. "Sorry, I just wanted to join the fun! Let this monster—"

His words cut off abruptly as one of the swirling lava pillars smashed into him, sending his blazing form flying across the battlefield and out of sight.

Atlas couldn’t help but smile faintly at the scene, despite the danger.

Then his expression hardened.

"Let’s end this," he said firmly. "Full damage, all at once!"

At that moment, the battlefield erupted in a storm of pure destruction. Every fighter unleashed their full power.

Raze suddenly shot upward. He slammed into the monster’s upper jaw, grabbing hold and pounding it with a flurry of brutal punches. The monster howled, thrashing wildly as it tried to shake him off.

Atlas gritted his teeth, summoning everything he had. Lightning began to swarm around him like living serpents.

[Unique - Stormcall Domain (Active Skill) activated.]

[Unique - Crimson Reaper’s Descent (Active Skill) activated.]

[Epic - Storm Chain: Judgment Spear (Conditional Active Skill) activated.]

With a roar, Atlas dived downward. The storm itself seemed to follow him. Bolts of lightning from every corner of the sky converged into his spear, fusing into one blinding current of energy. Explosions of thunder rippled through the air as he accelerated.

With both his own lightning and the natural storm feeding into his power, his attack’s strength multiplied beyond anything he had ever unleashed before.

But the monster was far from helpless. Massive lava pillars burst from the pool, twisting upward like burning serpents, forcing Atlas to veer sharply and spiral through the chaos. He dodged one, two, then dove lower again. Straight toward his target.

Just as he closed in, the monster opened its mouth wide, gathering searing magma for a counterblast.

Raze was faster. He slammed his fist into the monster’s jaw, holding it open while Kurogasa’s kusarigama chains shot through the air, wrapping tightly around the creature’s jaws and forcing them shut.

That was all Atlas needed.

He pulled on the storm. Drawing dozens of lightning bolts down from the heavens like golden ropes!

Then came the impact.

The world seemed to stop for a single breath before a blinding explosion shattered the air. The sound was deafening, a thunderclap so immense it felt like the sky itself was breaking apart.

Atlas couldn’t withstand the recoil; the shockwave hurled him backward through the rain, along with nearly everyone else nearby. He quickly regained his balance midair, stabilizing himself as he floated among the sheets of rain that poured endlessly from the storm above.

Below him, blast after blast shook the island. Lava pillars erupted in chaos, the entire volcano trembling as if it were about to burst.

And then... silence began to fall.

The lava’s light dimmed. The once-raging flames flickered out. The Lava Leviathan, its body scorched and fading, let out one final groan before collapsing back into the pool below.

A stream of system messages appeared before Atlas.

[One of the island’s rulers has been defeated.]

[Reward Distribution:

[ – All contributors receive 1,000,000 Experience Points.]

[ – An additional 500,000,000 Experience Points are shared among the top 100 highest damage contributors.]

[The island is now fully synchronized with your Interface. All monster positions, terrain sectors, and environmental variables can now be monitored directly through the system.]

The messages kept flashing before Atlas’s eyes until the final notification faded. Then he descended through the heavy rain, landing near the fallen monster’s body that now lay motionless atop the solidified lava.

Baldric and several others were already there.

"My lord, this thing’s body is huge," Baldric said with a grin. "We could probably cook the meat. I’m sure everyone will love it!"

Orren, the chef, stepped forward next. "This will be enough to feed everyone on the island, my lord. The meat carries a strong vitality. Stamina buffs for days, and it could even boost fire-element resistance and attack temporarily."

"Nice, I think that’s a great idea," Atlas replied.

The downpour was so heavy that speaking felt like shouting through a waterfall. "What about the other parts, Baldric?"

"My lord," Baldric answered quickly, "the teeth, scales, and claws can all be reforged into weapons or armor. So yeah, we’ve got plenty of resources from this one."

Just then, a deep rumble echoed from the distance. Raze, still in his transformed form and not yet reverted to Milo, was charging back across the battlefield. He must have been thrown far earlier during the fight.

Everyone watched as he approached the monster’s corpse. Without hesitation, he grabbed the creature’s massive jaw and pulled.

A sharp, cracking sound echoed across the island as the Leviathan’s entire body was wrenched free from the hardened lava pool. Raze didn’t stop there. He kept dragging it forward, step by heavy step, until even the monster’s enormous tail came fully into view.

"Nice, Raze! Good job, hahaha!" Baldric roared, raising his hand in triumph.

At that moment, Edrik approached Atlas, bowing slightly before pointing toward something in the distance. Atlas followed his gesture ,and there it was.

A massive golden chest, standing solid on the hardened lava. It had appeared the instant the monster had fallen.

"Nice," Atlas said. "Let’s see what kind of reward this monster left for us."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 350 - Spoils of Fire
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Without much expectation, Atlas opened the chest. A burst of golden light flared out, forcing him to narrow his eyes against the sudden glow. When the radiance dimmed, he tilted his head slightly in confusion.

Inside lay five items, stacked haphazardly atop one another. A longsword, a longbow, a halberd, a great hammer, and a great shield.

Each weapon shared a striking design: their surfaces shimmered with faint veins of ember-red energy, and the metal itself seemed to pulse with residual heat. Scales from the slain Leviathan were visibly embedded into their crafting.

But one thing instantly caught Atlas’s attention.

He reached down and lifted the long sword, nearly as tall as he was. The weapon felt alive in his hands, warm to the touch, as though fire still burned within it. When he inspected its status, his eyes widened in disbelief.

[Vulkaris Spineblade (Legendary)]

"What? Legendary grade?" he breathed, feeling his heartbeat quicken.

Driven by curiosity, Atlas immediately checked the remaining four weapons.

[Emberfury Longbow (Legendary)]

[Pyrestorm Halberd (Legendary)]

[Moltenheart Greatshield (Legendary)]

[Blazeforge Hammer (Legendary)]

He stood there in stunned silence for a moment before letting out a short, astonished laugh. All five of them. Legendary-grade weapons.

Atlas turned toward Edrik. "Five Legendary-grade weapons," he repeated quietly, almost as if saying it aloud would make it feel more real.

"That’s an incredibly generous reward, my lord," Edrik replied. "All five are Legendary-grade. Honestly, that’s almost too extravagant. But perhaps it’s because we defeated the monster after its level increased. Now at level 167, while it was only level 136 when we first discovered it."

Atlas nodded, a faint smile tugging at his lips. He gently set the longsword back into the chest, shaking his head.

"The longsword isn’t too different from a spear. They share the same fighting range," he said. "But I have no intention of learning another melee weapon. The halberd and hammer are the same, and the shield... that’s just not for me."

Edrik studied the remaining weapons, then spoke thoughtfully. "You could take the longbow, my lord. It carries a fire element. It might help you trigger your own affinity with fire as well."

Atlas lifted the Emberfury Longbow from the chest and stepped back, giving himself enough space to test it properly. He drew the string carefully, feeling the heavy resistance immediately.

Despite his high Strength stat, the tension in the bowstring demanded real power. It was a weapon that refused to be handled lightly.

"Every arrow fired becomes a streak of molten ember." He paused briefly, then continued, "Arrows explode in a cone behind the target. Splash damage. Burn effect."

From the skill description alone, it didn’t seem overly impressive. At least, not without the proper archery specialization. Its true potential would only reveal itself in the hands of someone with mastery over bow techniques and fire-element synergy.

Another ability caught his eye:

[Flame Serpent Volley: Arrows transform into serpentine fire trails that track multiple enemies.]

Now that was interesting. Auto-tracking arrows that could pursue several targets at once. Combined with a skilled archer’s precision and damage output, this bow could devastate enemy formations from a safe distance.

Atlas couldn’t deny that the weapon intrigued him, especially given its Legendary grade. He could keep it for now. Maybe trade it for another Legendary item that fits his build better. Yet finding a fair trade would be difficult, unless he auctioned it and used the funds to bid for something more compatible.

Among his forces, it shouldn’t be hard to find an archer with fire affinity. Out of the 721 combatants under his command, several were known for their ranged prowess.

He exhaled softly, lowering the bow. Should I pass this to someone else?

For now, Atlas decided to keep the longbow with him until he found the right candidate. He turned toward Edrik.

"Store this one under my name for now," he said. "Make sure the rest of the weapons go to the right hands. I want every piece used to its fullest potential."

**

That night, Atlas received a visit from Luna and Ember, something that had become a routine lately. Especially now that the deadline for the Dominion Convergence was drawing near.

Luna projected a floating screen before them, displaying live updates from the other lords within their alliance.

Since Atlas’s massive assault on the Bolin Alliance days ago, where his forces wiped out over a thousand enemy troops from multiple opposing lords, and Atlas himself personally eliminated 179 of Bolin’s men.. The political landscape had shifted dramatically.

"Just as you predicted, Atlas," Luna began. "They’re deploying their troops to scan every floating island in the surrounding sectors, especially those near our domain."

Atlas nodded slowly. "That was expected. They trust too much in their own strength. Our ambush may have hit them hard, but they’ll treat it as a minor setback. Since they see us as ambushers, they’ll try to counter that by relying on brute force and overwhelming numbers."

Luna nodded in agreement. "Even though we managed to wipe out a large portion of their troops, their remaining forces are still enormous. At least twice, maybe even three times our current numbers."

Atlas’s gaze hardened slightly, but his voice remained composed. "We still have time. We’ll keep tracking their movements and strike them through guerrilla tactics again. The more we chip away at their armies before the Convergence, the better our odds will be."

He folded his arms, thinking aloud. "And I’m certain they’ll spend the next few days leveling up their forces. Their pride won’t allow them to show weakness during the official war. That arrogance will keep them predictable, and that’s exactly what we’ll use against them."

Atlas fell silent for a moment, focusing his thoughts before speaking again.

"We can go all out to crush them in these remaining days. Even after the Dominion Convergence begins, we’ll still have chances to strike from the shadows and eliminate their forces one by one. That way, their strength will continue to crumble before the official battle even starts."

"The question is," Ember said then, her tone noticeably more serious than usual, "have you decided for sure whether you’ll challenge them in the next event, Atlas?"

Atlas nodded slowly, his expression firm. "Whether we issue the challenge or not, my decision is final. Their alliance will fall."

His gaze hardened. "The conflict between our alliances can no longer be softened. They’ll keep coming at us with their numbers, and they won’t stop until they’ve completely destroyed us. So we’ll do the same, and end it before they can."

Luna nodded in agreement. "In terms of rank, we still don’t have any Rank-3 Lords on our side, but several of us are already close. Many are nearing level 150."

Atlas nodded again. He himself was already level 139. Reaching level 150 was no simple task, it required time and enormous effort. Fortunately, he possessed a set of abilities and tools that made large-scale battles far more efficient for him. With each successful hunt or raid, his progress soared faster than most could manage.

"There’s no class advancement at level 150," Luna continued. "That’s why the gap between Rank-2 Lords and Rank-3 Lords is mostly just about level differences. The real transformation comes at the next advancement tier. But for now, we can still compete with them on nearly equal ground."

Atlas offered a faint smile, knowing what she meant. Luna herself was already just a few battles away from that milestone. Once she reached it, she would officially ascend to Pathfinder Lord Rank-3, strengthening their alliance’s overall power even further.

They continued discussing their current position carefully. If the Bolin Alliance needed to level up their forces, then so did Atlas and his own alliance. Both sides were racing against time to strengthen their armies before the Dominion Convergence began.

The problem was, the monsters on Shattered Ember were no longer enough to sustain the leveling needs of thousands of soldiers from the thirteen lords under Atlas’s alliance. The island’s creature population had already been reduced drastically.

"We can level up faster by ambushing enemy troops," Atlas said. "That method is far more efficient. Hunting monsters would only expose our movements, and once they notice that, they’ll come straight for us."

He paused briefly, then a small smile appeared on his face. "But... I have a few strategies in mind. We’ll start implementing them tomorrow. If they’re so eager to hunt us, then let’s make our position look like easy prey, and use that to strike back. Let’s see just how stubborn they really are. How long they’ll keep thinking brute force alone can win against us."

The truth was, Atlas’s alliance was far from weak. Their numbers were smaller, yes, but every single lord remaining was a survivor. Their systems were higher rank.

They were few, the last of those who survived. And for that very reason, they would keep fighting, and win every battle that dared to stand in their way!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 351 - Blinding Gale
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Several days passed as Atlas and his alliance continued coordinating daily, striking the enemy as efficiently and effectively as possible. Even though they could no longer wipe out enemy forces as quickly as Atlas had during the first ambush. Mainly because the opposing side was far more alert now.

The strategy still worked. Altogether, they managed to eliminate around 600 more enemy troops.

At last, the long-awaited day arrived. A sequence of system messages appeared, confirming the start of the next great phase.

[The Dominion Convergence – Official Commencement]

[The time has come. Prove which alliance stands as the strongest and most unified. Dominate the battlefield through strategy, coordination, and will.]

[Event Rules:]

[ - Qualified Lords must unite under a single alliance banner and register one representative. A minimum of 8 Lords is required to participate.]

[ - Lords who choose not to participate will receive a compensation reward of +50 Rank Points.]

[ - Demonstrate your power. The victor of this first round will earn an Arcane-grade Artifact.]

[ - Winners will also gain access to the next round Event Series, where the rewards escalate greatly. Including the chance to obtain Divine-grade Artifacts.]

[ - Remember: victory alone is not enough. Your score, efficiency, and performance determine your final ranking.]

[ - Prepare your troops. Every order and every strike will shape the outcome.]

[Good luck, Challenger.]

Atlas closed the interface, exhaling slowly. The real beginning was finally here.

The reward for this event was an Arcane-grade Artifact! It stood two tiers above Legendary-grade, since beyond Legendary lay the Mythic-grade.

Even more astonishing, the champion of this first event would advance to the next, far greater challenge, with the potential to earn a Divine-grade Artifact?!

"Have you ever laid your hand on something like that, Edrik?"

"My lord, I have only ever owned an item of Arcane-grade, and even that was incredibly game changing when used with the right mastery. So yes, I am certain that with a reward this grand, the competition will become far tighter, and alliances that once thought of stepping back will likely reconsider and join the event again."

Considering that before being summoned by Atlas, Edrik had been a rank-5 Guardian, with his level somewhere between 250 and 300, it made sense that he already had access to Arcane-grade items. But Divine-grade? What kind of abilities could someone possibly wield with an artifact of that level?

But just like in the normal lord battle events, the score from the overall performance would determine the final rewards as well. This time, if Atlas truly wanted to claim the best prize, he had to make sure he secured the best score possible.

Like aiming for at least an S score, or even higher than that. He was fully determined to reach it.

Atlas then stood and coordinated again with all the lords in the room. At that moment, he had also registered their alliance under the name The Vanguard Dominion, consisting of 12 Lords. Yes, only 12, not 13.

Atlas had another major plan in mind, one that required excluding one lord from the original 13 lords for this event.

He immediately reviewed the list of all alliances that had signed up. There were thousands of them, and that was only among rank-2 alliances. If the ones mixed with rank-2 and rank-3 were included, the total number would easily double.

Normally, one alliance could only challenge another alliance with equal or higher calculated battle power. So they could not challenge anyone weaker than themselves.

And at that moment, Atlas noticed that Bolin’s alliance was also listed there.

"The challenge phase will be open for one month, so we have one month to make the most of it," he said.

"Are you sure Bolin will wait for us in this situation, Atlas?" someone asked afterward.

"They don’t have any other choice but to wait, especially if they know we’re still on the available list. Whatever the case, they’ll wait," he confirmed.

**

There were several rules that governed how the transfer of resources from one island to another was handled. For example, once a lord entered an official event, they could no longer send resources.

At this stage, especially when they had already found the alliance opponent they would face, all transfers became restricted.

Because of that, it meant they had one full month to move resources to other islands, as long as everything was returned before the official challenge was accepted. Once the challenge began, every resource and troop had to be back on each lord’s own island.

That day, Atlas and all the lords in the alliance planned to transfer their strongest forces to one island owned by one of the lords on the alliance.

The process soon began, and after a few hours, Atlas found himself on an island covered in wide fields of grass and rolling hills, with no visible buildings or structures on the surface. In truth, every structure on this island was hidden underground.

This was the floating island owned by Kaeris the Wind Spirit. She was gifted with the system ability B-Rank Blinding Gale.

He stood on a tall hill as one by one the lords began appearing near him. A young woman, looking like she was in her early twenties, with short green hair and a bright, cheerful face, floated toward him. Her light armor and white-and-green dress with a white skirt drifted gently around her as she hovered in the air.

"Atlas, I’m so happy you included me in this huge operation. I feel both thrilled and a little overwhelmed," she said with a soft laugh, gliding closer until her face was quite near his, simply because she could float so freely.

"Kaeris, we’re all going to trouble you for the next month with four thousand forces from the other twelve lords staying on this floating island."

"No, not at all... I’ll be more than happy. This is an incredible honor, and I’m ready to support this alliance’s big mission with everything I have," she said, her cheerful expression unwavering.

So the plan was simple: after each lord selected their forces, the total came to four thousand units, including Atlas’s own four hundred troops that he contributed to the operation. All of them would stay on Kaeris’s floating island, with one main objective: reach level 160 within one month.

And why Kaeris?

Because even though her ability was only rank B, allowing her to generate cutting winds that obscure vision and reduce the precision of enemies fighting within the affected zones, that was only her main battle skill.

There was another feature tied to her ability: she could move her floating island at speeds many times faster than a normal floating island. She could even hide it behind a wind barrier created through the special structures built on her island.

So, simply put, this floating island was a massive fast-moving vehicle that could travel quickly while staying well protected.

With four thousand forces joining this expedition, plus Kaeris’s original troops, Atlas planned to take the floating island to the Pan-African Leagues, where he had already marked around two hundred unoccupied floating islands to raid and exploit as much as possible.

This would massively boost the levels of every force under the alliance.

During a large event like this, most lords tended to reduce their hunting activities, making this the perfect and safest time to launch the operation. And because Atlas had only registered twelve lords for this event, any opponents who tried scouting would not suspect anything when they saw only twelve floating islands belonging to the twelve lords in his alliance.

Atlas stayed inside a structure that was surprisingly comfortable, with couches, full facilities, and four large magic screens lining the walls. Each screens displayed a view from a different direction, along with a radar showing the territory they were passing through.

"How long until we reach our destination, Kaeris?" he asked.

The wind spirit continued floating as she always did, never once setting her feet on the ground. Though strangely, she still wore shoes.

"Twelve hours, Atlas. This is going to be a very fun trip. Fasten your seatbelts, get some sleep or do whatever you need, the great flight will begin soon!" she announced with bright enthusiasm.

Right after all the combatants were fully transferred to the island, and the six lords selected by Atlas boarded for the expedition, the floating island immediately began to move, shooting away at high speed from the area where the rest of the alliance’s lords remained.

There was no significant difference felt while staying inside this floating island. It was as if the island wasn’t moving at all, even though the magic screens clearly showed that it was speeding forward at an incredible pace, far faster than Luna’s floating carriage.

The real question was whether the resources they hunted with this many forces would be enough to push them to at least level 150 within a month.

That was why Tessa was also in the room, ready to explain their plan in greater detail. They would quite literally work day and night without pause to reach this goal.

If they wanted to surpass every other alliance out there, then they had to put in far more effort than anyone else, and they all understood that clearly!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 352 - Into the Stormlands
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

For the mission, Atlas brought six additional lords, since they needed to divide their forces so their home islands would remain guarded. Kaeris was, of course, included as the owner of the floating island. Then there was Luna, whose system ability was the most versatile. Celestia joined as well, and Tessa came along as the strategist. Kate was part of the team, and the last member was Rhaen.

Rhaen possessed the system ability Aetherlift Dominion, which made it easier for all forces to descend from the floating island to the islands below. Her ability allowed her to control both her troops and any bound items so they could stay suspended in the air for extended periods.

So the strategy was straightforward: they would deploy units from above, sending them down to the targeted floating islands beneath them. The four thousand five hundred combatants would then spread across each island, eliminate the monsters as quickly as possible, and return to the main island to continue the hunt.

According to Tessa’s calculations, the team needed at least one day to wipe out most of the island monsters. They needed around twenty-nine days in total, so the faster they improved through coordinated training, the sooner they could attempt to attack two or even three islands in a single day.

It was an extremely tight strategy and mission, one that demanded flawless planning and execution. Any problem that emerged along the way could disrupt the entire flow.

Even though... in truth, Atlas joined this journey not because he would take part in the mission itself. He had another mission of his own. That was also why he brought Serenith, Krythalis, Dullorak, and Everburn, mostly because of their flying abilities. And of course, he brought two of his personal shadows.

During the long hours of travel, everyone in the room worked on arranging their target islands carefully, one by one, deciding which ones they would strike first. A scouting team had already been sent ahead to gather information.

Their main target was to find islands they could clear without any clashes at all. That part was extremely important. Any unnecessary confrontation with other lords during such a tight mission would turn into a disaster.

Until finally... the floating island slowed down as the massive screen showed thick clouds ahead, and behind those clouds appeared a super massive island many times larger than Kaeris’s floating island.

It was an island filled with gigantic trees, the kind that served as the natural home of huge monsters.

Tessa, who stood at the front, explained, "Well, this was not our first choice. But we are lucky, because this island is completely empty of other lords. So we can clear it with full force. Even though it may take up to two days at most."

All the lords in the room immediately prepared themselves and coordinated their forces.

Atlas waited inside the room as the massive screens showed the combatants launching from the edge-launch platforms of the island. They shot downward at high speed, and a moment later they activated their fall-breaking gear.

At that moment Luna walked toward him. "Atlas, leave everything to us. We already divided the tasks well, and thanks to your idea we could take this step."

Atlas rose to his feet and gave a faint smile. "I really look irresponsible for handing this duty to you again, Luna."

"Well, every lord in the alliance gains from this mission, so no. We all carry this duty together."

Atlas nodded slowly. He then prepared himself and walked out of the room, taking the elevator until they reached the upper area of the floating island where the wind blew with great force.

He then summoned the Nimbus Carriage, and everyone on the team boarded it at once before departing from the island.

Atlas took another heavy breath as he truly left many responsibilities in the hands of many people this time.

Luna was indeed someone he could rely on in leadership. She was the original leader of this alliance, so giving this task to Luna was the best choice.

As for the temporary leader of the alliance while Atlas and the others were away like this, it was Arveline. She was the one blessed with King’s Rebirth and also had very strong leadership skills.

Besides that, for Gacha Haven itself, since Edrik was also with Atlas, he had to hand over the temporary leadership to someone else. Sadly, there was no one he could fully trust to handle administration and management among his Elite Subordinates, even though they had long experience. They all had their own unique styles and their own kind of chaos.

So Atlas asked Brigid to help Mira monitor all administration, activities, and management in Gacha Haven.

Brigid was someone very careful and very structured, extremely strict, very disciplined, and incredibly detailed, and she could analyze things deeply. She was exactly like Edrik, so finally Atlas had a second option besides Edrik to handle tasks like this.

**

The journey on the Nimbus Carriage took less than one hour because their target island was already within this region. And right as they drew near, Atlas could feel from even this distance that the air temperature had dropped sharply.

He looked out the window, hearing how the rumble of storm and lightning from the Nimbus Carriage was still weaker than the booming thunder and fierce storm far ahead.

And yes, their destination island finally came into view. He was truly stunned and fell silent as he saw from afar the thick dark clouds gathering heavily, covering the entire island while flashes of lightning struck the ground below with brutal force.

"We will not be able to get too close with the carriage," Edrik said.

"Yes, this is worse than the place where I did that quest back then," Atlas answered.

To build the Storm Generator Structure, Atlas needed exactly five Cores from Tempest-class monsters. These were monsters with advanced composite elements that could not be found in normal monsters.

They were usually found only in area boss types, and their numbers were few. Even worse, he needed five of them.

The Nimbus Carriage moved in and descended toward the edge of the island, and from here he could clearly see the massive tornado in the distance along with huge lightning strikes hitting the island nonstop. The large trees were charred black after being struck again and again.

If Atlas had never gone through this trial before, he would not have had the courage to enter that island.

"This is an island accessible up to lord rank four, so it is not normal for a lord rank two to enter," Edrik explained.

"Someone around level one hundred would probably be burned to death if they entered, judging by how constant the lightning is. Anyone would be at risk of being struck and killed at any moment. This is truly a terrifying island."

Even though Atlas personally had an Affinity with Lightning that should stop the storm from affecting him too much, it also meant he would not be effective when fighting the monsters here. So he would face another trial to develop new skills and new resistance to fight monsters of the Tempest type.

Just as the Nimbus Carriage sped along the edge of the island, the wind and rain hit with such force that even the carriage was pushed off its path by the pressure coming from the island.

A rank four island truly existed on a different level.

Right when the carriage door opened, Dullorak quickly cast his buff skill on everyone, and moments later they all shot out of the carriage. Atlas felt Serenith grab his hand as they rushed out together and flew toward the island.

Even after landing inside the island, they hit the ground hard as the roar and chaos of the storm that slammed across the whole island made this place feel like a prison of torment for anyone bold enough to enter.

Atlas narrowed his eyes a little as he glanced at Serenith, whose bright red hair was drenched and falling over her face, even though she still let out a small laugh.

"I love storms. I love explosions, lightning, and bombs," she said and giggled.

"Well, even though you know your element is not effective here, Sera?"

She only giggled again and nodded eagerly.

They then moved between the towering trees whose branches and leaves shook wildly each time the wind changed direction. Here, if someone lacked proper control over their own body, the storm would lift them off the ground and carry them away with ease.

They continued until they found a spot among large rocks and stood there, the two massive stones on either side giving them enough shelter to take a short break and begin discussing their planning in that place.

Atlas turned toward Dullorak and waited for an explanation from this Mage.

"So, Dullorak, how about my current situation to fight the storms?"

Because surely fighting an enemy with similar elements was not the most effective way to win.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 353 - Lessons in Lightning
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Dullorak cast a magic spell, and right after that all the rain and storm around them froze as if time had stopped.

The effect spread and formed a cube-shaped area where the fierce rain and wind no longer passed through them but were pushed aside.

Even though they could have set up a Rank A Ethereal Tent to protect themselves, that would only waste time. So now they had a better space to discuss what they planned to do next.

Edrik prepared a small resting spot with sturdy camping chairs and a place to sit, while Dullorak began explaining what Atlas would be learning here with this challenge.

Dullorak stood before the others as he started his explanation.

"By nature Lightning is weak against the Earth element," he said as he explained the details. "Because this element can disrupt and ground the energy. That means if someone has strong mastery over Earth, it becomes the easiest way to handle the monsters we will face here. Especially since the monsters in this place are mostly above level two hundred. And the higher grades will be a real challenge if we cannot counter Lightning properly."

"Earth? Okay," Atlas repeated.

"Earth is the best, although we can also use other options. For example, Water can help because it spreads electricity and forces it to dissipate over a wide area, weakens single target lightning, and turns lightning speed into a drawback."

"We can also use Ice," he continued. "Ice does not naturally resist lightning, but it can slow the lightning user, force more predictable movement, and freeze the ground to limit lightning dash paths."

"We can even fight with the same element. Lightning against Lightning will cancel, clash, or override each other depending on mastery."

Next, Dullorak explained in more detail the strengths and weaknesses of each element, showing clearly that as someone who held mastery over many elements, or... more precisely many heads with their own mastery, he understood all of these concepts deeply and could explain them to Atlas with ease.

The next part of the lesson came when Dullorak asked the others to demonstrate how, even without the most effective counter element, they could still fight elemental monsters well as long as they truly understood their own strengths and the best fighting techniques they had.

Until, a few minutes later, Kurogasa returned to them just as something cut through the raging storm outside, crashing through the trees and appearing near their area.

Atlas quickly stood up when he saw something that looked like a large cat, with black fur glowing red, and strong lightning surging and bursting from its body. Combined with the violent storm outside, anyone hit by this creature’s attacks would be shocked badly and might lose their life with ease.

Edrik then spoke quietly. "This is a Thunderclaw Panther. This monster has high speed and a heavy burst of lightning. It is extremely dangerous in storms that boost its power."

At that moment Kurogasa, who had already joined them again, looked at Atlas and gave a small bow toward him. "Allow me to demonstrate the technique to handle this monster, my lord," he said.

Atlas swallowed hard and agreed.

This was seriously a monster with at least level two hundred fifteen. In its own habitat like this, strengthened by thick elemental energy around it, its attacks would multiply in force and become extremely fatal. And this was only one of them.

The monster roared loudly, moved at high speed, and slammed against the barrier. But only a strong vibration shook the air as the creature failed to break through it.

Atlas focused all his attention as Kurogasa darted out of the barrier and leaped into the air with perfect precision even though the storm and lightning filled the whole area.

The monster roared loudly, and the lightning spread in every direction from its roar.

The creature made a light jump, yet it moved at great speed, dashing off a tree and striking toward Kurogasa who floated in the air. But Kurogasa vanished.

Then something happened that normal eyes would have trouble believing.

The monster sped around, leaping, striking, and lunging through the area while Kurogasa’s shadow seemed to appear again and again, always attacked yet never hit even once.

Until several shadow chains shot up from the ground and locked the monster midair, stopping its movement in an instant. Then the creature was slammed down to the ground.

Kurogasa appeared from several directions at once and moved his hands quickly as the shadow chains kept binding the monster that could no longer move. It roared again and again as bursts of lightning exploded wildly from its body. One strike of that lightning whip would surely be painful beyond belief. This was a level two hundred fifteen monster.

The creature then seemed to charge its power when a large explosion erupted, and lightning flared out, consuming the shadow chains as blast after blast struck outward. The monster looked as if it was trying to unleash everything it had to break free.

Atlas swallowed hard as he saw how massive the damage from the monster truly was.

Until the explosions slowly faded, and Kurogasa stood on one foot suddenly on top of the creature whose body had gone limp and no longer moved.

Atlas remained silent as Dullorak then explained, "Kurogasa used the shadows to void most of the damage. Shadow is an excellent counter to Lightning, not in a simple rock paper scissors way. Shadow does not resist lightning, it breaks the rules of lightning. It disrupts the elemental flow, hides movement, bends timing, and creates blind spots that lightning cannot lock onto."

"When the monster reached the tiny moment where its element stopped channeling right after releasing a major skill, Kurogasa cut into that opening with full precision and subdued it without taking any hit in return."

"What if he fights several of them at once?" Atlas asked.

At that moment Edrik, who stood near them, cleared his throat and gave a small bow toward Atlas. "Let me handle four of them, my lord."

"That is a level two hundred fifteen monster, are you sure? I still need you in Gacha Haven, Edrik, so do not push yourself just to show off."

"Yes, and let me do it with twenty monsters later, my lord." Serenith then threw her words in as well. "I will burn all my stamina and use full force of the fire element. I will crash that storm dry and see which one is stronger, lightning or fire, hehe," she said without any guilt.

Dullorak responded briefly, "Lightning is mostly a composite of fire and air, so naturally lightning tends to be stronger. It carries both heat and speed, and that mix gives it a sharper edge in direct clashes. But if we talk about mastery, then the element topic can be skipped completely. Mastery changes everything."

Dullorak explained everything with great detail, yet Atlas truly wanted to see how Edrik would handle four of the same monsters at the same time.

Because a few minutes later those monsters really appeared and moved toward them.

"Now, they are yours, Edrik," Atlas said with a faint smile, truly wanting to see how Edrik would utilize his Ancient Element in this battle.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 354 - Not All Shadows Strike Alike
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Even though Kurogasa and Edrik shared the same basic element, Dark, and its advanced version, the Shadow element, the two of them had very different fighting techniques and combat skills. In truth, even if Edrik looked like a simple executive administrator of the island, or even if his title only said The Golden Steward...

But no. He was not that simple. Before becoming a lord, he had been a feared assassin who once required dozens of people just to bring him down.

Then, Edrik shot forward at high speed as he boldly confronted the four monsters whose sizes nearly reached his chest.

The four monsters struck at him at the same time. It was deadly. Atlas watched with tension.

But what happened next was Edrik suddenly vanishing as the monsters attacked. He appeared in the air, leaping and twisting with elegant movement as his body spun to the side. Then he vanished again when the others attacked.

In the next moments, Edrik completely avoided every strike with perfect precision while dashing and matching the monsters’ speed by using his short teleportation skill.

At this point he was truly showing off with his Ancient Element.

However, it was clear Edrik could not keep using that power repeatedly, because at a certain moment one of the monsters charged with full force and slammed toward him with a massive burst of lightning. Atlas widened his eyes at what happened.

But Edrik simply cast a barrier in front of him, and the monster’s attack was held back and swallowed by the shadow barrier he formed.

"Edrik is absorbing all of that attack into his Space Ancient Element," Dullorak explained.

Atlas watched again with tight nerves because he knew how devastating the attack from even one of those monsters could be.

A loud explosion erupted from the monster’s strike and sent Edrik flying backward at high speed. The other monsters rushed toward him from the sides.

If Kurogasa had taken more time to defeat the earlier monster, it was because he wanted to demonstrate the technique he used to win without taking a single hit.

This time it was clear that Edrik was also using technique and strategy, but that did not mean he was untouched. He was clashing head on, and he was taking hits.

Until a certain moment, Atlas saw it clearly. "This is the moment!"

He spoke out of reflex, because he noticed the same opening Kurogasa had exploited earlier. One of the monsters showed that tiny gap when the lightning was not charging through its body. And Edrik used that opening perfectly.

He appeared above the monster and struck with his dagger which...

"No way!" Atlas murmured softly.

Edrik’s dagger was glowing with lightning, and he drove it straight into the monster beneath him.

A series of explosions blasted out with intense force. Even Edrik himself was thrown away by the shockwaves.

"Edrik used the lightning charge attack from the other monster that he trapped inside his dimensional space, then used that stored lightning to strike the next monster at the exact moment its lightning protection dropped," Dullorak explained afterward.

What happened after that was Edrik practically trying to dodge and withstand the monster’s attacks. He was thrown again and again, even though he did not look hurt at all.

Whenever he found an opening, he struck back with fierce force, making the monster taste its own lightning again.

One, two, and after some time, all the monsters fell under a battle display that was not as surgical or precise as Kurogasa’s. But Edrik’s fighting style was ferocious and brutal, built on movement that was still very controlled.

The difference in how they had grown was clear, as well as their fighting styles, even though they had not yet regained all the power they once held in the past. But the techniques they remembered and the muscle memory they carried allowed them to face monsters of this level and this brutality without much trouble, without hesitation, and without needing long preparation.

Edrik returned to the safe area and gave a small bow toward Atlas, while Atlas smiled and let out a soft laugh after watching how they fought.

"Seeing how all of you fight," Atlas continued with a humble tone as he took a breath.

Edrik replied, "You have far greater potential, my lord. In such a short amount of time you are already ready to fight at this level with your destructive power. Give yourself a little more time and you will become a literal destructor, a massive force that wipes out anything in your path. And I am not exaggerating this. You have shown it again and again. And I believe you will be able to learn the technique from before in a short time if you truly commit to it, and I know you always do."

"Well, thanks for the praise, Edrik," he said, then turned toward Krythalis.

The insect beastkin bowed slightly and answered, because this was his task now.

Krythalis summoned his insect army, and they shot out of the barrier and vanished into the storm.

Kurogasa said it would take him much longer if he went scouting alone because the outside condition was unbelievably brutal. He even openly admitted his limit here.

Whether he truly could not scout as perfectly as usual, or if he was simply being modest, Atlas could not tell. It was not surprising though, because even their levels were far below the monsters outside. This was a rank four island after all.

But with Krythalis and his insect army, the scouting and mapping of the entire island would carry far less risk if he was the one doing it.

Now came the next question as Atlas and Dullorak continued discussing the strategy for how Atlas should handle fights with the monsters on this island.

Considering Atlas also had High Affinity with the Dark element, he also held the advanced version, the Shadow element, with High Affinity as well.

Should he fight using Dark and Shadow in this opportunity?

But the answer Dullorak gave after that gave Atlas a clear picture.

"Having High Affinity with Shadow is not enough, my lord," Dullorak said calmly.

"Shadow can work, but the risk is much higher. You would need far more time to apply the technique safely, and every mistake will hit harder in a place filled with lightning. For now, it is more practical to use Earth. Earth is the natural counter, and you can keep the danger under control. You also have Medium Affinity with Earth, and if you push that, it may rise to High and help your progress for your next planned evolution."

Atlas nodded softly, understanding the whole concept.

High Affinity with Shadow was like holding a legendary grade sword. The item had great potential, but in the hands of someone inexperienced it would never perform the same as when used by someone skilled and trained.

That was the most straightforward way to describe this situation.

It was not that Atlas had no intention of mastering his Shadow. No, he would do that. But he also knew he could not master everything at the same time.

So one step ahead first, and he would surely walk every path later to become stronger.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 355 - The Earthward Guard
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

There was a skill called Elemental Convergence that had been part of Atlas’s arsenal for quite some time. He’d obtained it ever since forming his first sacred bond with Lyrassa. This was a skill that allowed him to fuse two elements into a single, powerful combined effect.

Essentially, this skill granted Atlas the miraculous ability to create a composite form of two basic elements. Even without having a natural affinity for them.

He had experimented with it many times before, blending water with air, fire with earth, and several other combinations.

However... due to the skill’s overall impact being inferior when compared to his other, more refined abilities, especially in terms of damage - it had never seen real use in battle.

The main reason was that, during combat, Atlas could only afford to use skills that were efficient and truly effective. So even though Elemental Convergence held great potential, he couldn’t make full use of it when it truly mattered.

[Epic - Elemental Convergence (Active Skill, Versatile) activated.]

[Temporarily fuses two elements for powerful combined effects.]

Atlas had once demonstrated this skill in front of Dullorak. The man’s response was blunt: the elemental force produced by the skill was only comparable to having a low-level affinity. What Atlas had wasn’t even a proper skill. It was merely the ability to access a composite element.

The point was... a person could theoretically have an affinity for hundreds of elements. It was no different from someone owning a thousand different weapons at home: swords, spears, and everything in between. But simply having them didn’t make one skilled. In fact, someone with only a handful of affinities, yet who could wield them masterfully, was far more dangerous.

That’s exactly why both Kurogasa and Dullorak had been guiding him to first master Water and Lightning. The two elements directly supported by his class. Anchor himself in those first, then explore the others little by little. Mastery over many elements wasn’t something easily achieved... but it was still possible. Just look at Dullorak himself.

Though, to be fair, the man had an unfair advantage. He could absorb the powers of others and make them his own.

"For now, focus on Earth, my lord," Dullorak said calmly. "I have a theory... but it’s better if I keep it to myself for now. I don’t want to interfere with the natural process of how things are meant to unfold."

"Well, whatever it is," Atlas replied. "We’ll just go about it the usual way, right?"

Dullorak and Kurogasa both bowed to him after that.

They would be staying on this island for a few more days, or weeks. After all, the monsters they’d been using for practice were far from the main targets they were hunting. No, not even close.

These were just the scattered weaklings. Plentiful in number, hundreds, maybe even thousands of them. And if just a few of them could already bring about battles this intense, then one could only imagine the sheer difficulty and danger the island truly held.

What would the actual field boss of a rank-4 floating island even be like?

Atlas began preparing. He equipped his soulbinding armor, though this time, instead of using the Wavebreaker Spear, he chose another spear, one infused with Earth element. It had been prepared in advance by Baldric.

He was ready to dive deeper into the Earth element, just like he had done not long ago, when he trained while carrying Vienne in his arms.

Remembering that little girl now... made Atlas feel a sudden pull to go home. What would she be like by the time he returned? Hopefully, she’d still be a baby... or at least a toddler.

**

One, two, three days passed just like that... They kept moving from place to place, setting up camp in different spots. The protection dome from Dullorak and the Ethereal Tent both played a huge role in helping them stay sane and safe on this island, which never stopped being battered by storms and lightning, twenty-four hours a day.

Luckily, all the water from the constant downpour spilled off the edges of the floating island, so the place never ended up flooding despite the relentless storm.

After three full days, Atlas had gone through battles that left him questioning everything. What did it even feel like anymore? He’d been struck by lightning countless times. Each hit made him feel as though he were standing on the edge of death.

The attacks from the monsters... sure, the pain might have been just as intense, maybe even worse at times. But what made the whole thing feel ridiculous was this: Atlas wasn’t using any of his Lightning Skills. Which meant his mobility was severely limited. He couldn’t perform those short-range teleportations like Edrik could.

So, he just pushed forward with what he had, his Earth element. And in that struggle, something happened. He managed to trigger several new Earth-based skills.

One of them allowed him to thicken his skin using the Earth element, giving him extra durability. Another let him anchor himself to the ground, making him unshakable when holding a position. Then there was a skill that used Earth energy to boost his mobility, letting him move faster across terrain. And finally, a defensive skill that summoned an earth shield, one that reacted to incoming attacks by sensing the elemental mana in the air and intercepting them accordingly.

[Rare - Stonehide (Conditional Passive Skill) activated.]

[Temporarily hardens the user’s skin with Earth energy, greatly increasing physical defense.]

[Rare - Rooted Stance (Active Skill) activated.]

[Anchors the user to the ground, preventing knockback and reducing stagger from heavy hits.]

[Rare - Terra Stride (Active Skill) activated.]

[Channels Earth energy into the legs, enhancing speed and allowing smoother movement over any terrain.]

[Elite - Earthward Guard (Active Skill) activated.]

[Summons a reactive earth shield that automatically intercepts incoming attacks by detecting ambient elemental mana.]

Atlas stood alone, his eyes fixed on the two monsters darting toward him from opposite directions in the distance. He gripped his spear tightly, his jaw clenched in grim determination.

The Earth element swirled wildly around his body as hardened particles formed plates of earthen aura, orbiting him like a protective shell.

Earthward Guard. Even though it was an Elite-grade skill, it wasn’t weak by any means. The problem was, the monsters he faced now were on a whole different level. Making even this solid defense seem inadequate.

He dashed forward just as the two monsters lunged at him from behind. With a sharp turn, he blocked their attack using his spear, barely holding his ground.

The impact sent him flying backward, and in that split second, a third monster launched itself from the side, aiming straight for him.

Still off-balance, Atlas couldn’t react in time. Both monsters were already mid-air, claws extended, about to strike from above.

He wasn’t fast enough to dodge.

The swirling Earth energy around him suddenly surged and shot upward, intercepting just as one of the monster’s massive claws came crashing down toward him.

There was a violent clash. The Earthward Guard shattered under the blow, unable to withstand the force. Both claws tore into his chest a heartbeat later.

Atlas slammed into the ground with brutal force, the impact shaking the dirt beneath him.

Now both monsters loomed over him, ready to strike again, and he could barely lift his weapon.

"Damn it!" he growled through gritted teeth.

In that fleeting moment, his mind raced, desperate to figure out how to make the Earthward Guard keep protecting him.

And then... something shifted.

[Triggering an Advanced Element from Earth...]

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 356 - Crystal Cuts Deeper
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[Triggering an Advanced Element from Earth...]

[Process failed. Insufficient comprehension.]

Atlas was caught in a dire situation, his breath ragged, when he suddenly felt a powerful surge of Earth energy, racing in from a distance with tremendous speed.

Several sharp shards of crystalized earth came shooting through the air like a storm of knives, pelting the monsters attacking him. Each shard glinted like a jewel, but hit with the force of a blade.

He narrowed his eyes, watching the sudden onslaught unfold.

Within seconds, the three monsters collapsed. Bodies torn apart, impaled, and shredded as though they were nothing more than paper.

Atlas slumped down again, catching his breath, just as Dullorak approached. Yes, with a new form this time. His head now bore a reptilian shape, complete with a crystalline frill.

"Keh keh keh keh... what pathetic little creatures," the voice came out sharp and arrogant.

It was another of Dullorak’s many personalities. Atlas accepted his help as the man pulled him up.

The reptilian head tilted slightly, its voice smug and proud. "My lord, do you see now? How my Lumencryst, the Crystal Earth Light element, tore these foolish beasts to shreds? They didn’t even know they’d died until their guts hit the ground."

"Yes, that was incredible," Atlas said with a small laugh. "I really hope I can use a similar element someday."

With that, they made their way back toward the shelter, where the others were waiting.

Lumencryst?

Atlas heard the name of yet another element, and he could see with his own eyes just how terrifyingly effective it was in real combat.

So even with that reptilian-shaped head, this version of Dullorak had access to the Light element? Interesting.

"Tell me, Dullorak," Atlas said, still catching his breath, "is it possible for me to control Earth with that kind of precision too, even if I’m only using the basic element? Your spell attacks... They were unbelievably sharp and powerful. Just imagine, raining strikes like that across a battlefield... it’d leave holes through every enemy out there."

"Keh keh keh keh..." the reptilian head let out a proud, gravelly chuckle before responding. "What you saw. That was Crystal, the advanced form of Earth. Now, when you blend it with Light, it becomes Lumencryst. This combination gives the attacks not only cutting force, but also speed, control, and the power to disrupt enemy elements."

He leaned closer, his tone even more arrogant now.

"These strikes don’t just pierce flesh. They slice through elemental defenses too. Anything trying to resist with magic? Cracked, broken, and shattered. Keh keh keh keh keh!"

Atlas nodded slowly, absorbing the explanation. Crystal... an advanced element of Earth.

And if Crystal could already do that. What kind of power would he unlock if he learned to wield it with Light, just like that?

**

After rejoining the others, Atlas brought up the topic of the Advanced Element he had nearly triggered. Right before it failed, and Dullorak arrived to save him just in time.

In serious moments like these, Dullorak always chose to speak through his Archmage Eldros head.

"My lord, you were very close," he said.

"But as I’ve told you before. Revealing the nature of what lies ahead would rob you of the understanding you were meant to earn. I could guide your hand and bring you to that power with ease... yet doing so would only blunt the blade you are meant to sharpen yourself."

Atlas gave a small nod, understanding the reasoning. "Got it. You want me to figure this out on my own. So I fully grasp the process from start to finish. That way, I won’t just be able to use it. I’ll be able to master it. And later, apply the same to other elements as well."

Dullorak went on to explain further.

The core message was clear: he and Kurogasa were only here to guide Atlas. Just enough to make sure he stayed on the right path. The real breakthroughs had to come from Atlas himself.

By unlocking each ability and affinity through personal struggle and discovery, every step he took would become deeply connected to him. Shaped by his understanding and experience. His mastery would be grounded, not just in knowledge, but in intuition and ownership.

Not that he expected to be spoon-fed. But with mentors like Dullorak and Kurogasa, armed with their unmatched knowledge and precision. Atlas knew he couldn’t ask for anything more.

They continued their discussion, shifting focus to the results of Krythalis’s scouting over the past few days. The core finding was simple: So far, he hadn’t been able to locate the monster they were searching for. Or more precisely, he hadn’t seen them directly.

"There are several zones scattered across this island," the beastkin reported. "In those areas, the concentration of lightning elements is... chaotic and violent. Anyone who approaches is struck down before they can even initiate a proper fight. The energy there is so densely packed... we’ll need someone who can neutralize or suppress that surge if we’re going to get close."

"That should be where the monsters reside," Dullorak responded quietly.

Serenith, standing among them, chimed in with a sparkle in her eyes. "Then let me strike from above! I’ll unleash my biggest explosion. The absolute strongest one I have. I’m sure that’ll bring the monster down in one shot!" she giggled at the thought.

"Lady Sera," Dullorak replied. "I do not doubt the sheer destructive power you can summon. But the elemental concentration in those zones is too extreme. It would suppress much of your explosion’s strength before it even reached the target. And if we fail to kill the monster with that strike... all we will have done is announce our presence, and waste the opportunity."

"Ahh, what a boring answer," she said, pouting with a playful smirk. "You always ruin the fun," she giggled again.

The discussion then shifted toward planning their strategy for attacking the suspected zone.

"I can use my skill to shield us and help neutralize the lightning element," Dullorak offered, "but with my current strength, I won’t be able to contribute much in terms of damage output during the actual fight."

Edrik stepped forward, bowing slightly toward Atlas. "There’s one concern, my lord. If we can’t eliminate the target quickly, we may be forced to use instant teleportation items to escape. We’ll only have a narrow window."

His concern was clear. Would their combined firepower be enough to take down a monster of this level? It wasn’t unreasonable to doubt. The threat they were facing now was far beyond the norm.

"If this proves too risky for us to handle alone, we can retreat for now and call in reinforcements," he added, already considering contingency plans.

Morganna, Raze, and the others could be summoned if more strength was required. Even Tessa could potentially assist using her unique ability. But Atlas suspected that, for a monster of this level, not even Tessa’s system-based abilities would be enough. At least not without a cost too great to bear.

"We’ll give it two more days," Atlas said firmly, making his decision. "Until then, Krythalis will continue gathering more data."

But even as he spoke, a quiet resolve formed within him. If what they truly needed was more raw firepower. Then so be it.

Let’s see...

Would two days be enough for Atlas to forge that change?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 357 - The Imagination Principle
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

After witnessing firsthand how Dullorak wielded the Crystal Element, Atlas couldn’t help but see the stark difference. Dullorak didn’t just launch crystal shards, he commanded them. In mid-air, he could shift their trajectory, rotate them in precise formations, even suspend them around himself like a ring of blades before the battle had even begun.

It was as if those crystal shards were alive, and extensions of his will.

And that was exactly what Atlas began trying to imitate, using only his Basic Earth element.

He grew increasingly fascinated with the Earthward Guard skill. As it stood now, his use of the Earth element only allowed him to form crude, condensed shapes. Dense, but lacking the physical definition or finesse of Dullorak’s crystal.
