Which meant this battle would become a test of endurance. Either the monster would manage to land its deadly attacks, or the attackers would survive long enough to break through and defeat it.

Atlas flew in a wide circle around the monster from a distance, while Elyndra moved in from the opposite side. Both of them were preparing to unleash their abilities.

Then, in that moment, the lightning jellyfish monster released large glowing bubbles from its body, and they shot outward in every direction.

Atlas darted aside, avoiding the drifting spheres.

But when he dove toward one direction, he caught sight of something else in the corner of his vision. One of the orbs was flying straight toward the other monsters on the battlefield.

And in that instant, Atlas understood.

This monster was a support type.

As long as this creature was not destroyed, the other two boss monsters would never be defeated.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 366 - Annihilation in the Storm
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Atlas could now control far more crystal shards than before. In total, thirty-two rotated wildly around his body. He shot through the air at high speed, dodging every attack from the jellyfish monster while sending shards crashing down from above.

The monster split its focus between attacking Elyndra and attacking Atlas, unleashing one powerful ability after another and shifting its targets constantly.

When massive lightning bubbles appeared around the creature, dozens of them at once, Atlas immediately sent his crystal shards flying. They struck the bubbles and burst them apart quickly.

Elyndra kept firing magic missiles nonstop at the monster as well.

A loud roar echoed as four massive cannon-like blasts formed around the creature’s body and fired in all directions.

Atlas and Elyndra flew fast as the cannons swept across the sky, chasing them.

They were incredibly fast. Atlas could not outrun them, only dodge at the last second as the huge beams chased him relentlessly.

Another attack pattern ended. Atlas continued sending his crystal shards at the monster, though without a specific skill the shards simply struck its body without dealing meaningful damage.

The lightning jellyfish shifted its tentacles and suddenly dashed, teleporting to a different position in the sky.

Atlas and Elyndra dove and kept launching attacks at the creature from afar.

A sharp roar echoed again as the monster released lightning in every direction.

Elyndra flew upward and formed a massive protective cube around the monster, trapping the lightning blasts inside. She held the barrier with great strength until the final explosion blasted against her and sent her flying back at high speed.

"Elyndra!" Atlas shouted loudly.

But when he saw the girl flap her wings and rise again, he knew she was still alright.

Atlas had to find the chance to activate his ultimate attack. But before that moment came, they needed to keep forcing the monster to fire its abilities again and again until an opening appeared.

The problem was that this creature seemed to have an endless set of magic spells. It unleashed magic nonstop without ever showing signs of slowing down.

At one moment it roared and detonated the entire area with waves of lightning. Then came cannons, then lightning bubbles, then volleys of magic missiles scattering across the sky.

Getting close to it was nearly impossible. The only advantage was that the monster itself had poor mobility. It moved slowly, and although it could teleport, it did so rarely, suggesting the cooldown made it inefficient.

Then, as Atlas dodged explosion after explosion in the air, he suddenly found himself trapped. Dozens of magic bubbles surrounded him at once.

Elyndra darted toward him and cloaked both of them inside a protective shield sphere.

Explosion after explosion erupted around them until blinding light filled the entire sky.

The monster was firing attacks from every direction.

Elyndra, floating in front of Atlas, coughed blood as she forced herself to hold the barrier against such overwhelming power.

"Elyndra?" he said.

"I can handle it."

They remained inside the protective sphere for several seconds while the outside world shook with constant blasts. Then, finally, the barrier shattered and the two of them were thrown down, slamming into the ground together.

Another massive lightning cannon formed immediately and shot toward them.

Atlas and Elyndra had no time to speak. They just flew apart as fast as possible to dodge the attack.

The monster’s assaults were unbelievably powerful and deadly.

But at that moment, Atlas sensed something. The opening was coming.

The monster showed a slight delay before its next skill.

Atlas used Magnetic Step skills several times and shot through the air to dodge, this time using the shadow tendrils as additional hands.

[Epic - Terra Pulse Annihilation (Active Skill) activated.]

He pulled all thirty-two Earth Crystals toward his palm, where they spun violently, and the two shadow tendril limbs moved with him, helping restrain the chaotic energy gathering in his hand.

Atlas rushed through the sky while charging the skill. This attack would be far stronger than the last time. He was using thirty-two Earth Crystals now. But it would also deal brutal damage to his own arm.

Fortunately, the shadow tendrils reduced the drawback a lot.

Atlas grit his teeth hard as the muscle fibers in his right arm continued to tear with every second. He dodged the monster’s attacks again and again while charging the ability, his movements sharp and fast.

Elyndra flew on the opposite side, launching a constant barrage of magic missiles from afar.

Atlas kept circling the monster from afar, dodging again and again as it unleashed repeated magic spells across the entire sky. Its abilities were truly overwhelming.

Magic cannons blasted outward in every direction, lightning bubbles spread rapidly, magic missiles filled the air, and Elyndra held them back as best as she could. But Atlas could see she would not last much longer. He had to end this now.

Another massive barrage of explosions erupted around the monster, and Atlas knew this was the opening.

He clenched his teeth and shot forward at full speed. This was do or die. He had to land the strike.

At that moment, Elyndra swooped in from behind, grabbing him around the waist and helping propel them both forward toward the monster with a burst of speed.

Atlas grit his teeth, no longer feeling the pain as the massive coiling energy in his hand surged. He thrust the charged magic toward the monster.

Yes. This was the perfect moment.

Time stopped.

Atlas saw the magic sphere touch the monster’s body. And then...

Everything exploded at once as Atlas was thrown backward along with Elyndra, both of them sent flying violently through the air.

In that instant, Atlas felt the effect of the Demonic Reaper fade from his body, returning him to his normal form.

They crashed to the ground, rolling and bouncing uncontrollably. The earth itself could not soften the impact as they were thrown far across the field. They separated mid-flight, each smashing through thick tree trunks.

Atlas finally stopped after slamming back-first into a massive tree, landing in a sitting position against its base.

His vision blurred, his breath unsteady... but through the haze he saw the system message glowing before him.

He had defeated the monster.

[You have killed Skyrift Vespera Lv. 212]

[You received 21,546,289 Exp]

[You have leveled up]

[You have leveled up]

[You have leveled up]

[You have leveled up]

[Level 167]

[Exp: 2,300,203/5,625,326]

Atlas was still gasping for air, his body feeling as if it were being pulled down into the ground, ready to collapse into unconsciousness. But he could not faint here. He could not run out of stamina now. He had to stay awake. The battle was not over.

He pushed himself up and staggered toward Elyndra, who lay on her side on the ground.

He shook her gently and turned her body over. "Elyndra!" he said, watching her closely.

At last she opened her eyes. She had used an incredible amount of energy, and she had taken the brunt of countless attacks so Atlas could land that final heavy strike.

Atlas helped her sit up, looking at her expressionless face. Then she gave a faint, tired smile.

"We defeated it," she whispered.

Atlas nodded slowly. "Yes... we defeated that monster."

Elyndra leaned closer and embraced him. "I was so scared," she said in a raw, trembling voice. "The attack was so strong. I was afraid I could not protect you. I was afraid I would fail my task."

It might have been the first time Atlas ever heard Elyndra speak so openly.

Atlas returned the embrace. "You did your job extremely well. You even helped me deliver the attack at the very last moment."

They stayed like that for a short moment while Atlas looked out toward the distant battlefield.

The other two field boss monsters had also been defeated by Edrik and the others. Now the question was whether these three monsters would be enough for Atlas to secure the two monster cores they needed.

At that moment Atlas finally felt the sharp pain in his arm. "Argh..." he reacted quietly.

Elyndra immediately loosened her embrace and checked his right arm. Atlas looked as well, and he froze.

The outer layer of muscle was completely torn. Nearly a third of the surface of his arm looked destroyed from the attack he released.

"My lord, I will heal your wound," she said.

But Atlas knew better. Elyndra had already used nearly all her Mana and Stamina during the battle, and he could see it clearly in her pale face and the way her hands trembled weakly.

Atlas gently stopped her hand.

In the distance, Edrik was sprinting toward them, teleporting repeatedly until he appeared a short distance away.

"What about the monster cores?" Atlas asked.

Before Edrik could answer, Atlas saw Dullorak in the distance finally stop his lightning attack and descend to the ground.

That alone was enough of an answer.

They had secured the two monster cores they needed.

The battle was over, and they had won - decisively.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 367 - Lightning Left Behind
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The Nimbus Carriage shot forward with flashes of thunderous lightning, leaving the massive floating island of storm and lightning far behind. Even the booming crashes and roaring explosions from the island were still clearly heard as the carriage, fragile and small compared to the mighty island, sped away into the distance.

Could Atlas really say he had managed to conquer this floating island, the rank-4 floating island with its brutally harsh environment?

Yes, he and his team had taken down several field boss monsters, creatures with Epic-grade strength, and they even managed to bring back trophies from the hunt. Yet he knew well that an island of that size should still harbor monsters far stronger than the ones they encountered.

At the very least, they were lucky that the monsters they met were only Epic grade. Not even Unique.

Some time later, they approached Kaeris’s Floating Island and soon after that, everyone was given treatment, because their condition had been that terrible. Almost everyone, even Kurogasa, who always seemed the most composed and able to handle anything with near perfect control.

**

Atlas found himself sitting on a bed, leaning back after who knew how long he had been asleep since arriving here. Two people were tending his wounds at his side, casting healing magic over him as they worked.

"How is Elyndra’s condition?" he said afterward.

A young woman in her early twenties, also one of Atlas’s subordinates, lifted her face. "My lord, Lady Elyndra has recovered, but Lord Edrik forbids her from using her power for now because she exhausted too much strength in the last fight, so she might not be able to use her abilities for a while after this."

Atlas gave a slow nod.

He looked at the wound on his arm, the one whose marks were now fading as the muscle fibers had finally reconnected. Even though the large scar was still visible, it looked like his skin held the trail of a river cut deep across it. And now both of his arms carried that same mark.

At this rate, Atlas would truly end up collecting many scars across his body.

Even though the healing could help regenerate him, it did not mean the scars would vanish perfectly one hundred percent. It was still fortunate that he regained full function of his hand and did not lose it altogether.

So, scars were still better than losing his life, at the very least

Until the door to the room was knocked on and two people walked inside, a girl in casual clothing with black hair and beside her a short-haired redhead.

"Yo, Atlas," Ember said.

"Hi, I see you two are doing well," he said with a faint smile.

"We should be the ones saying that after you almost dragged yourself and your precious people into death in that suicide mission, haha," Ember said as they stood near Atlas while he was still receiving treatment.

"I heard you managed to get what you were looking for there," Luna said afterward. "Congrats for that. Although, I am sure only you would dare to pull off something this crazy. At least I believe everyone in your team really acted perfectly and executed everything so well."

"Everyone fought hard," Atlas said, turning to Luna, and she gave him a smile after that.

"And... thanks to you as well," she continued.

Atlas tilted his head.

"I also gained experience points from the battle you went through. You know that, right?"

"Yes, I knew it, but thanks to you our combat potential was pushed to the maximum, Luna, so what about it."

"Now I have reached level 175. And with at least two more wins in the lord battle, I will reach rank 3."

"A lord at level 150 should qualify for rank 3. It is great to hear you are catching up. With this, we have a bigger chance of winning in the next battle."

She smiled and nodded afterward, responding to Atlas’s words.

Actually, Atlas himself was already at level 167, which meant he had also met the first qualification to rise to rank 3. However, his rank points were still far too low to reach that rank. And his subordinates, overall, still had not caught up in terms of their average levels.

So, if Atlas truly advanced to rank 3 before he was fully prepared, it would turn out badly as well.

"How many rank points do you have right now, Atlas?" Luna continued.

"Ninety," he said. "I still need four hundred ten more rank points to reach rank 3."

Luna nodded after that. "You also just advanced to rank 2 last season, so you still have plenty of time."

At least now that Atlas was at a safe level, he could focus on sharpening his skills and strengthening his combat techniques. He also needed to train every element he had already acquired.

He had truly reached a new stage in both Earth and Magnetic Earth, yet even then it was still far from perfect, and his tasks were nowhere near finished.

He still had to improve his combat techniques using Shadow and Blood, which were both still quite behind. Not to mention he needed to keep pushing his progress with Fire and Air after going deep with Water and Earth.

Training after training, yet Atlas could clearly see the results of everything he had done.

Like... he actually managed to defeat a level 212 Epic Grade monster. He and Elyndra took it down with only the two of them.

He knew that he received support from Luna’s ability, and Dullorak had assisted from afar as well, but it was still an incredibly huge leap from anything he ever thought he could achieve.

Because once monsters pass level 200, they also receive massive upgrades to their abilities, just like humans.

Some time later, another person entered the room, and just as Atlas expected, Edrik stepped inside, already in perfect condition and looking completely fine.

"Well, get enough rest, Atlas. You can take it easy for now while we finish leveling up the forces," Ember said.

Luna smiled and nodded gently. "Tell us anything you need."

Then the two of them left afterward.

Edrik then stood at the end of Atlas’s bed with a faint smile and gave him a slight bow.

"You seem fully recovered, my lord. Planning to go on another expedition while we still have time?" he said, though Atlas knew the man was teasing him.

"You are getting bolder, huh, Edrik."

Edrik smiled after that. "I want to show you the incredible loot we gained in the final moments of yesterday’s battle," he said.

"The monster cores?" he continued with a curious expression.

During the trip back, they had all been in such terrible condition that there had been almost no conversation until they were rescued on this island.

Edrik bowed again and lifted both his hands.

A faint energy began to appear as something formed above his palms. It was shaped like a sphere, no, slightly elongated at one end. Like an egg...

A massive egg with a black outer layer and streaks of yellow running across the entire shell.

"An egg?"

Edrik gave a faint smile. "A beast egg, tempest-class. This is a great gift for you. You possess elements that match it perfectly," he continued.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 368 - The Light We Share
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Edrik placed the egg on the small table beside the bed, where a round cushion had already been prepared for it. It seemed Edrik had arranged all of this even before showing the item.

Atlas then lifted his right hand, the one still being healed, causing the two healers to pause in what they were doing to him.

He touched the surface of the egg and felt the faint vibration of electricity reacting even before his fingers made full contact.

"That reaction did not happen when I touched it," Edrik said.

"Is it because of lightning?"

"Not only because of Lightning, my lord. Beasts, especially those with high grades, have awareness far above the lower ones. They choose and they judge. If you can supply enough mana to hatch this egg, there is a high chance you will form a strong affinity with the beast, which will make it much easier for you to tame it."

Atlas looked back at Edrik and leaned his body against the pillows again. "Which monster?"

Edrik smiled once more. "The bird. The one we faced yesterday was a different type from the one we fought before. This one has a talent similar to phoenix rebirth. After we killed it, it did not leave a monster core. Instead, it returned in the form of an egg. So, what will come out of that egg is a bird-type monster, at least Epic grade."

"At least?"

Edrik bowed again afterward. "If we can supply high-quality mana and provide other nutrients during the incubation process, the grade might become higher than Epic, my lord."

"Interesting. I think it would be a good idea to return to that island and hunt more birds. What do you think?"

Atlas said it with a flat expression, matching Edrik’s equally flat face, and even the two others in the room looked shocked when Atlas said such a thing.

"M-my lord," the girl beside him said nervously. "Y-you have not fully recovered."

Edrik let out a long breath after hearing that suggestion.

Atlas smiled and answered the girl, "No, I am only joking."

He then gave a small laugh and looked back at Edrik.

"I know you are not completely joking," Edrik replied. "But to give you an answer, finding a beast with phoenix rebirth talent is not easy, my lord, so out of ten boss monsters we might encounter, the chance is even zero."

"It is unusual for you to stop me like this," he said with a grin, still joking.

Atlas then gave a slow nod, since he already had experience with supplying mana to an egg like this.

"For the other supplements the egg needs, I will prepare everything. You only have to supply your mana regularly, once a day. And after you finish that, I will take it back and store it inside my dimensional space."

"Good, I leave everything to you."

Done with the egg, a very great loot. Some time after that, Atlas finished his treatment and was now able to use his hand again, although he was still not allowed to use mana or any elemental ability with that hand to make sure everything worked and recovered properly first.

There was one thing Atlas wanted to do, and because of that he found himself later on the upper surface of the island, where wide stretches of mountains, valleys, and hills lay. He walked toward a large tree at the edge of a field of blooming flowers.

There was a woman sitting on a metal bench there, who noticed Atlas’s presence a moment later.

A girl in a simple white dress with green hair, who then stood up and hurriedly walked back toward Atlas.

"My lord, uhm, I... I was just... is there anything I can help with..." Elyndra said, sounding a bit nervous, which was obvious from the way she spoke.

"I wanted to meet you, and I knew you were here," he said, then looked toward the flower garden. "Let us sit there. This place is quiet and bright, after the weeks we spent surrounded by storms."

The girl regained her composure, bowed once, and accompanied Atlas as they walked until they sat together on the bench.

Elyndra sat on the left side of Atlas, though she kept a slight distance and took the very edge of the seat, as if she had done something wrong even though she should have been relaxed and comfortable sitting there.

They stayed there for a while, sitting together in silence, with only the sound of the wind blowing, softer this time, making the flowers across the wide field sway gently and release a refreshing scent that felt truly calming.

"I heard it from Edrik." Atlas finally spoke.

He turned toward the girl, and Elyndra looked back at him for a moment before lowering her gaze slightly.

"You pushed yourself too far. How bad is the effect?" he continued.

Elyndra lifted her head briefly. "I will not be able to perform my duties during this phase, my lord. Possibly ten years, at the fastest."

She fell silent for a moment before continuing, "Faeries have very strict limits on how we use our power."

She continued speaking in a tone that sounded more like telling a story. For a while, Atlas simply listened and asked small questions in response to what she shared.

Until, some time later, Atlas asked, "Will the bond help you remove the seal that restrains your power?"

Right after Atlas said this, Elyndra went silent again, completely still, as if something was holding her lips from speaking.

She then tried to speak with a lower voice. "Yes, my lord... it will allow me to use my power freely, and it will let me accelerate my level much faster. It will also evolve me and advance my class," she said softly.

Atlas fell silent for a moment, choosing his words carefully before continuing.

"I..." he paused briefly. "Need an affinity for the Light element," he said.

Elyndra looked at Atlas again with those gentle, calm eyes of hers.

"Is something like that something I can gain if..." he searched for the right words, "I form a bond with a faerie?"

Elyndra showed a clear change in her expression, visible through her eyes. "My lord..." she answered. "Humans mostly gain advancement based on what they already possess, and they also receive the unique traits belonging to their bond fae partner. So if you bond with a fae who has an affinity for the Light element, the chance of inheriting it is very high."

Atlas nodded slowly, then continued. "If both of us benefit, is that enough of a reason to perform this bond, Elyndra?"

"M-my lord..." she said, startled. "Forgive me..." she hesitated again.

"Yes, I need it. And I know you can provide it, not because I would ever force it with authority, but because we both stand to gain from this. So let me carry the part of your power that will push us both ahead, and let us take this path as partners by choice, not by duty, shaping the future we aim to reach."

Elyndra froze at Atlas’s final words, and... the change in her expression became unmistakably clear afterward.

"My lord... for a fae, this is a great honor," she said softly. "We are given only one chance in our lifetime to choose the human we will serve, and that choice becomes part of our soul. It is something we are born longing for, a moment that defines who we are meant to become."

She drew a small breath, her voice trembling yet steady with conviction.

"When you offer me this path... I fear that my readiness might seem improper, because the truth is I am willing. I am truly willing to give this one chance I possess, the only one I will ever have, to serve you."

Her eyes lowered with respect. "Allow me to become your strength. Allow me to walk beside you with the power I can offer. If this bond will shape what lies ahead, then let me be part of the future you strive to reach."

What did this bond truly mean for the faeries? After all, they lived a different kind of life and grew in ways entirely unlike humans.

Was it something like marriage?

No, they could still marry other faeries if they wished.

Then was it slavery, a form of servitude?

Whatever it was, Atlas would not allow such a fate to define him. He would decide for himself how to forge this path, for his sake and for everyone under him.

Atlas reached for Elyndra’s hand, and the girl slowly moved closer to him, falling gently into his embrace.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 369 - Faerie Soulbond
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Growth should always be something positive. There should be nothing wrong with that statement. Massive growth, big leaps, or even something that is fast, wild, and brutal.

The same happened with Atlas. He rose to rank 2 within only two lord battle seasons, and even in the past few weeks his level had skyrocketed. He was already close to level 200 even before finishing one more class advancement quest he received at level 100.

He acquired all basic elements, one special element, Dark, then advanced elements and composite ones as well.

He built an alliance by leading twelve other lords under his banner. He accepted challenges from alliances far stronger than his own.

Atlas also formed a blood contract with Morganna, a sacred bond with Lyrassa. And now, another bond with Elyndra?

Was this rapid growth truly a good thing? Was it the right path? Was taking everything he could acquire always the correct choice?

Had he overlooked something important through this fast rise, leaving parts of his development untempered and opening a deep weakness inside himself?

Like... how was he supposed to integrate everything? All basic elements, all special elements, all advanced and composite elements, or even ancient elements if he ever reached them?

Was he missing something valuable by growing too fast?

Was he?

Should he hold himself back?

Atlas stood straight as he looked at Elyndra, who now stood before him with her hands joined and head lowered, while her green and golden light-like aura seemed to dance gently around her form.

Was this the right decision? Was this truly the time? Should he not restrain himself a little longer and let everything progress more naturally, ensuring he mastered everything perfectly before taking another path?

Should he not?

[A unique bonding ritual has begun.]

[Warning: The Faerie Soulbond can only occur once in a Fae’s entire lifetime.]

[Proceeding will permanently intertwine your essence with Elyndra of Yunatea.]

But... Atlas lifted his head and shook it. He had a thousand reasons to keep moving forward, to break through everything, to take everything. Like...

Which was better? To hesitate and miss the opportunities passing right before his eyes, or to take everything as long as he could hold it?

He was an amateur who still knew little about this world, about the lord battles. But no, he did not need to know everything. Because he had everything he needed to find every answer.

His elite subordinates, they knew more, had lived through more. Edrik, Kurogasa, Dullorak, they always gave him the best feedback, ensured he took the best path, and supported him in every situation.

Atlas might seem reckless, indeed. But... he could never be extraordinary or exceptional if he chose a mediocre road, right?

He had to take the extraordinary path to become the strongest and most remarkable.

[Elyndra has willingly offered her soul to you.]

[Your acceptance establishes a sacred, irreversible pact.]

[Your spirit is being aligned with hers.]

[Your cores are synchronizing.]

[A faint resonance moves through your body...]

[Light, wind, purity, serenity...]

[As the ancient magic of the Fae acknowledges you as Elyndra’s chosen partner.]

Atlas felt the elemental particles around them surge violently, and the energy flowing from Elyndra’s body wrapped around him as he stood directly before her.

Responsibility did not burden a person. It made them stronger. It pushed them through things they had never reached before, things they could not do, could not endure, or lacked the courage to face.

And the one thing Atlas wanted to be certain of was...

He had the will to walk this harsh path, and the harsher it was, the better it would be if it pushed him to advance faster and grow stronger.

He committed to this.

He would evolve, again and again, farther and higher. High Human? He would not stop there. He would keep evolving and growing stronger.

To master, to conquer, to claim victory... Was that not the very essence of a Lord chosen by this system?

[You have successfully completed the Faerie Soulbond with Elyndra, the Halo Wind Seraph.]

Atlas smiled when the system message appeared before him. He tightened both his fists firmly.

He had to learn to truly let go and stop holding himself back. Not because he had been restraining himself intentionally, but because he needed to chase harder and strike even further.

Morganna had already pointed this out to him. She said that Atlas was holding himself back. Edrik had repeatedly pushed him to take steps Atlas spent too long deciding on.

Like... he was not even close to the level of a lord who could hold a domain for hundreds or thousands of years. Atlas had only been a lord for barely over a year. Right?

[This bond is not merely magical.]

[It is spiritual, elemental, and eternal.]

[Her one lifetime choice... now belongs to you.]

At that moment, Atlas saw Elyndra’s eyes open again as she lifted her face and looked straight at him. A pair of golden wings unfolded from her back, and her armor materialized fully around her.

Atlas could feel the new power, something entirely new, radiating intensely from the girl. Like... could he easily say that Elyndra had become almost thirty percent or even fifty percent stronger?

He could feel it clearly, the surge of elemental particles strengthening around her body.

[Light Element Affinity: None → Low]

[Due to high compatibility, affinity growth rate increased by 300%.]

[Your body now accepts Light mana naturally.]

[The doorway to High Human evolution is opened.]

"High Human..." Atlas murmured softly as he read the notification.

So it is getting closer. Just a little more, and I will evolve and break out of this human vessel, to finally become a better race with a longer lifespan than a weak human, just like Morganna always says.

[New Skill Unlocked]

[Haloborn Resonance (Passive)]

[Light and Air abilities gain +25% efficiency]

[Incoming Light damage reduced by 40%]

[Healing received increased by 30%]

[Your presence stabilizes Elyndra’s core, accelerating her recovery by +150%]

Haloborn Resonance... with Elyndra being a healer, even though she possessed versatile abilities, she still could not sustain herself in prolonged battles. With this skill, Atlas not only empowered himself but could also empower Elyndra whenever they fought together.

A one hundred fifty percent boost was more than double her normal recovery speed. This was an incredibly massive upgrade.

Now that Atlas had access to four basic elements and two special elements, he would truly gain the ability to reach every advanced and composite element available in the system.

Atlas could practically wield all elements if he genuinely put in the effort to utilize everything.

Like... even in yesterday’s battle against that Epic-grade monster above level 200, Atlas only used his newly trained Earth and Magnetic Earth elements. He did not even employ everything he had. He did not use a single skill from his Tempest Reaver class.

But he also knew he could not master every style of combat. Having many abilities was not better than mastering fewer ones to a deeper, sharper level.

So Atlas would continue focusing on the fighting style he already excelled in. Combining short-range melee combat with massive area-of-effect skills that dealt enormous damage.

[Joint Soul Passive]

[New Blessing Unlocked...]

And he knew the rewards still had more to reveal.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 370 - Dual-Cast Ultimate
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[Joint Soul Passive]

[New Blessing Unlocked: Seraphic Link (Passive)]

[Atlas Gains: Stronger Light affinity growth, improved mana purity, enhanced resistance to corruption, curse, void, and necrosis, and minor passive regeneration during daytime or near Light sources.]

[Elyndra Gains: Full seal suppression when near Atlas, mana stabilization and faster core healing, +50% skill recovery speed, and +1 Class Advancement potential unlocked.]

[Elyndra can now grow without limits under your authority.]

[Her level cap and class cap have both been released.]

[A portion of your experience gain will flow into her core once she recovers.]

Atlas had not even finished admiring each reward he received, because every system message that appeared kept giving him something even more extraordinary.

This time it was the Seraphic Link, granting advantages to both Atlas and Elyndra.

Just look at what he gained: resistance to corruption, curse void, necrosis resistance, and even passive regeneration.

Atlas swallowed hard. He now had affinity for both Dark and Light. Would this not allow him to stay fully active for twenty-four hours straight?

Even though he still had no idea what would happen if he combined Dark and Light together. What kind of ability would emerge from that fusion?

Besides that, Elyndra also received more benefits whenever she fought near Atlas. This faery bond truly made fae into the primary support for a human partner, since they could only perform at their full potential when close to their chosen human.

It was still something remarkable. With this, Elyndra would no longer be held back by anything, and she would strengthen Atlas’s team even more.

[The Halo Wind has accepted you.]

[Your spirits have intertwined.]

[Faerie Soulbond: Complete.]

Atlas stood still for a moment as he looked at the system message. Was this where it ended?

Not that he was complaining. He simply wanted to be sure there was nothing else he needed to understand from this faery bond.

He turned toward Elyndra once everything settled, until the girl spoke again in her quiet, calm voice.

"I have... something more that I will give to you, my lord..." she said softly.

"And..." Atlas answered quietly. "What is it?"

The girl approached, the pair of wings folding neatly behind her back. She bowed once and offered her hand.

Atlas instinctively took it, and more system messages appeared before him.

[New Faerie Bond Gift Detected]

[Evaluating compatibility...]

[Your combined Light resonance exceeds the threshold.]

[You have received a new Legendary Dual-Cast Ultimate: Lightstorm Judgement.]

[Category: Single-Target Divine Execution]

[Sub-type: Seraphic Authority]

[Your connection to the Halo Wind amplifies the spell’s destructive purity.]

"Legendary skill?" Atlas whispered, staring at the girl again.

She nodded gently, and then more detailed descriptions of the skill formed before Atlas’s eyes.

[Legendary - Seraphic Breaker: Dawnfall Lance (Conditional Active Skill)]

[A dual-soul technique that manifests the Lance of Dawn. Light-based actions charge the spear. Once fully charged, it is hurled or used in a high-speed impalement, detonating in a burst of purified Light.]

[Delivers a fully charged single-target divine impalement for 1,200% Light–Storm damage, ignoring 60% defense, with an additional 300% pure damage infused directly into the target’s core on impact.]

Atlas slowly read the description of the skill, and one thing he immediately took note of was that this was a skill cast together with Elyndra, marked as Legendary grade, and categorized as a single-target strike.

"Single-target skills are something I am lacking right now," he said. "And this answers that problem."

The girl gave a soft smile. "Once you activate the skill, my lord, it will manifest the Lance of Dawn," she said.

Atlas nodded slowly. "The lance needs to be charged, and both of us can charge it by using Light-based skills during battle."

He tried to understand the activation conditions carefully.

"But since I have not developed my Light element very far yet, once I activate this skill, I will need you to provide most of the charge for the lance."

The girl bowed gently again. "I will fulfill that task to the best of my ability, my lord."

A single-target Legendary skill, built from Light and Storm elements.

Atlas swallowed hard just reading through it.

This was truly an extraordinary gift, even though he only now realized that the ability still adapted itself to Atlas’s dominant fighting style, which used spears.

Finished with all these things, she did not realize they had already stayed in this place for two hours. Has time really passed that long?

They then walked slowly, planning to head back inside as they moved side by side afterward.

"Do you miss Yunatea, Elyn?" Atlas asked, speaking in a relaxed tone.

The girl did not answer right away. Instead, she lifted her face and smiled. "Home is a place where someone is waiting and looking forward to your return," she said.

She let out a small laugh after that. "And Gacha Haven is my new home," she added, turning toward Atlas.

"I might miss Yunatea, my lord, but there is no one there who remembers me anymore."

Atlas reached out and touched the girl’s head, gently brushing her hair. "Have you made good friends with the people in Gacha Haven?"

The girl smiled and nodded. Then she looked forward as they walked, and Atlas could see her steps were lighter than usual.

"I did not just find friends here, but teachers, companions in hardship, and even... a family," she said. "Mira is very kind and always shows me around the island. Lord Edrik always checks on my condition and asks if I need anything."

She continued. "Lady Morganna, she is truly a strong and inspiring figure. Lord Dullorak... he really holds every bit of knowledge the world has. And he can even access many elements."

The girl kept talking, mentioning the names of people in Gacha Haven one by one. It was clear she had formed far deeper connections than Atlas had ever known Elyndra had made with them.

They kept talking and walking slowly while the air on the island felt truly calm and gentle. Because of the island’s unique nature, there were no structures at all on the surface. It felt like walking across an empty island.

Until... in the distance, Atlas noticed someone standing there, straight and still, as if waiting for their arrival.

Edrik stood tall right in front of him when Atlas finally stopped before the man.

Edrik gave a small bow toward Atlas, his face as flat as always.

"The Heart of the Void," he said, speaking about the item Atlas had won at the auction some time ago.

He then continued. "It has been waiting and is ready for you to conquer, my lord. You are more prepared now than you have ever been at any time before..."

He paused after a long explanation. "You never fail to make me proud of you."

Atlas smiled faintly, then walked past Edrik and tapped his arm.

"I guess you have been waiting for this moment, huh," he said.

They walked together afterward.

Edrik spoke again. "You never fail to take the best step you can, my lord. This is a smart move. And... I am certain things will not end here..."

"You are too straightforward."

"My apologies, my lord. I am only trying to offer the best advice I can. And there are still many options ahead, each one bringing strong benefits and remarkable upgrades. I can help remind you of them."

Shut up!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 371 - The Heart of the Void
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Atlas gave himself a moment to simply enjoy his day. The delicious dishes and the company of a few important people from Gacha Haven filled the wide dining hall, a room large enough to hold thousands of tables.

The room’s ceiling rose high above them, even though the entire space was actually deep underground.

At that time, only they remained inside, since the others were out landing and hunting on the floating island beneath this one.

After spending nearly three weeks inside a storm-filled island, he truly missed meals as good as this, prepared by the best chefs Gacha Haven had.

While savoring the food, he also checked his latest status. He had leveled up drastically, and he still had several stat points left unassigned. Because of that, he took a moment to think it over and review them as he ate.

[Name: Atlas Blackthorn]

[Level: 167]

[Strength: 450 | Agility: 370 | Intelligence: 420 | Constitution: 320 | Stamina: 400]

[Available Stat Points: 2]

[Job: The Lord]

[Class: Tempest Reaver]

Nice, it looked truly perfect. And level 167 stood there with a grand presence. He only needed a few more pushes to reach level 200. Although for that, he also had to catch up and reach rank 3 first.

His target was clear after this. He would push all his forces to break through level 150, and if possible go beyond that, maybe even reach 160.

After that, he planned to gather as many Rank Points as he could for the next event. If he managed to reach rank 3 quickly, he would gain far more access to many features and privileges that only higher rank lords could use.

At the very least, with a level this high, Atlas now had enough strength to face Bolin and his army.

Atlas had massive area skills, ones he had already used some time ago. Even so, he knew that what he destroyed back then had only been that lord’s weakest troops.

But Atlas also understood that he could not afford to underestimate other lords either, not when they had yet to reveal the true power they carried, or what they had been preparing for the next battle against his Alliance.

He drew a slow breath. In less than a week, they would all return to their own floating islands and declare the challenge for the Alliance Battle.

Atlas did not want to waste this short amount of time. He needed to bring home one more remarkable achievement.

Because of that, later that afternoon, when another session was ready to begin, Atlas, along with Elyndra, Edrik, Kurogasa, and Dullorak, gathered on the island’s surface. They stood among large stone formations that formed a wind shield, giving them a safe space to stand and discuss on the small rocky ground beneath their feet.

They were all sitting this time, with Atlas in the center. Dullorak sat cross-legged, his body slightly lifted, positioned not far in front of Atlas.

"The Heart of the Void," Dullorak said as a small crate suddenly appeared in his hand. It floated slowly and steadily toward Atlas afterward.

"I have made some modifications to this core, my lord," Dullorak continued. "And with this method, you will be able to absorb the core more efficiently, although the process will take a bit more time."

"It is okay. Now tell me what you have done, Dullorak."

"Before that..." Dullorak replied calmly. "Do you not want to know more about the High Human, my lord, since you already have an affinity for the Light element as well?"

"Yes," Atlas answered. "How do I reach that phase? How do I evolve my body into something higher?"

Dullorak nodded slowly. "In truth, when a human reaches a higher level and masters an element of a higher rank, either advanced, composite or ancient, they will naturally gain a longer lifespan."

Atlas nodded quietly, because the concept of a High Human itself was a better version of an ordinary human. A longer lifespan, stronger regeneration, better control over magic, and so on.

Edrik then responded. "Among us, the closest examples might be Karian. He has a better body and everything else because of his habit of consuming higher grade monsters, including dragons. While Milo, even though he had a higher rank in the past, when we speak of lifespan, Karian’s age had already surpassed Milo’s before."

Atlas simply nodded at that explanation. "Are Karian and Milo already considered High Humans?"

"Not yet, but they are close," Edrik said. "They can be described as the peak a human can reach. But they still have a lifespan that is not as long as beings like vampires or spirits. They can still grow old."

Dullorak continued. "A High Human," he said, pausing for a moment. "In theory, they are almost immortal. Not because they have no limit, but because they can keep regenerating the cells in their bodies. And this regeneration speed surpasses the natural cell damage a human normally suffers. So as long as they keep caring for their body and continue to train, they will keep living."

"Got it," Atlas replied.

"However..." Dullorak continued. "Simplifying the High Human by calling it only better regeneration feels a bit misleading. Because in truth, to reach the High Human stage, a person must change several parts of the body they have now, sometimes even more than that."

Atlas frowned for a moment. "That does not sound pleasant."

Dullorak nodded again. "It is quite complex. Let us call this process Body Refinement. A human can perform a special ritual. The ritual forces the body to purge weak cells and rebuild stronger ones. The process is long and painful, but if the person survives, the new body structure becomes the foundation of a High Human."

"So that indirectly replaces the body I have now?"

"Not a full replacement, my lord, but slowly renewing the damaged parts and perfecting them."

"Can we perform that right away? Is what I have now enough?"

Dullorak gave a faint smile and nodded slowly. "Before I answer your question, I have one of my own."

Atlas nodded as well, ready to listen.

"How far would you allow something within you to change?"

Atlas fell silent, his mind turning over the question. It was one of those things that needed a moment of thought before its meaning truly settled in.

"Does it matter to you," Dullorak continued, "if that change turns you into something other than a High Human. Perhaps another race that stands above ordinary humans?"

It should have been an easy question for him. After all, he had already formed a bond with Morganna, a vampire.

His first child was not even human but a Dhampir. One with a lifespan far longer and strength far greater than any normal man.

There was also Lyrassa, who in her previous life had lived for more than five hundred years, and now Elyndra, a Faerie who had become part of his life as well.

To Atlas, whatever made him stronger was something he could learn to accept.

"As long as I still look human on the outside, I think I can live with it," he said with a faint smile. "Even the Undead seem tempting when you realize they can wield Animus endlessly without rest."

But... No, he was not ready to become one of the Undead. Not if it meant losing the simple joy of tasting food.

"So... is that answer good enough for you?" he asked.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 372 - Teach Me Darkness
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"All right then," Dullorak replied after a brief pause. "We can proceed with absorbing the Heart of the Void," he concluded.

Atlas turned his gaze toward the core resting inside the small crate before him.

"I’ve made a few modifications to this core," Dullorak continued, "so that you can absorb its essence and merge it into yourself more efficiently. You’ll do it by..."

He then began explaining the overall concept of the process. In essence, Atlas would need to cultivate the core within his own inner core. Absorbing its energy by entering a state of deep consciousness.

Within that mental realm, he would find himself inside the Heart of the Void, where he must face and overcome whatever challenges awaited him there.

In simpler terms, it would be a battle, or perhaps a series of trials. While his physical body remained still, his mind and soul doing all the work.

It sounded straightforward enough, and as Dullorak described the process, it seemed smooth enough that Atlas would barely feel the transition at all.

"This will take time," Dullorak added, "but you can repeat it daily until you’ve completely absorbed the core. With the Light element affinity you already possess, we should be able to neutralize most of the curse that the core carries, and integrate it perfectly into your being. It’s better if we move on to practice right away, my lord."

"Agreed," Atlas replied.

Atlas closed his eyes and did exactly as Dullorak had instructed. He focused on sensing every elemental particle around him. Almost immediately, he saw a massive surge of dark elemental particles pouring out from the core before him.

The concentration was immense. Far greater than anything he had felt before. It reminded him of what Kurogasa had said: that the price he had paid for this item was nothing compared to the true value it held, especially for someone who knew how to use it properly.

And then... after a few moments, he felt his body being pulled forward, the sensation similar to being drawn through a teleportation portal.

When he opened his eyes again...

He found himself standing upright on a vast circular platform carved with strange magical patterns beneath his feet.

In the distance, tall trees encircled the area, forming a silent perimeter around him. At a glance, it looked as though he was standing in the middle of a ritual circle. Or something very much like it.

He looked up and saw a full moon hanging bright in the sky. Then he glanced down at his hands, flexing his fingers slowly.

He could feel everything. The faint touch of air on his skin, the movement of his muscles. Even though he knew this was all happening inside his mind, the sensation was startlingly real.

Interesting , he thought. Now then... What should he do next?

At this moment, Atlas only intended to check what kind of changes Dullorak had made. Not that he planned to immediately focus on conquering the core.

He had been warned that this would be a long process, so it was better for him to take it slowly and get used to it bit by bit.

Besides, he needed to keep his attention on the major battle awaiting him soon.

Then... something stirred nearby. A surge of dark energy burst out, forming shadowy shapes that twisted and writhed wildly, coalescing into dark tendrils that moved as if alive. They tangled together, growing larger and larger until they began taking form.

Atlas stepped back, bracing himself for whatever was coming. He tried to summon his spear, but nothing happened.

So, he could not use any of the items stored within his dimensional storage.

The massive surge of shadow began to compress, shrinking down until it took the shape of a figure. One that stood only up to Atlas’s chest.

Atlas tilted his head slightly, studying the thing that had appeared before him. A person, or something like one. Dressed in tattered clothes, its body thin and frail, with long, tangled hair that fell forward, hiding most of its face.

Damn it, that’s creepy.

The figure slowly raised its bony right hand, brushing the messy strands away to reveal a wide, gleaming eye that stared straight at Atlas. Its mouth stretched into a grin, showing a row of crooked, uneven teeth beneath that unsettling gaze.

"Fresh soul..." the strange creature rasped. "Khi! Khi! Khi!"

It laughed in a harsh, breathless way, like someone choking on their own laughter.

"Hey, hello there," Atlas said casually. "Can you tell me who you are? My friend mentioned that a challenge would be waiting for me here."

He frowned as he studied the figure before him. "But all I see is a starved-looking thing like you. So, where’s the real challenge supposed to be?"

"Khi! Khi! Khi!" the creature laughed again, its grin stretched across its flat, pale face. "You’re quite arrogant for a living being."

The energy around it suddenly grew heavier. Shadows erupted once more from its back, those tendrils twisting around its body, cloaking it until its thin frame appeared fuller, and stronger.

"That’s... cool," Atlas said with a faint smirk. "So that’s the potential of the Shadow Element when it’s fully mastered."

He tilted his head slightly. "Can you teach me how to use that ability?"

"Khi! Khi! Khi!" the figure wheezed again, laughter scraping from its throat. "You’ll end up trapped here. So you might as well watch how I do it... For all eternity!"

Atlas gave a faint smile, his eyes sharpening. "Unfortunately, I don’t plan on staying that long."

He quickly steadied himself, clenching his fists and taking a firm stance.

Before coming here, Dullorak had given him a clue. That this challenge would be much easier if he used the Light Element.

Maybe this was the perfect moment for Atlas to truly master it.

The strange figure, now completely cloaked in shadows, looked almost like a living storm of darkness. Then, in a blur, it launched itself straight at Atlas.

Atlas gritted his teeth, his body flaring with a radiant aura that wrapped around him in brilliant light, making him the only glowing figure in that pitch-black space.

He raised his guard just in time.

Too fast!

The creature appeared right before him in an instant, its movement almost impossible to track. Atlas barely had time to lift both arms to block the incoming strike.

Then, everything froze for a split second, followed by a deafening impact.

Damn it! That’s strong?!

The force of the hit lifted Atlas off his feet, hurling him backward. He crashed hard into the ground, rolling several times before slamming into something solid. A line of trees at the edge of the platform.

Before he could even catch his breath, the creature was already there again, standing in front of him.

It grabbed him by the throat, easily lifting him off the ground.

"I’ll show you what it feels like," it hissed, "when your soul is ripped out of your body."

Its left hand plunged straight into Atlas’s chest, sinking through flesh and bone as though his body were made of mist. Its fingers closed around something inside.

A surge of unbearable pain shot through Atlas’s entire being. It felt as if his very essence was being torn apart.

"No! No way!" he gasped, his eyes wide.

Am I... going to die here? the thought screamed in his head, panic flooding through him.

Dullorak never said the challenge would be this hard!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 373 - Inhale the Sky
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Atlas felt an unbearable pull. So strong it felt as if his very soul was being ripped out of his body. Then, suddenly, a blinding light pierced through his vision. He gasped sharply, struggling for breath as his body convulsed, and slowly began to reorient himself to his surroundings.

Panting heavily, he looked around and saw Dullorak sitting cross-legged, floating slightly above the ground. Elyndra was there too, her face filled with worry, and beside her stood Edrik and Kurogasa, both watching closely.

They had managed to pull him back into the real world.

Dullorak’s aged face softened with a small, almost amused smile. "How was the experience, my lord?" he asked calmly.

"The demon..." he rasped, still trying to catch his breath. "The demon tried to rip my soul apart."

"Yes, but don’t worry," Dullorak replied calmly. "I was watching everything that happened. You were never in real danger. You’ll be fine."

Atlas exhaled shakily. "Would I have truly died if you hadn’t pulled me out just now?"

"Yes and no," Dullorak replied. "Part of your soul would have been drained, but you wouldn’t have died completely. At least not right away. You’d have been trapped in a cycle of suffering. Each time losing more of yourself. Until you finally ceased to exist."

Atlas sat there, still trying to steady his breathing. What he had just gone through was unlike anything he had ever experienced.

He had faced countless life-or-death moments before. During brutal training sessions, and even on the battlefield against enemies far stronger than himself. But this was something else entirely.

For the first time, he had felt completely powerless. All he could do was endure as the demon tore into him, unable to fight back.

Wiping the sweat from his forehead, he clenched his jaw.

"I couldn’t do anything in there," he said, his voice tight with frustration. "I couldn’t even summon my spear. What am I supposed to do if everything I have is locked away from me?"

Dullorak nodded slowly before speaking. "This will take time, my lord. As I mentioned before, you’ll need to do this consistently, every single day. The concept is a little different this time..."

He continued with a long and detailed explanation, though Atlas tried to simplify it in his mind. In truth, he knew he would only truly understand it through direct practice.

What he was trying to absorb wasn’t just ordinary elemental particles like he usually did. This wasn’t simple mana that could be shaped at will.

What he aimed to conquer and absorb was a core filled with countless remnants. Fragments of souls and energies from beings who had once lived and possessed their own freedom.

To make matters even more complicated, every soul he needed to subdue carried its own distinct will and personality.

Dullorak continued, "You must master yourself. Your core, your entire soul, and then overpower their souls, their will, and their essence. Only then can you claim control over them and fully absorb the core. This process will grant you an upgrade beyond anything you can imagine, my lord. The potential here is truly immense."

"Understood," Atlas said firmly.

All he needed to do was keep trying, again and again, without stopping. And that was the one thing he was exceptionally good at.

Once Atlas set his mind on achieving something, he would pursue it relentlessly until he succeeded.

He was, in every sense of the word, a machine.

**

If Atlas were to be honest, among the four basic elements... He still wondered which one left the deepest impression on him.

Was it Fire?

After all, his brutal training had begun on a volcanic island, where he spent every day surrounded by molten lava, forcing his body to endure destruction and rebuild itself over and over again. That was the first time he truly understood what it meant to be broken down to nothing.

But as fate would have it, when he began developing his elemental affinity, the element he unlocked with the highest resonance wasn’t Fire. It was Water.

Not long after that, he awakened Lightning.

He had already pushed his mastery of Earth so far that he even unlocked its advanced form. Magnetic Earth.

But what about Air and Fire? Those two basic elements were still lagging behind for him.

Because of that, Atlas decided to adjust his daily routines in the coming days: first, continuing his attempt to conquer the Heart of the Void; second, providing mana for the new egg; and third... Well, this!

Atlas shot through the air, plummeting from an incredible height, his body slicing through the sky as he free-fell toward the floating island below.

A grin spread across his face as the fierce wind roared against him, tugging at his clothes and hair.

Below, thousands of soldiers had already leapt ahead of him toward the floating island, while Atlas used the dive merely as another form of training.

It was almost nostalgic. He had been in a situation just like this when he was chosen to become a Lord.

Back then, he had been falling freely through the air. Straight toward the depths of a bottomless ravine, toward certain death.

If the system had been even a fraction of a second slower, he might have been named a Lord at the exact moment his body shattered upon impact.

But look at him now, diving headfirst through open skies without a trace of fear.

The truth was, he had become completely at ease with the heights that once terrified him.

Atlas looked around, watching the fierce currents of air rush past him, wrapping around his body and slamming against him with incredible force.

He drew in a deep breath and closed his eyes. In that moment, he could feel an almost perfect stillness. A rare peace that came only with solitude.

Did he miss those days before he became a Lord? The days when he was cast out, unwanted by anyone?

Yeah, maybe he did. The truth was, Atlas had always been a lone fighter. He had learned to rely only on himself, to keep moving forward no matter what. Even now, despite his rank and power, that part of him hadn’t changed.

Sure, he could easily order his subordinates to handle everything for him. Each of his elites possessed incredible abilities of their own. If he wanted to, Atlas could sit back and let them run the entire island without lifting a finger.

But that wasn’t who he was.

Here he was again, pushing himself harder, forcing his limits, refusing to settle. Always striving to break through barriers that everyone else thought were impossible.

When something seemed out of reach, Atlas only pushed himself even harder to conquer it.

He clenched his teeth, feeling the air roar against his skin as he continued his high-speed descent.

He would master the Air Element. This one! This element would be his next conquest.

He grinned to himself, determination burning bright in his eyes.

He felt his entire body wrapped in the fierce currents of the rushing wind. He didn’t care about anything else. He refused to use any other element to reach the ground.

He would command the air itself to carry him!

In that very moment, Atlas felt a massive surge of Air elemental particles enveloping him. The force pulsed through his body like a living storm.

He clenched his fists tightly, eyes snapping open just as he looked down.

The vast stretch of land was rushing up to meet him.

Only moments away now!

[You have successfully triggered a new skill...]

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 374 - Gale Veil
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Someone’s falling from above!"

Someone shouted among a group of others, all dressed in full battle gear as they stood on an open field surrounded by tall, wild trees.

"What are you talking about?" another one called back.

The first man ran to a clearer spot and pointed upward. "Look! Someone’s falling!"

"No way... did they miss the jump?"

"Looks like it. Every unit should’ve already been deployed onto the island."

"But why haven’t they opened their glider yet? At that height, they’ll hit the ground hard if they don’t slow down!"

"Wait!!!" another one yelled suddenly. "That’s Lord Atlas!"

"No way!"

In an instant, twelve of them broke into a sprint, rushing across the open field in panic, trying to reach the spot where Atlas was about to land.

But before they could get far, they all froze. Some even stumble to the ground in shock.

Because out of thin air, someone appeared before them. A man in a perfectly tailored black suit, his dark hair neatly styled, his expression calm and composed.

"Edrik... Lord Atlas!" one of the soldiers gasped.

Edrik raised his hand slightly. "Do you really think the Lord would do something reckless enough to put himself in danger?"

The soldiers went silent, glancing at each other awkwardly.

"Uh... isn’t that exactly what he always does?" one of them muttered.

"Hey! Watch your mouth!" another snapped.

"No, I didn’t mean it like that!" The first soldier protested quickly.

They all tensed up again as Atlas drew dangerously close to the ground. Every single one of them held their breath, eyes wide in disbelief. At that speed, without any form of protection, the impact would be fatal.

Worse still, Atlas was falling headfirst. If he hit the ground like that, it would be over instantly.

A heartbeat later, he struck the earth. The sound was sharp and heavy, followed by a violent tremor that rippled through the grass. Everyone flinched, squinting to see what had happened.

None of them dared to move past Edrik. If Edrik believed everything would be fine, then they had no right to question it. Still, tension clung to the air like static.

As the cloud of dust began to settle, they could finally see Atlas in the distance, lying in the middle of a cracked patch of ground. The impact site was clearly visible around him.

No one approached yet. They didn’t need to. Each of them was connected to Atlas through the system, and the readings were clear.

"Lord Atlas is fine," one of them murmured in disbelief.

"But how...?" another whispered. "I didn’t see him use any skill. He just fell, like he was unconscious! How did he survive? Did he just... use a flying ability?"

"I don’t remember Lord Atlas ever having a flying ability," one of the soldiers said, still staring in disbelief at the distant figure.

Edrik began walking toward the impact site, calm and composed, the others following behind him with hesitant steps.

"Gale Veil," he said quietly.

"Gale Veil?" one of the soldiers echoed in surprise. "No way... was that really Gale Veil?"

[Skill unlocked: Gale Veil.]

[Elite - Gale Veil (Defensive / Passive Trigger)]

[Reduces fall damage and absorbs kinetic force. Can also weaken blunt attacks.]

Another soldier spoke up hesitantly, "Gale Veil should be a high-grade defensive skill, right? Usually, only those with strong affinity toward the Air Element can use it."

"Air Element?" someone else echoed in disbelief. "Since when does Lord Atlas have a high affinity with Air?"

The group fell silent for a moment, the only sound being the crunch of boots on grass. Then Edrik’s calm voice cut through the air, steady and unreadable, as he continued walking without turning around.

"Anyone can unlock something extraordinary when they’re pushed into a desperate situation. When there’s no other way out," he said quietly.

Suddenly, Edrik stopped in his tracks. The soldiers froze too, a chill running down their spines as he slowly turned to face them. His expression was cold, his eyes sharp as blades.

"One, two, three," he said evenly. "Three of you have an affinity with the Air Element." His gaze then fixed on one of the men. "You even have a high affinity."

The man, looking to be in his thirties, flinched and instinctively took a small step back.

"You want Gale Veil?" Edrik said

The soldier swallowed hard, his body trembling. "Y-yes, Edrik... do you have a skill scroll for Gale Veil?"

Edrik’s expression didn’t change. "Yes. The name of the skill scroll is ’Fall from extreme height without any protection.’ "

"Hy-Hyaa!" the man yelped, stumbling backward in shock, his face turning pale as he gulped again, wide-eyed.

Edrik’s face remained completely expressionless as he turned away from the soldiers and continued walking toward Atlas. From a distance, it was clear that Atlas was lying on the ground, motionless. Almost as if he had simply decided to take a nap there.

But there were no signs of injury. Not a single scratch. He just looked... deep in thought.

"Are we not brave enough to die?" one of the soldiers muttered quietly.

"Right," another replied. "We’ve been on countless missions that constantly pushed us to the edge of death. Those extreme, suicidal assignments."

"But maybe... that still isn’t enough," someone else said under his breath.

"So, what? You planning to try the same stunt Lord Atlas just pulled?"

The man who had been teased earlier swallowed hard and said nothing.

They all fell silent again as Edrik reached Atlas’s side. He moved with calm precision, lowering himself to one knee beside the lord.

Atlas still hadn’t moved, his expression distant.

They all tried to listen closely to what Edrik was saying.

"You’ve unlocked an Elite-grade skill in just one attempt, my lord," Edrik said calmly.

Atlas sat up, brushing the dirt from his shoulder and shaking his head a little.

"The wind was so loud it made my ears feel a bit numb," he said with a grin before turning toward the soldiers. "Hey, everyone. You’ve done well. Is everything alright?"

The soldiers, startled by his casual tone, quickly straightened and bowed in unison.

"Lord Atlas, we received the best training from this expedition, and our levels have increased significantly!" one of them said with visible pride.

"I’m glad to hear that," Atlas replied as he rose to his feet again.

He stretched his arms, rolling his shoulders and moving one arm to the side, then the other, before raising both above his head to loosen up.

Atlas began hopping lightly on his feet, shaking off the stiffness, while Edrik stood tall nearby, his usual composed presence unchanged.

"Ah, I unlocked the wrong skill," Atlas said casually.

"The wrong skill?" several of the soldiers repeated at once, completely stunned.

"Why wrong?" one of them asked, confused.

Atlas chuckled. "I was hoping to unlock an actual flying skill. But with Gale Veil activating automatically like that, it means I won’t be under pressure to unlock something better."

He then turned toward Edrik. "Do you think if I keep doing free-fall jumps over and over until I’m completely exhausted, it’ll help me unlock a flying ability, Edrik?"

The soldiers’ jaws dropped. They stared at Atlas, horrified by what they had just heard.

"My lord," Edrik said in his usual calm, measured tone, "I’m not sure whether you’re training to master the Air Element... or simply bored. But in theory, your current affinity with Air is already strong enough to let you glide or even hover briefly in the air."

The soldiers collectively exhaled in relief after hearing that.

So that meant Lord Atlas wasn’t really planning to jump from the sky again, right?

Right...?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 375 - Wrong Room, Right Morning
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Atlas was half-asleep, his eyelids heavy. It felt like the deepest, most peaceful sleep he’d had in weeks. But... something felt strange.

"It’s so cute... it looks so tender, I want to eat it. Uhm..."

A woman’s voice reached his ears. Soft, playful, and close.

Too close.

Atlas’s eyes snapped open, and he flinched slightly. Someone was lying right beside him.

A woman, her crimson hair falling across her face, her breathing calm and steady in sleep.

Atlas jolted upright, pushing the blanket off his body. He wasn’t wearing a shirt, but thankfully he still had his pants on.

"Sera?" he muttered in confusion.

What was she doing in his room?

Serenith was there, fast asleep beside him, wearing only a tight-fitting sleep shirt and short pants.

"I like the meat... it’s so big..." she mumbled in her sleep, her voice hazy and innocent as she shifted and stretched slightly.

"Sera... how did you get into my room?"

At that moment, Serenith stirred awake, blinking her eyes open. When she turned toward him and realized where she was, her face froze in shock.

"My lord!" she gasped, bolting upright and instinctively checking herself, as if to make sure she was still fully dressed.

"Why do you look so surprised?" Atlas asked flatly, his expression unreadable.

Serenith lifted her head, her face flushing. "My lord, you suddenly appeared in my bed! Why didn’t you say something first instead of sneaking in like this?"

Atlas sighed and pressed a finger against her forehead, pushing her back slightly.

"Are you drunk or what? This is my room," he said firmly.

But then... he paused.

He looked around the room properly for the first time. The walls were soft pink, the curtains sheer and frilly, and every piece of furniture was delicately decorated with ornaments and trinkets he’d never seen before.

"Wait..." Atlas said slowly, eyes narrowing. "Why is everything pink? I don’t recognize any of this."

He turned sharply toward Serenith.

"How the hell did I end up sleeping in your room?"

Serenith suddenly burst into laughter, lowering her head as she tried to hold it in. Then she lifted her face again, her expression bright with amusement as she lightly smacked Atlas’s knee.

"My lord, you sneaked into my room and now you’re pretending not to remember."

Then she paused, eyes widening slightly. "My lord, please tell me... nothing inappropriate happened last night, right?"

The realization of her own words hit her a second later, and her face turned crimson. She covered her mouth with both hands, gasping.

"No... My first time has been taken!"

Atlas sighed and pressed his finger to her forehead again. "I didn’t do anything. Stop making things up," he said flatly.

Truthfully, he didn’t remember how he’d ended up there. But he was pretty sure nothing had happened. At least, he hoped so.

Then came a sharp knock on the door.

"Yes..." Atlas answered automatically.

Why was he responding? This was Serenith’s room.

"My lord, it’s time to supply mana for the egg," came Edrik’s calm voice from outside.

How the hell did Edrik know he was here?

"Atlas isn’t here. Come back later," Atlas said quickly.

But before he could say anything else, the door opened.

Edrik stood there, perfectly composed in his usual crisp black suit, his face as unreadable as ever. He gave a short, respectful bow before speaking.

"Permission to enter," he said evenly.

"You just barged into Sera’s room. Where are your manners, Edrik?"

"No, my lord," Edrik replied calmly. "I merely needed to ensure that Sera was safe. Now that I see it’s you here instead... I can rest easy."

Shut up, Atlas thought grimly, his expression twitching.

Edrik walked in with his usual unreadable expression, his steps quiet and precise. Serenith immediately pulled the blanket up to her chest, covering herself even though she was still fully dressed.

"Stop acting like you’re not wearing anything," Atlas said dryly, sliding off the bed and sitting on its edge.

Edrik dragged a small table closer and placed a thick mat on top. From his dimensional storage, he retrieved the egg, which shimmered faintly as it appeared in the air before settling gently on the surface.

"The room doesn’t get much sunlight," Edrik said in his calm tone. "It’s easy to lose track of time when you’re resting here. Still, it’s already midday, my lord."

"Really? I didn’t notice," Atlas said, rubbing his eyes.

He must have been completely drained. After all, the day before, he’d spent hours jumping from extreme heights down to the floating island below. Over and over again.

How many times had it been? He’d lost count.

He had been pushing himself to the limit, hoping the exhaustion would trigger a breakthrough and help him unlock a powerful new skill. But in the end, nothing had happened.

"I hope your rest was at least satisfying," Edrik said in that same even tone.

Serenith’s cheeks flushed slightly.

She had been there yesterday, watching over Atlas while he trained recklessly.

Atlas frowned faintly, trying to recall what had happened after that. If he remembered correctly, he’d asked Edrik to help him back to his room once he could barely stand.

But... Atlas distinctly remembered entering his own room.

So why on earth had he woken up in Serenith’s bed?

Edrik bowed slightly, noticing the curious look on Atlas’s face. "Last night," he began calmly, "you requested to enter this room, my lord."

"So, you were the one who brought me here!"

"I only escorted you past this room," Edrik replied. "You were the one who stepped inside on your own."

"Oh!" Serenith exclaimed, her eyes lighting up. "So Lord Atlas did come in here by his own will?" she said, giggling softly.

Without warning, she darted forward and wrapped her arms around Atlas’s arm, pressing against him with a bright smile. "I’m so happy!"

Then she froze, suddenly realizing what she was doing. Her face turned red, and she gasped.

"So it’s true... My first time has been taken!"

Atlas sighed heavily and, for the third time that morning, poked her forehead with his finger.

"Nothing happened. Stop imagining things," he said.

He pulled his arm free and walked over to the table where the egg rested.

"How will we know when this thing is going to hatch?" he asked while placing his hand gently on its surface.

As he began channeling his mana into it, he felt how drained he still was. His body ached from the brutal training of the previous day, and his reserves were running low.

Still, he forced his mana to flow, and soon a faint current of energy streamed into the egg’s shell.

Sparks of light and thin arcs of electricity began to flicker across its surface as the energy connected.

"We’ll know the exact moment it happens," Edrik explained calmly. "It will crack on its own and reveal what’s inside."

Until... Atlas tilted his head in confusion. The egg had suddenly stopped responding to his mana.

"The egg," he said, glancing toward Edrik. "It’s not reacting to my mana anymore. Did I do something wrong?"

Edrik cleared his throat softly before answering. "The egg needs a mother, my lord."

"Mother?!" Atlas repeated. "What do you mean by mother?!"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 376 - Parenthood Denied
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Atlas took a deep breath and stepped back, sitting down on the edge of the bed again. Still trying to process how his morning had started with waking up in Serenith’s room and now included another one of Edrik’s "brilliant" ideas.

Before he could say anything more, Serenith had already stepped closer to the egg. She placed her hands gently on its surface and began channeling her mana into it.

"Oh! The egg is responding to my mana!" she said with a delighted giggle.

Atlas frowned, glancing toward Edrik, whose expression... as always, remained completely unreadable.

"Does that mean I’ve become the egg’s mother, Edrik?" Serenith asked cheerfully, her eyes sparkling as she looked back at Atlas. "My lord... does this make me your fourth wife?"

"Can you both stop with these ridiculous ideas," Atlas said flatly before turning his attention to Edrik. "Edrik. Explain."

Edrik bowed slightly before explaining, his tone steady and measured. "My lord, under normal circumstances, the egg would hatch on its own without any external interference. However, when someone supplies mana during this stage, it affects the nature of the creature that will emerge. Since Sera has a strong affinity with the same element as the beast within, the egg is responding directly to her mana."

Atlas tilted his head slightly. "And what did you mean by ’mother’ earlier?"

Edrik straightened, his tone calm and precise as ever. "The egg typically requires mana from two individuals of opposite genders. You can think of it as a symbolic balance.
