Like a father and mother for the creature. So, you may consider whatever hatches from this egg as your child with Sera, my lord."

"Yeay!" Serenith exclaimed instantly. "My first baby! I’m so happy!"

Atlas rubbed his forehead, sighing deeply at both Edrik’s overly logical explanation and Serenith’s dramatic reaction.

He took another breath and looked at the egg again. "Does it still need my mana, or is Sera’s enough for now?"

"Sera’s mana alone will be sufficient," Edrik replied calmly.

Atlas smiled faintly as he watched the egg glow softly in response to Serenith’s mana. The light pulsed with a steady rhythm, almost like a heartbeat, a clear sign that the creature within was reacting positively.

If the monster had been that strong before it was killed, then with the influence of both his and Serenith’s mana, the beast that would hatch from this egg could be something far greater.

Still... the thought of this being a "baby" between him and Serenith was just absurd.

No! He shook his head. He refused that idea entirely. This was simply a monster egg. One he and Serenith were helping to hatch, nothing more.

He smiled again at the thought, but the calm moment didn’t last.

Serenith was hugging the egg tightly. "My cute baby," she cooed.

"That’s not your baby!" Atlas said immediately.

"Agree," Edrik added in his usual flat tone, causing Serenith to glance at him with a puzzled look.

"I mean," Edrik continued without any hint of emotion, "babies normally come out directly from your stomach, Sera. So you can do it the correct way next time."

"Shut up, Edrik!"

Serenith stared at Edrik for a moment before bursting into laughter, giggling until tears welled in her eyes.

"Edrik really works hard to help the Lord collect more wives, huh?" she teased.

"You’re catching on quite well, Sera," Atlas replied dryly.

"But you skipped me and claimed Elyndra first." she sniffled dramatically, pretending to cry.

Atlas could only sigh and rub his forehead as Serenith continued to cry loudly in the room. Whether those tears were real or not, she was definitely making a scene. Actual tears running down her cheeks as she sniffled and whimpered dramatically.

Why did his mornings have to start with this kind of drama?

After a while, the emotional chaos finally calmed down. The so-called "moment" ended, and the three of them left the room together.

Soon they were seated in the usual dining area, surrounded by the familiar noise of morning activity. There were still thousands of people in the same hall, all busy preparing for the journey ahead.

They were en route to the next island, and the large-scale hunt would begin in just a few minutes.

Only four days remained before the expedition would come to an end, and they would soon return to their region. According to Luna’s latest report, their progress was nothing short of extraordinary.

Nearly 95% of the soldiers participating in the hunt had already reached level 150, and around 30% of them had even advanced to level 160. It was an incredible achievement by any standard.

In just two more days, every member of the force was expected to be at least level 150. This would grant a tremendous boost to Atlas and his entire alliance, greatly strengthening their position.

After all, Atlas knew that the enemies he would soon face would likely have armies just as powerful. Most of them probably above level 150 as well.

And who knew? There might even be some who had already crossed into level 200.

After finishing his breakfast that day, Atlas spent a short while training, running across the upper surface of the floating island. The wind there was fierce, constantly threatening to drag anyone careless enough to the island’s edge and throw them into the open sky below.

It was a wild and unstable place, even though its owner only possessed a B-rank system ability.

Still, training like this no longer offered any real challenge for Atlas. Running? That was nothing to him anymore. He could probably sprint for an entire week without rest and still be perfectly fine.

So, he pushed himself further. Jumping off the edge of the floating island straight toward the ground far below. Yet even that no longer felt dangerous.

Each time, he activated a passive skill that allowed him to hit the ground unharmed, making the fall feel more like a simple landing than a death-defying act.

Thankfully, he still had options. Far more demanding, far more brutal challenges that truly tested him. The kind that left scars on the mind rather than the body.

Damn it.

Now he stood once again on that wide, circular ritual floor. The same one inscribed with glowing magic patterns. The air around him was dim and heavy, the only light coming from the pale, distant moon above.

And there it was again.

The demon.

Its gaunt, skinny figure emerged from the darkness, its long, tangled hair obscuring most of its shriveled face. It looked every bit as starved and lifeless as before, its presence radiating hunger and malice in equal measure.

"Khi! Khi! Khi! Look, the coward has come back again!"

"Shut up and fight already!" Atlas snapped, cutting off the starving demon mid-laugh. His teeth clenched tightly as his fists began to glow with a brilliant golden light, illuminating the dark chamber around them.

This time, the result will be different!

The demon blurred forward, its movement impossibly fast.

What! How?!

Before Atlas could react, a light tap from the creature’s hand sent his body flying backward, crashing hard into the trees behind him. The impact shook the ground, splinters scattering everywhere.

He tried to rise, but before he could even lift his head, the demon was already standing right in front of him again.

"I’ll show you what it feels like," the demon hissed, "when your soul is ripped out of your body."

"Can’t you come up with a more creative line?!" Atlas shouted back.

Then came the pain. Searing, indescribable pain that tore through his entire being. It felt like his very soul was being dragged violently from within him.

"No! No way!" he gasped, his eyes wide in panic.

Am I... going to die here?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 377 - I Will Become That
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Atlas once again found himself drifting at the edge of his own consciousness, taking several deep breaths as he looked at the figure of Dullorak floating before him.

How many times had he tried this with the Void Core? If he counted correctly, this was the twelfth attempt. Yet every single time, it felt as if his very soul was being pulled apart, shredded into pieces he could barely hold together.

He knew, of course, that nothing fatal would happen to him. Dullorak and the others were there to make sure of that, always keeping him safe. But still... it was not enough.

Like... this time, Atlas was fighting something completely different. If before he had struggled to master his mind in countless training sessions, now he was facing something deeper. Now he was fighting to control his own soul.

It was an extraordinary kind of training. And surprisingly, astonishingly difficult.

A faint smile appeared on his lips, followed by a quiet chuckle.

Of course, he had never expected his training to become monotonous, had he? Only through challenges like this could he keep evolving, pushing himself toward new and greater heights.

"Damn it!" Atlas muttered under his breath.

He would do what he always did. Keep trying until he broke through this limit.

Once he was done with the Void Core, he moved on to the next part of his training. He would jump from the height toward the floating island below.

He had truly lost all fear of heights now, which was an incredible improvement. Atlas felt like he could finally breathe freely as he dove through the air, gliding and maneuvering with ease. He even began practicing his combat techniques by controlling the surrounding air element.

He could glide, slow his fall, and change direction at will. Atlas no longer needed any kind of gliding device.

Although, at this point, he was still only using the most basic form of air element manipulation and had not yet managed to trigger any new skill that might be even more impressive.

But well, as long as he enjoyed the process, he would keep doing it. Day after day, pushing himself further.

Then came the next morning, and the one after that, until finally, the day arrived when they were scheduled to return to their own islands.

Atlas stood before thousands of soldiers on the surface of the floating island, the wide grass field swaying gently in the soft morning breeze. His elite subordinates and several other lords stood by his side at that moment.

"Can’t we just go back to our own islands right away?" he asked, glancing toward Luna with a hint of hesitation in his voice.

"Atlas!" Ember was the one who responded immediately. "You’re the leader of this alliance. You have to give them your inspiring words!"

Atlas fell silent for a moment.

Ember added quickly. "We ended up clashing with Bolin because of your trash talk that night, remember? So show some spirit!"

Atlas gave her an awkward smile at that. "Your words sound more like you’re blaming me than cheering me up, Ember!" he said.

Ember just giggled in response, clearly amused.

Atlas took a deep breath, and his eyes sharpened with unwavering focus. He wore his full Nightfall Sentinel Armor Set. Then he summoned his Wavebreaker Spear, the legendary-grade weapon he had not used for quite some time.

With firm steps, Atlas walked forward until he could clearly see the thousands of soldiers lined up before him in perfect formation. The army before him was not made up of only his own subordinates, but also those who served under the other lords of the alliance.

Still, because they would soon fight as one united force... At this moment, they truly stood as one. Each of them would rely on the others to win their battles ahead.

Atlas paused for a moment, realizing that his speech today would be broadcast to every island of every lord, allowing even those who were not present to hear his words.

He gave a faint smile, knowing full well that Luna had gone to great lengths to place him in this position, huh?

Taking another slow breath, Atlas looked once more at the sea of people standing before him. He steadied himself, reaffirming everything within him before he spoke.

"I..." Atlas began, pausing for a moment as his voice echoed across the vast field, carried clearly through Kaeris’s control of the Air Element.

"I was falling into the deep ravine the moment I was chosen as a lord. No matter what I did or tried to do, I was already as good as dead at that moment, if the system hadn’t saved me and given me this chance."

He continued. "I started my journey as a lord on an island no bigger than half a football field. Just a stretch of desert with no resources at all."

Atlas tried to keep his words brief, yet he felt the urge to speak his heart completely.

"I trained! Harder than ever, as far as my mind and soul could endure!" he continued, his voice rising with each word.

"I completed my rank-1 lord battle in just two seasons, and by my third battle season, I had already reached level 167. Ready to face rank-3, even standing close to rank-4."

He paused again, taking a breath.

"I pushed myself, truly pushed! Not a single day passed without training, without forcing myself to go higher, to defeat someone stronger."

He fell silent once more. "For what reason?"

Atlas shook his head. "I have none."

He shook his head again, slower this time. "I simply gave myself entirely to this lord battle system."

The soldiers before him stirred, reacting faintly to his last words.

"I sold my soul to this lord battle system," he said firmly. "If the system demands a powerful lord, one who wins again and again..."

"I will become that!"

Atlas paused again, and this time he could feel every word burning inside him, ready to be spoken with his whole soul.

"If the system demands a lord who can lead, who can plan, or even be cunning. Someone who uses every bit of his strength to win..."

"I will become that!"

He said it firmly, a faint smile touching his lips.

"If the system demands a lord who can command vast armies, earn their respect, and be fully followed by his people..."

"I will become that!"

He said it again, his eyes growing sharper with determination.

"We will move forward and face something enormous," he continued. "A challenge that will never seem reasonable to anyone. Except to those who believe in themselves and are willing to give everything for this war."

He paused once more before continuing. "We have trained, prepared, and planned every single possible strategy, down to the smallest detail. We might look reckless, maybe even foolish."

A faint smile curved his lips. "But that’s good... it means we’ll have the chance to strike back at them, to shock them with what we’ll show at the finish line."

He swallowed hard and then raised his voice, his words thundering across the field.

"We will march forward! And we are ready to win!"

For a moment, the entire area fell silent. Then, all at once, the stillness shattered.

Everyone roared, lifting their weapons high as their voices thundered across the field. The sound was raw, filled with passion, echoing from the depths of their throats.

Atlas could feel it. The atmosphere, the sheer intensity of it all, pulsing through the air like a living force.

Yes! There was not a trace of doubt left in him. Atlas knew it with absolute certainty.

He would win this war!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 378 - Papa’s Home
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Atlas stood last, watching as hundreds of his soldiers entered the portal that would take them back to Gacha Haven. Only his elite subordinates remained behind him as he finally stepped into the teleportation portal himself.

In that brief moment between spaces, one thought crossed his mind. Who would he want to see first once he arrived on the island?

When he opened his eyes again, he could already smell the familiar air of Gacha Haven, feeling its warmth stirring both his senses and his soul.

A satisfied smile spread across his face. Yet almost immediately, he noticed something. His soldiers before him were stepping aside, opening a path for someone.

Atlas paused for a second, looking ahead.

Then his expression softened, and he broke into a wide smile, even letting out a small laugh. Someone was there waiting for him. No, not someone. A child.

A little girl with braided hair tied neatly behind her head, looking about five years old, wearing a fitted outfit with a light protective outer layer.

"Papa!" her voice called out brightly.

Atlas began walking forward as the girl ran toward him with all her strength. He quickened his pace, lowering himself just as she leapt into his arms. With a laugh full of relief, he lifted her high into the air.

"Papa! You’re back, Papa! I missed you so much! You were gone for so long, Papa... Papa..." she said, her voice trembling with pure joy.

"Vienne..." Atlas whispered her name, his voice trembling as tears welled up in his eyes.

He had never felt a joy this complete, this overwhelming, at seeing someone again.

He pulled the little girl into his arms and held her tightly against his chest.

"Papa, look at me! I’ve grown! I’m stronger now! Mama taught me how to fight with a sword..." her voice trailed off as Atlas closed his eyes, drowning in the warmth of a happiness so deep it left him breathless.

He hugged the girl even tighter, as if trying to merge her small heartbeat with his own. At that moment, Atlas could feel it... love. A love so pure and so powerful that it filled every part of his being. Only when holding his little girl, his Vienne, could he feel something this real.

"You’ve grown so much... you look like you’re already five," he said softly.

Then he set her down and knelt in front of her so that their eyes met at the same level.

"Papa! I want to show you how I fight with a sword! Uncle Baldric made me a really cool and strong armor! I can jump really high now! And I can’t wait to take down my first enemy with my sword!"

Atlas frowned slightly, his heart tightening. Morganna...

She’s only a little kid... he thought, tears silently filling his eyes as he looked at his daughter. His child who was already dreaming of battle.

Atlas nodded slowly, realizing that Vienne’s presence gave him an even stronger reason to keep fighting. His gaze hardened with quiet resolve.

He lifted the little girl into his left arm, and she wrapped her small arms around his neck, resting her head against his shoulder.

A moment later, Mira came running up to them, stopping right in front of Atlas.

"My lord... I missed you so much..." she said softly, hugging his right arm and brushing her cheek gently against it.

"Hi, Mira," Atlas replied with a warm smile. "You look like you’ve been doing really well."

They began walking together at a slow, steady pace.

With a bright, innocent tone, Mira started speaking. "My lord, I took care of the island really well! I regrew the plants, the carrots, the fruits, the flowers, the trees, and all the grass in every corner of the island! I made sure nothing was left untouched. Everything’s perfectly taken care of!"

Atlas smiled quietly at her cheerful report, but then he paused and turned his head toward her.

"Lyrassa? Has she woken up yet?"

Mira giggled softly. "Lyrassa is still asleep. It seems she’s enjoying her long rest this time."

It’s already been over a month, Atlas thought. But she should be fine. Maybe she just needed more time.

He wasn’t too worried. Even though Lyrassa was the main support of Gacha Haven, Elyndra could still step in and handle the role when needed.

After that, Atlas spent the next several hours doing a quick check with everyone in Gacha Haven. He reviewed the progress on the Soulbound Armor project, the maintenance of the spider spirit beasts, and the preparations for the next war.

He did all of this while still carrying Vienne in his arms. The little girl kept playing with his hair, occasionally tugging at it or, at times, nibbling at his ear playfully, making Atlas chuckle more than once despite his busy schedule.

By the time evening arrived, Atlas made his way to Morganna’s residence. He entered the grand hall where the vampire queen stood, holding a glass filled with a deep red liquid.

"Mama! Look! Papa’s back! Mama!" Vienne called out excitedly.

Morganna turned around at her daughter’s voice, her crimson eyes softening the moment they landed on Vienne. Of course... Atlas had already expected that reaction.

He watched quietly as Morganna stepped closer, her movements elegant and unhurried, until she stood face to face with him.

"Since when did your level reach two hundred?" Atlas murmured, noticing it only now.

Morganna smirked at his words. "So, you haven’t even checked Vienne’s level yet after being away from her for so long?"

Atlas blinked in surprise. She was right. He hadn’t checked Vienne’s level at all. Not even once since he returned. Until...

[Name: Morravienne]

[Level: 116]

[Strength: 378 | Agility: 433 | Intelligence: 168 | Constitution: 215 | Stamina: 353]

[Balanced Vitality (SS) - Predator’s Instinct (S) - Blood-Mana Conduit (A)]

"What?!" Atlas almost shouted in disbelief.

"Papa, look at my level! Cool, right? I’m level 116!" Vienne said proudly.

Vienne’s Agility was an incredible 433. That number was astonishingly high. With that kind of speed, she could already hold her own in battle. And her Strength...

Atlas swallowed hard as he read the numbers, his chest tightening with both awe and unease.

Morganna smirked again. "Don’t you dare compare her to your fragile, weak race."

Atlas shook his head slowly. Of course, it had to be the effect of the soul-link that connected him, Morganna, and Vienne. His own rapid level-ups during his recent hunts must have influenced Vienne’s growth as well.

At this rate, in just a few more months, Vienne could actually be ready to join them on the battlefield. But even the thought of seeing her fight, of seeing her bleed, twisted something deep inside him.

No matter how strong she became, Atlas couldn’t bring himself to accept the idea of his own daughter facing that kind of danger.

"Mama said I’ll join the war too, Papa! I’ve been training! Fighting with a sword and magic! I love running fast and using my sword fast!" Vienne said, her voice bubbling with joy.

Atlas’s heart ached. He hugged her tightly, holding her small body close against his chest. A soft smile touched his lips, followed by a quiet, tired laugh.

"Thank you... for taking care of her while I was gone."

Morganna’s expression didn’t change. "She is my daughter. You have no right to thank me for that."

Atlas fell silent, then reached out and gently took her hand.

In that moment, without another word, Morganna stepped closer until she leaned against him.

For now, Atlas let the quiet linger. Let this small, fragile peace fill the space between them. Just for a while. Before the real battle began.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 379 - The Convergence War Begins
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Thirteen lords of the Vanguard Dominion Alliance gathered inside the Noble Meeting Hall of Gacha Haven. Today, Atlas would officially issue the Dominion Convergence Challenge, a declaration of war they had planned since the very beginning.

Atlas double-checked the status of Bolin’s alliance to make sure they were still active. Yes, they were. A faint smile crossed his face. Bolin’s group had not yet begun any official campaign, which meant the timing was perfect.

He looked around at the lords gathered before him. Each one met his gaze with firm resolve. They had made this decision together, and they would face whatever outcome awaited them, together.

Bolin would take this challenge seriously. He had to. After all, Atlas had already dealt heavy damage to his alliance before. There was no way Bolin would back down now. And Atlas knew that Bolin would be ready to unleash his full power to crush him and the entire Vanguard Dominion if he could.

Atlas exhaled quietly. "Let’s do it," he said in a low voice.

He pressed the Challenge button. Immediately, a glowing system message appeared before him.

[Atlas, Leader of The Vanguard Dominion, has issued a Dominion Convergence Challenge against Bolin, Leader of The Celestial Sovereignty.]

He watched as the system processed the declaration, then another message appeared.

[The Celestial Sovereignty has accepted the Dominion Convergence Challenge issued by The Vanguard Dominion.]

[The Convergence War between The Vanguard Dominion and The Celestial Sovereignty has officially begun.]

[There are 63 days remaining in the campaign.]

[Victory will be determined by the total accumulated score achieved by each alliance during this period.]

[Should either side surrender before the campaign concludes, all territories, resources, and strategic assets owned by the defeated alliance will be automatically transferred to the victor.]

Atlas smiled faintly when the system confirmed that the challenge had been accepted almost immediately after his declaration.

[A Virtual Convergence Space has been constructed between The Vanguard Dominion and The Celestial Sovereignty to facilitate strategic discussions and battle coordination between both alliances.]

[You may now set up the Convergence Gate at any location of your choosing.]

[Inside this virtual space, all participating lords can communicate freely, but no physical interaction or combat is permitted.]

A soft flash of light appeared before Atlas, and he felt something materialize in his palm. A small coin engraved with the symbol of an open portal at its center.

He rose to his feet, turned slightly, and tossed the coin toward the far side of the room. The moment it hit the ground, the air shimmered and twisted, forming a tall gate with a glowing circular portal at its core.

Atlas gave a slow nod. Luna stepped forward first. Moments later, Edrik, Luna, and Ember followed behind him as they all stepped through the portal together.

**

Atlas found himself standing in a room that was... uhm... rather strange. It was an open space, a wide field covered with grass, almost like the surface of an island. Yet right in front of him stood a transparent barrier that, despite being see-through, was clearly visible, dividing two separate spaces between them.

He quickly noticed an old man with long white hair and a matching beard, wearing a white robe combined with light maroon armor. The man’s eyes were sharp, filled with the same annoyance that Atlas remembered from that night. The night Atlas had fallen from the sky and slaughtered his people.

Three figures stood beside the old man, Bolin.

Bolin walked forward until he was standing quite close to Atlas. It was easy to see that he was tall, even taller than Atlas himself.

"I thought you’d run away, making excuses. But it seems you’ve chosen to surrender your life instead," Bolin said firmly.

Atlas smirked. "I actually thought you would be the one running, looking for weaker opponents to face. But here you are, still standing. I have to admit, your courage is impressive. You really hope we’ll take your challenge for this battle."

"You’ll soon learn not to speak so recklessly in front of your superior, Atlas," Bolin replied coldly.

"Oh?" Atlas raised an eyebrow, pretending to look surprised, his jaw slightly dropped. "I only learned from the best. And of course, that means you, senior Bolin. So tell me, how do you want this battle to go? Should we take it slow, let my alliance crush every lord in yours one by one? Or shall we end this quickly, just me against you? It’ll save us both some time, and I’ll only need to take one head."

"Such arrogance," Bolin muttered. "I’d like to see how long that arrogance lasts. Fine, we’ll do it slowly. One by one. I’ll make sure to win every battle between us. And don’t you dare surrender before it’s all over."

"Good idea," Atlas replied calmly. "Just to make it clear, I won’t show any mercy in any of the battles we fight." He ended the opening exchange with a thin, challenging smile.

According to the agreement they had made, Atlas and Bolin immediately began preparing as a virtual table appeared between them. The table was divided into two sides, and on each side, there were five glowing points that could be filled.

They would start with the Defense and Attack categories, where both sides would assign five lords for each category. The two would then take turns attacking and defending based on their chosen formations.

Atlas followed the plan he had prepared from the very beginning, carefully setting the names of each lord he was sending into this battle.

[Defense Category:]

[1. Arveline SS-Rank (King’s Rebirth)]

[2. Thryssia SS-Rank (Law of Dominion)]

[3. Nysera SS-Rank (Eclipse Dominion)]

[4. Gwyndelle A-Rank (Mystic Crop Growth)]

[5. Variel A-Rank (Soul Ward)]

[Offense Category:]

[1. Atlas SSS-Rank (Ultimate Gacha)]

[2. Lumea SS-Rank (Spectral Legion)]

[3. Thaliera S-Rank (Heavenfall Pact)]

[4. Nysera SS-Rank (Eclipse Dominion)]

[5. Katriel S-Rank (Predator’s Memory)]

Atlas had made a quick adjustment to the lineup he had set earlier. In the Defense category, he replaced Luna with Gwyndelle, a change made after several careful considerations that he and his team had already discussed. The rest of the lineup remained the same as before.

The system’s ability ranks appeared only from Atlas’s perspective as the alliance leader. What both sides could see, however, were the names of each Lord and their individual ranks.

From Atlas’s alliance, all his lords were at rank 2. But what caught his attention was Bolin’s lineup. Bolin had placed all of his rank 2 lords in the Offense category, and then put two rank 3 lords in Defense. It was clear that Bolin was saving his strongest forces for an all-out confrontation in the final battle. Interesting.

"Where’s your rank 3? Only two of them? I thought you’d go all-out from the start to put up a real fight. I just hope your rank 2s won’t disappoint me, Senior Bolin," Atlas said casually.

Bolin began walking slowly, their eyes locked as they now stood directly face to face.

"Atlas," Bolin said, his tone low and sharp, "let me make this perfectly clear. I will destroy you, down to your very roots. I will kill everything you hold dear. Your people, your friends, your family, your alliance. I will wipe out every single one of them. Remember my words, Atlas. You’ve bitten the wrong dragon, and you’ll soon understand just how badly you’ve misjudged your place before me. You think your little tricks have weakened my alliance? You’re giving yourself far too much credit."

Atlas gave a faint smile. "Please, let’s begin. I’ll give you the honor of choosing which of your lords will be the first to lose their head."

Bolin’s eyes glinted sharply as he glared at Atlas. For a moment, they both stood still, as if ready to tear through the barrier between them and settle their grudge right then and there. Then Bolin smiled faintly and took a slow step back.

He turned toward the virtual table that hovered between them, reached out, and picked up one of the shining coins representing his chosen lord for the first battle. Without saying a word, Bolin flicked the coin toward the area where Atlas had already placed his defensive lineup.

[Bolin has chosen Zhen Liang to face Gwyndelle from the Vanguard Dominion Alliance in the defense position.]

Gwyndelle?

Atlas glanced toward Bolin. Whether by coincidence or intent, Bolin had chosen a lord with one of the lowest system ability ranks within Atlas’s alliance.

But... never underestimate the value of a system rank, especially when that system was specifically designed for defensive warfare.

Right after that, the virtual table shifted and expanded into a full 3D projection. Two islands materialized side by side, and two massive virtual screens appeared, each one facing Atlas and Bolin respectively.

The first battle was about to begin, with Gwyndelle standing as the defender against the enemy’s assault.

Atlas then turned around as the ground beneath them began to collapse rapidly, breaking apart like a massive moving puzzle.

In just seconds, only a small patch of land remained beneath their feet. Soon they realized that this small floating platform was suspended directly above the two islands, which were now facing each other and ready for combat.

[The battle will commence in 120 minutes.]

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 380 - Let Them Drown
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Two massive floating islands hovered below Atlas and the others. From above, it was clear that Gwyndelle’s island, the defending side, was positioned lower than the enemy’s.

From this height, it was difficult to see what exactly was happening on the surface below. Perhaps only the flashes of explosions would be visible. But huge floating screens would project every significant moment of the battle, highlighting the key events as they unfolded.

And at that very moment, the war began.

Atlas and Bolin stood far apart, both watching the floating screen to follow the unfolding clash. Every so often, they glanced at each other, ready to react should anything critical happen below.

Suddenly, several massive teleportation portals swirled open around the outer perimeter of the enemy’s floating island. One after another, waves of soldiers emerged. Dozens of them, marching forward in formation, fully prepared for an all-out assault.

Bolin smiled faintly, a sharp and cunning expression crossing his face as he glanced toward Atlas, as if the victory were already his.

Moments later, enormous beasts began to emerge from each portal, dozens of them. They looked like gigantic fire lions, their manes blazing with roaring flames.

A forest-covered island. How perfect for an elemental fire attack.

Bolin hadn’t just chosen an opponent with weaker potential; he had also picked the most effective kind of attackers to burn it to the ground.

The massive beasts opened their jaws, and enormous fireballs began to form inside their mouths. Their thunderous roars echoed through the air, reaching even Atlas’s height. Then, in an instant, the fireballs shot out in every direction toward the island below.

Explosions followed, unavoidable, deafening. The outer forests of Gwyndelle’s island burst into flames, the inferno spreading rapidly as pillars of fire consumed the once-green canopy.

At that moment, Bolin began to laugh. A restrained chuckle at first, but soon it broke free, echoing through the space.

"Look at them," he said with a grin. "Watch how everyone on that island will burn to ashes, just like the woods feeding the fire."

Atlas turned to face him, his expression tightening as his fists clenched by his sides.

Meanwhile, the barrage of massive fireballs continued to rain down, one after another, accompanied by deafening roars and thunderous explosions.

From their height, Atlas could clearly see the outer forests blazing wildly, thick smoke spiraling upward into the sky. At this point, the enemy troops had yet to move. They were simply controlling their beasts from afar, letting the flames do the work.

"Where are your troops, Atlas?" Bolin taunted. "Are they hiding? Soon, they’ll all be reduced to ashes. And the best part... I’ll let you watch it happen with your own eyes."

More explosions followed, the fire spreading rapidly through the dense forest. The thick canopy acted like fuel, helping the blaze devour everything in its path.

Then, the enemy army began to move. They marched forward, their armor glowing as if wrapped in living fire. The lord Bolin had chosen for this battle possessed an ability that granted his soldiers extra protection within fiery terrain. The flames didn’t harm them, they thrived in it.

Hundreds of enemy soldiers, roughly six hundred by Atlas’s estimate - advanced from every direction, charging straight through the blazing inferno. Behind them, the massive beasts kept launching fireballs farther ahead, deep into the forest.

If this continued unchecked, the entire island would soon be engulfed, turning into something that resembled a volcanic wasteland. A burning ruin like the Shattered Ember.

It all happened in an instant. The enemy forces had fully advanced onto Gwyndelle’s floating island, ready to begin the true battle.

Atlas lifted his gaze toward Bolin, who was barely holding back a laugh, clearly enjoying the sight of his opponent’s island being devoured by the flames. From the massive floating screen, Atlas could see the inferno growing even larger, the smoke rising so high that it reached the altitude where he stood, despite the distance.

And then... something unexpected happened.

Everyone’s eyes widened as a massive domain suddenly formed around the island, expanding at a breathtaking speed. In mere seconds, it sealed off the entire battlefield, trapping the raging fire, Bolin’s army, and all of their beasts within a colossal shimmering barrier.

Bolin’s expression froze in disbelief. He stared at the island, his eyes wide as he realized what had just occurred. The enormous island was now completely encased, and even the smoke from the massive blaze had vanished inside the dome.

On the giant screen in front of Atlas, the scene became clear.

A figure hovered high above the island, an elegant woman with long, flowing blonde hair, dressed in a sharp executive-style outfit, a delicate crown resting on her head. Her arms were raised, commanding the power surrounding her.

It was Queen Selvia... or rather, the head of Queen Selvia - who possessed the ability to summon an immense water domain that could cover an entire island. One of the many heads controlled by Dullorak.

Below her, several groups of mages were seen gathering, channeling their power together. At the center stood a woman whose hair flowed like water itself, her armor gleaming, her eyes a deep, radiant blue.

Atlas had deliberately stationed several members of the Hydros team there, alongside Selvara and Dullorak, all operating under Gwyndelle’s command.

Atlas lifted his head, his eyes sharp and focused, a faint smirk curling on his lips as he looked toward Bolin - whose expression, no matter how hard he tried to hide it, was filled with shock.

"Using fire against a forest is truly genius, Bolin," Atlas said calmly.

He didn’t bother to explain further. The strategy was simple, almost too simple. Prepare a massive army with high water-affinity spellcasters to counter and protect against any fire-based assault.

At that very moment, the results became clear. Torrential rain began to pour down across the island, and massive water storm tornadoes formed in multiple regions. They spun violently, devouring the raging inferno, pulling the blazing flames into themselves, and extinguishing the fire with astonishing speed.

Now... It was time for Gwyndelle to reveal the new forces they had nurtured. Atlas watched closely, anticipation flickering in his eyes. He wanted to see for himself how the Rank-A Item: Guardian Seed Cuboid, the artifact he had entrusted to the girl, would act - and what kind of power it would unleash next.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 381 - Gwyndelle’s Garden of War
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Atlas stood silent for a moment, watching the sight of Dullorak hovering high above the island, orchestrating the chaos that unfolded below. The rain poured down in heavy torrents from the sky. Not just rain, but waves of water twisting into violent tornadoes that swept across the battlefield, swallowing the fires and explosions in their path.

Floods of water, as if drawn from the depths of the ocean itself, surged downward, crashing mercilessly against the enemy troops scattered across the island. The soldiers struggled to hold their ground as the powerful currents drenched and battered them from every direction.

Dullorak now fought using only one of his heads - the only one capable of fully harnessing the element of water. Because of that, he poured every ounce of his power into this single element, unleashing its full destructive force.

Yet from Atlas’s perspective, this decision carried its own risks. Yes, it greatly increased Gwyndelle’s chances of victory in this defensive battle. However, it also meant that all of Atlas’s elite subordinates and forces participating in this event were temporarily bound under Gwyndelle’s command.

It was a strategic gamble, one that reduced Atlas’s personal strength for the sake of the alliance’s collective power. But Atlas wasn’t aiming to win just his own island. He intended to secure victory for every single lord within his alliance.

Even more remarkable was Luna, whom Atlas had deliberately excluded from the main lineup. He had other plans for her. Her unique system ability allowed her to empower any lords and their elite subordinates across every battle in the event.

Here, Luna would become the hidden force moving from the shadows. A silent presence that would grant immense strength to every engagement they fought.

Atlas had a very clear target in mind.

He stared intently at Bolin as the enemy forces below were swallowed by the roaring storm and the massive tornadoes tearing through the island.

Atlas would win every single battle in this event, no matter what it took. And he was absolutely determined to make it happen!

From the massive screens, the scene shifted rapidly, showing the chaos unfolding below. The enemy troops were being swallowed by torrents of water and raging tornadoes, their bodies hurled against the massive trees surrounding them.

At that very moment, the defensive plants, the Guardian Plants, began to reveal their presence across the battlefield. Enormous vines burst from the forest floor, whipping through the air and coiling tightly around enemy soldiers, dragging them away from their comrades.

Elsewhere, hundreds - no, thousands, of explosive seeds detonated, releasing a thick poisonous mist that spread across the area. The enemies caught within it staggered and faltered, their senses dulled and disoriented.

Then came the strike of the massive trunks, sharp and fast as spears, piercing through the soldiers’ armor and impaling their hearts with brutal precision.

In the midst of the chaos, Gwyndelle’s troops finally made their move. They were warriors perfectly attuned to the forest, blending seamlessly with the trees, their camouflage flawless. They moved swiftly between the shadows, emerging just long enough to drive their spears through the enemy before vanishing again into the dense foliage.

What unfolded on the screen was nothing short of a massacre. The army that had once held the advantage in a fire-based environment was now being annihilated in a forest strengthened by water and nature’s fury.

In only a matter of moments, the enemy’s casualties had already climbed into the hundreds.

Atlas turned his gaze toward Bolin, whose face had grown tense as he stared at the screen. His expression flickered between barely contained rage and a burning determination - as if he was already planning how to utterly destroy Atlas in the battles to come.

Was it frustration from watching his seemingly perfect strategy crumble before him? Or perhaps his plan had been far too predictable from the start?

Because now, it was undeniable - the Gwyndelle team had achieved a flawless victory in this first battle, not a single casualty among their ranks.

Atlas and the others watched in silence as the images on the screen showed the enemy troops being mercilessly crushed, their numbers dwindling at alarming speed. The slaughter continued for several minutes, and the surviving forces found themselves trapped, unable to escape the confines of the massive dome that sealed the island.

Would Bolin’s forces surrender to stop the bloodshed?

No. They fought on stubbornly until, at last, the battle came to its inevitable end.

[Victory for Gwyndelle!]

[Gwyndelle has successfully defended her island, annihilating over 70% of the enemy forces and executing the opposing Offense Lord.]

[A decisive triumph for the Vanguard Dominion Alliance.]

[Reward: +25 Rank Points granted to the alliance.]

[Initial Battle Score: Recorded and updated.]

The system message appeared clearly across the screen and in the vision of everyone standing there.

Atlas watched as the enemy’s floating island began to collapse rapidly from all sides, disintegrating into fragments of light. At the same time, Gwyndelle’s island expanded like a shifting puzzle of land pieces forming from the void, slowly growing even larger than before.

Bolin turned his gaze back toward Atlas, a thin smile curling on his lips.

"I see you went all out, even in the first battle, huh?" he said. "But I doubt you actually think you can keep this up forever, do you? I have more than twice the number of lords you do, Atlas!"

Bolin then reached toward the virtual table and picked up another shining coin representing his chosen lord for the next battle. Without a word, he flicked it toward the area where Atlas had already placed his defensive lineup.

[Bolin has chosen Xun Qiang to face Variel from the Vanguard Dominion Alliance in the defense position.]

Bolin smirked once more, clearly confident in his choice.

But Atlas only mirrored that expression, a faint smirk of his own forming as he saw Bolin’s selection. Once again, Bolin had chosen a target with one of the lowest system ability ranks within Atlas’s lineup.

Variel’s system ability was Soul Ward. When her subordinates fell, fragments of their spirits briefly manifested as ethereal guardians, shielding nearby allies before fading away.

It was a remarkably strong defensive ability. But it also revealed the harsh reality behind it. For Variel’s power to truly awaken, her soldiers had to fall first.

The question now was... how would Variel withstand Bolin’s next assault?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 382 - Death from Behind the Throne
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The surface of Variel’s floating island soon appeared on the massive screen. Among all the islands Atlas had seen so far, this one looked perhaps the most ordinary of them all.

There was nothing particularly striking about its design. Most of its structures were made of polished white metal, shaped like domes of varying sizes, though the differences between them were barely noticeable.

Everything essential to the island’s function seemed to be spread across separate facilities scattered throughout the land. Some for farming, others for livestock.

The island itself was mostly dry, covered in stretches of yellowish savannah with only a few sparse patches of trees here and there. It was, by all means, a landscape far from ideal for long-term defense.

If not for Variel’s remarkable defensive capability, she might have been eliminated from the Lord Battle system long ago.

On the screen, Variel and her troops stood stationed along the island’s perimeter, clad in armor and armed with their respective weapons. Massive catapults and machine cannons were positioned across several points, manned by her soldiers who were ready, yet visibly ordinary in comparison to the overwhelming forces seen in the previous battle.

Bolin, watching the same view, turned toward Atlas and smirked. His expression clearly said what he didn’t bother to voice... This poor island looks pitiful, it might as well crumble from the sky right now.

From the enemy’s side, even minutes before the battle officially began, the sky was already filled with movement. Dozens, no, hundreds - of flying beasts soared into the air. They had the bodies of lions and the massive wings of eagles, cutting through the clouds with terrifying speed.

On each beast’s back rode a warrior armed with long-range weapons, ready to strike. Their shouts and laughter echoed across the sky as they swarmed above the floating island like a storm of predators, circling and waiting for the signal to attack.

Variel... if Atlas had to be honest, she possessed the weakest aerial defense among all his lords. She did have units capable of fighting in the air, but their numbers were painfully limited. Even worse, this particular enemy appeared to command flying beasts in overwhelming quantities. An army specialized for domination from above.

"Ha!" Bolin finally let out a mocking laugh, his tone dripping with ridicule. "That little, pathetic island - look at it! Atlas, tell me you’re not serious. You really brought a lord this weak into a battle of aerial supremacy? What did you expect? That this poor thing could win against my sky legion? Hah!"

Atlas didn’t respond. He only stared at Bolin in silence, his expression unreadable.

Because he knew Bolin wasn’t wrong. Variel was strong, incredibly strong in defense. But against an assault from the skies of this scale, even her ethereal guardians, born from the spirits of her fallen soldiers, might not be enough to turn the tide.

The battle began in an instant. As everyone expected, a relentless rain of arrows and magic descended from the sky, drenching the entire island in destruction.

Explosions erupted one after another, shaking the ground as the enemy’s attacks poured down without pause. Variel’s troops scattered in chaos, trying desperately to defend themselves. Their frontline tankers cast protective buffs to hold the line, but the sheer intensity of the bombardment left them overwhelmed. It was an unending storm of fire and steel.

Bolin looked absolutely satisfied with what he saw. He turned toward Atlas, his lips curling into a restrained, mocking laugh, clearly reveling in how perfectly his army countered the formations of Atlas’s alliance lords.

"How will you withstand an assault of this scale?" Bolin taunted, his chin tilted slightly upward, his voice dripping with arrogance.

Then, the situation grew even worse. More shadows appeared in the sky, larger, darker, heavier. Massive drakes with wings as wide as the horizon burst through the clouds, their wings beating with terrifying power.

From their jaws erupted blazing torrents of fire that scorched the ground below, sweeping through Variel’s ranks. Her troops scattered and fell into disarray, desperately trying to avoid the inferno that now consumed the battlefield.

And then it became clear - riding one of those colossal drakes was the enemy’s commanding lord, leading the charge himself from above.

From the massive screen, the image zoomed in on a young man with short blond hair, standing calmly atop the back of his drake. He wore a casual, almost modern outfit beneath his light armor, his expression composed as he commanded his army to burn, crush, and destroy everything below.

All eyes were on the screen, including Bolin’s, who looked proud of the powerful lord he had brought into battle.

But then, amid the chaos, the roars, and the explosions. The blond lord suddenly froze. His eyes widened in shock.

Bolin stiffened, his confident smirk fading into confusion. "What... what is happening?"

The young lord glanced down at his chest. Something sharp was jutting out from the left side of it. His hands trembled; his face turned pale.

A faint, burning figure appeared beside him. A silhouette wreathed in embers, its body skeletal and its right arm still buried through the lord’s chest from behind.

Gasps filled the air. Bolin’s eyes went wide, his jaw dropping open in disbelief as he stared at the screen.

Atlas, meanwhile, could barely hide the faint smile tugging at his lips. Of course... he had planned this.

He had sent Everburn, the phantom assassin whose ability rendered him completely invisible and adaptable to any battlefield, to assist Variel in this fight. And now, that hidden card had struck with devastating precision.

Everburn had only been waiting for the right moment - for the enemy lord to reveal himself. Then, with silent precision, he struck. No one saw him move. He slipped past the guards unnoticed, his blazing skeletal form hidden until the very last instant, and drove his burning arm straight through the lord’s chest.

Oh... such an elegantly simple yet devastating strategy.

If Variel’s forces could not fight effectively against an army that ruled the skies, then there was no need to. Just eliminate the enemy’s lord, and the entire battle would end in an instant.

Moments later, the drake beneath its dying master began to lose control. With a guttural roar, the creature plummeted toward the ground, crashing violently into the island below. The blond-haired lord’s body was flung through the air, rolling hard across the scorched earth until he came to a halt.

At that exact moment, Variel stood there, her expression calm and resolute, her right foot pressing down on the chest of the fallen lord whose strength had already faded away.

[Variel has successfully assassinated the opposing Lord while sustaining only 13% damage to her island.]

[As a result, she is entitled to claim 100% of the assets, resources, and remaining forces belonging to the defeated Lord.]

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 383 - Eclipse Dominion
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

There was a break of several days before the next battle would take place. Not because of any delay from the system, but rather... perhaps because Bolin needed time to recover from the crushing defeats he had suffered in the first two battles.

Whatever the reason, Atlas used this quiet period to return to his usual routines. Ensuring Serenith continued supplying mana to the egg, and working with the Void Core, though he still hadn’t made much progress on that front.

He also spent most of his time with Vienne, making her his main focus during the break.

There was still no word from Lyrassa. She remained sealed within her cocoon, and no one knew whether she would stay inside for the entire duration of the Dominion Convergence. It had already been over a month, nearly two.

Still, Atlas had achieved two monumental victories. Both Gwyndelle and Variel had grown significantly stronger as a result. They gained vast resources from their wins, along with the enemy troops and assets now placed under their control.

Though neither of them would be assigned to another battle again in this event. The rewards they had secured would serve as an extraordinary advantage for the future.

Even if their system abilities were only classified as A-Rank, the sheer volume of resources and forces they now commanded would more than make up for that limitation. Turning them into invaluable assets for Atlas’s growing dominion.

Another battle was about to begin. Atlas once again found himself in the same floating patch of land, with Bolin already there, standing tall with a confidence he had clearly managed to rebuild during the break.

Bolin stepped forward, his chin lifted slightly, arrogance once again etched across his face.

"Don’t get too proud of your first two victories," he said with a sneer. "You know we still have plenty of battles ahead. And you know what... did you really think I wouldn’t notice that you’ve been lending your forces to other lords?"

The old man smirked, suppressing a laugh that trembled on his lips. "You’ve made a fatal mistake, Atlas. You’ll suffer a massive loss because of your arrogance! You’ve underestimated this entire war!"

As he finished speaking, Bolin flicked another shining coin across the virtual table, signaling the start of the next match.

This time, it would be Nysera’s turn to defend.

Nysera possessed the ability known as Eclipse Dominion . She could envelop the entire battlefield in darkness, weakening the energy flow of her enemies while strengthening all forces attuned to shadow or night.

Her system ability was SS-Rank. A rarity of overwhelming power.

This would be a completely new level of battle. Atlas knew just how formidable Nysera could be, not only in defense but in full-scale warfare. She had the power to control the entire environment - to reshape the battlefield itself into a massive domain of her own creation.

**

Just as the next battle was about to begin, Atlas could clearly see the image of Nysera’s island appear on the massive screen. It was... dark. So dark that it was difficult to describe properly.

The island was covered in trees that seemed to have been consumed by dark energy for far too long. Even though it was broad daylight, the entire island radiated an eerie shadowed aura that swallowed the light around it.

The structures scattered across the island were complex. Towers, metallic fortifications, and domed halls forged from dark steel. The architecture looked sturdy and efficient, yet it carried a heavy, foreboding atmosphere, every surface marked by shades of black and deep violet.

On the screen, Nysera herself came into view, standing at the forefront of her army. Behind her, hundreds of soldiers waited in disciplined formation, their armor glinting faintly under the dim glow that lingered across the island.

She was... the Shadow-Elf Twinblade Assassin.

She was an elf, but of a rare subrace. Her kind bore the unmistakable traits of the Dark Element: pale gray skin with a faint bluish tint, sharp narrow eyes that glimmered with a cold focus, and long, pointed ears. Her hair, jet-black and tied in a single high ponytail, swayed lightly in the air.

She wore a compact assassin’s robe of dark charcoal fabric with a high collar, tailored for mobility and precision. In her hands, she held two short, curved black-steel blades.

The battle began!

From every direction, swirling portals burst open around Nysera’s island. Some high in the sky, others forming across the ground. From within them emerged wave after wave of enemy troops, many mounted on enormous beasts that resembled armored horses. Some galloped through the air while others charged swiftly across the terrain below.

"Oh, they really do have a lot of lords capable of commanding beasts in such massive numbers," Atlas murmured under his breath.

Edrik, standing just behind him, nodded in agreement. "It seems they’ve mastered resource and force distribution quite efficiently, my lord."

"I can see that," Atlas replied with a faint smirk. "And I think we should learn to distribute our strength just as effectively. There’s a lot to take from this."

The battlefield erupted in chaos. Roars of beasts and the shouts of soldiers echoed across the sky. Loud enough to be heard even from where Atlas and the others stood above.

But then... a sudden, thunderous impact shook the air.

Bolin’s eyes widened. "What was that?" he muttered.

A massive domain had just formed. Swiftly expanding and enveloping the entire island. Within seconds, it had trapped both Nysera’s defending forces and the attacking army inside a colossal dome.

Darkness consumed everything. The interior was so completely shrouded in shadow that even the massive screen above could no longer display what was happening inside.

Bolin leaned over the edge of the floating platform, trying to catch a glimpse of the battle below. But no matter how hard he strained to see, the darkness offered no hint of what was taking place.

Only those within the island. Only those inside Nysera’s Eclipse Dominion knew the truth of what was unfolding in that pitch-black abyss!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 384 - Dominion Declared
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The battle raged in complete darkness. Yes, literally in total, consuming blackness.

Bolin looked visibly uneasy; it was clear that he had no data on Nysera or her abilities, meaning his forces hadn’t prepared any countermeasures. And of course, fighting against a Dark Element user was never easy. Without a strong number of Light Element users, there was almost no way to properly fight back.

The clash didn’t last long. In just a few minutes, the outcome appeared before them.

[Fengrath has declared surrender after suffering 38% total troop losses.]

[Nysera has successfully defended her island, sustaining 45% structural and force damage.]

Atlas fell silent as the system message appeared before his eyes. He kept his expression steady, refusing to let any reaction show.

The result wasn’t as good as he had hoped.

Nysera had indeed proven her incredible defensive power. But the damage she had taken was significant, nearly half her island’s strength. Even worse, the enemy lord had managed to escape after declaring surrender, robbing them of a full victory.

They only claimed a portion of the opponent’s resources and rank points, though it would be enough to help Nysera and her island recover in time.

Still, one thing was certain from this battle. Even though Nysera possessed an SS-Rank system ability, it was clear that the enemy she had faced this time also held tremendous potential. Strong enough to inflict such heavy damage despite being engulfed in her domain of eternal night.

**

Another battle!

The next confrontation started almost immediately after the last one. But Atlas remained focused, carefully observing and ensuring that every plan and strategy they had prepared would unfold flawlessly.

No mistakes were allowed. Not even one.

Now it was Thryssia’s turn.

The girl stood tall on a high cliff, clad in heavy armor. Her short black hair framed a determined face, and in her hands, she held a massive war hammer that looked powerful enough to crush through a solid stronghold.

Around her stretched fields of lush green grass, and at the center of her floating island lay a vast, serene lake that mirrored the sky.

Tessa possessed the ability Law of Dominion, classified as SS-Rank. Yet Atlas was convinced, deep down, that this ability was far beyond that. Maybe closer to SSS-Rank in potential.

She could literally declare binding laws within the battlefield itself. Each law she invoked consumed part of her personal essence or stored experience, but if used strategically and with precision... she could render an entire enemy army completely powerless.

The battle began, and almost immediately, dozens. No, hundreds of swirling portals appeared. Not just around the outer edge of the island, but also high above in the sky.

Atlas tilted his head slightly, curious to see what would emerge from them. Then, in a flash, they came.

From the portals burst waves of troops equipped with advanced mechanical gear. Sleek exoskeletal support systems that integrated seamlessly with their armor. The ground units marched out in formation, their every movement reinforced by glowing mechanical joints and mana-powered servos.

Meanwhile, the air units descended using propeller gliders, allowing them to maneuver freely through the sky at impressive speeds.

Most of the enemy soldiers carried mana cannons or rifle-type weapons, their weapons humming with compressed energy ready to fire.

The enemy lord clearly possessed a system ability that enhanced technological synchronization, amplifying the precision, efficiency, and coordination of mechanical-based units. In essence, their entire army functioned as a single unified machine.

Bolin let out a restrained laugh, turning toward Atlas with a smug grin. "Now, you’ll see just how overwhelming true numbers can be, Atlas!"

Atlas didn’t flinch. His gaze stayed firm, calmly assessing the sheer size of the opposing force. At least a thousand fully equipped combatants, all of them elite and ready to fight.

The enemy wasted no time. The moment they landed, their formation tightened, and with roaring engines and synchronized shouts, they charged forward in a massive assault straight toward the center of the island, toward Tessa’s waiting army.

And then, Atlas raised both hands slowly, drawing Bolin’s attention back to him.

He stretched lazily to the right, then to the left, his expression utterly unbothered.

"Boring," he said.

Bolin froze, his expression twisting in disbelief at Atlas’s single word. Because right after Atlas said it... Something happened.

The enemy’s flying troops suddenly began to lose control. Their formations broke apart as one after another plummeted from the sky. The propeller gliders spun wildly before crashing, and the ground forces stumbled, their movements faltering.

The mechanical suits and mana-powered devices that had looked so advanced just moments ago... all went silent. Their systems completely shut down.

Atlas glanced at Bolin and smirked, a glint of amusement in his eyes.

Bolin hadn’t learned a thing.

On that night, Atlas had brought Tessa into his ranks, and with her ability, she had rendered Bolin’s army completely helpless, while Atlas attacked from above.

Maybe Bolin had misunderstood what Tessa’s Law of Dominion truly meant, thinking it only related to limiting the level of enemy troops. That was his mistake.

So, this time, he had sent a lord commanding an enormous army. A thousand soldiers, but with average levels and heavily reliant on mechanical systems.

And once again, he was wrong.

Because Tessa’s law didn’t just affect levels. It dictated rules .

With the enemy’s main forces now crippled, Tessa’s army surged forward.

The once mighty mechanical army was now little more than helpless targets as the battle erupted below in a storm of power and steel.

Another easy victory?

Atlas watched the battle unfold carefully. Even without their mechanical support systems, the enemy troops were still combatants, trained fighters who refused to surrender easily. They continued to fight fiercely, forcing Tessa’s forces to stay alert and focused.

The battlefield raged under Tessa’s control, but even so, destruction was unavoidable. Atlas could see death and chaos spreading across both sides as fire and steel clashed again and again. The sheer number of enemy soldiers still posed a challenge for Tessa, making it difficult to end the conflict quickly.

Eventually, however, the enemy lord raised the signal of surrender.

[Enemy Lord has surrendered after losing 32% of their total forces.]

Bolin’s face twisted in frustration. His hand clenched tightly, and for a brief moment, it looked like he was about to slam his fist into the virtual table between them.

Grinding his teeth, Bolin finally threw the last shining coin toward Atlas.

This would mark the next battle.

And this time, it was Arveline’s turn, with her SS-Rank ability: King’s Rebirth.

Atlas’s expression shifted slightly, part anticipation, part excitement. He had been waiting for this one.

He truly wanted to see how Arveline would fight her battle. Because he knew something about her that made her army unlike any other.

Her soldiers didn’t fear death!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 385 - Resurrection Is Her Weapon
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

On the massive screen, a woman came into view. Someone who had long been the center of attention within Atlas’s alliance. According to the statistics and internal rankings, in terms of offense, defense, and strategic capability, she stood directly below Atlas himself.

She was Arveline, a lord blessed with an SS-Rank system ability that Atlas personally considered nothing short of extraordinary. Borderline insane in its potential.

Her ability, King’s Rebirth, allowed her to resurrect after death once the battle ended. In other words, even if she fell during combat, she would return once the war concluded.

Just imagine how absurd that was.

Arveline was, for all intents and purposes, a lord who could never truly lose. At least not permanently.

Of course, the ability came with its own complex conditions. It required her forces to have defeated a certain number of enemies across recent battles, as well as a balance between her troops slain and those she had slain herself. Meeting these requirements wasn’t simple. But when she did, the results were unstoppable.

Still, it would be an insult to credit her success solely to her system ability. Behind it, Arveline was known as a disciplined, tactical, and highly intelligent leader. Someone who commanded with both strength and purpose.

On the screen, she stood tall and radiant. Her long, red hair flowed behind her like fire in the wind. She wore light white armor trimmed with gold, a crimson cape billowing dramatically at her back. Her expression was bold, unwavering, and confident, and in her hands she held a long, elegant spear of white-gold metal. An emblem of authority and power that matched her very name.

Atlas stood still, his eyes fixed sharply on the screen as the next battle began.

"The likelihood of her army being resurrected increases depending on how much they’ve contributed to her directly," Edrik said quietly from behind him.

Atlas nodded. "Yes. Not only can she resurrect herself, but her troops as well. Though the success rate isn’t absolute. Still, that’s what makes Arveline and her army so fearless. They fight without hesitation, without fear of death."

The enemy force attacking Arveline’s island was massive. Bolin clearly wasn’t holding back.

But Atlas had made thorough preparations. Over the past month, he had carefully increased the levels and combat readiness of most of his alliance’s armies. And now, this was the result.

After a brutal and decisive confrontation, the final outcome appeared before them.

Arveline had achieved a complete victory. Her forces had utterly destroyed the enemy’s army, and she herself had slain the opposing lord. It marked the end of the Defense Category, with Atlas’s alliance sweeping every single battle.

Two of Bolin’s lords had surrendered and escaped, avoiding execution, but it changed nothing. The result was absolute.

[The Vanguard Dominion Alliance has achieved an overwhelming victory with an almost perfect score.

Allied forces have successfully defended every battle.]

[Reward Unlocked: Access granted to the Strategic Phase, allowing reallocation of resources and forces among allied lords as needed.]

Atlas stood there, watching with a faint smirk as his body was slowly drawn into the swirling portal before vanishing completely from sight.

He didn’t say a word.

Let the old man stew in his own defeat.

**

Atlas found himself once again among his fellow lords. Even though they had secured victory in their first set of battles, the war was far from over. And now, a few complications were beginning to surface.

"Nysera needs time to recover," Luna reported calmly. "Possibly one to two weeks."

Atlas nodded and opened the tactical display before him, reviewing the lineup for the next category. The Offense Category, where Nysera had originally been scheduled to participate.

"There are two Rank-3 lords in our next battles," Atlas said after a moment of thought. "I can take one of them myself, and perhaps we can assign the other to Lumea." He paused, then continued, "If Nysera isn’t ready in time, our only available options will be our S-Rank lords."

"I can take that position, Atlas," Luna replied softly but firmly.

Though her system ability was only S-Rank, Luna still held a high standing within the alliance. Her strength came not only from direct combat but also from her exceptional capacity to empower and support other lords, even without being directly in the battle.

It was a difficult decision. Atlas didn’t want to risk weakening their offensive force, especially with the All-Out Confrontation Category still waiting at the end. His ideal plan was clear, aside from himself, every lord participating in that final showdown should possess at least an SS-Rank system ability.

Yet the situation was tightening, and Atlas knew that every choice he made now would shape the outcome of what was still to come.

After carefully reworking the strategy and weighing every possible scenario, Atlas finally came up with the final lineup.

[Offense Category:]

[1. Atlas SSS-Rank (Ultimate Gacha)]

[2. Lumea SS-Rank (Spectral Legion)]

[3. Thaliera S-Rank (Heavenfall Pact)]

[4. Luna S-Rank (Celestial Vanguard Transformation)]

[5. Katriel S-Rank (Predator’s Memory)]

[All-Out Confrontation Category:]

[1. Atlas SSS-Rank (Ultimate Gacha)]

[2. Arveline SS-Rank (King’s Rebirth)]

[3. Thryssia SS-Rank (Law of Dominion)]

[4. Lumea SS-Rank (Spectral Legion)]

[5. Nysera SS-Rank (Eclipse Dominion)]

Atlas could now reallocate the resources and forces of every lord under his command. That meant he could strengthen one lord’s forces with the support of another. And even redeploy powerful subordinates like Dullorak and Everburn to assist other teams where they were most needed.

He had only one goal in mind: to finish this entire event with a perfect score. And he was absolutely determined to make that happen.

Next in line were Katriel, Lumea, Thaliera, and Luna. Each preparing to bare their fangs in the Offense Category.

A few days later, when the next round of battles was ready to begin, Atlas once again stood across from Bolin in the familiar battleground. He knew this time would be different. The moment was approaching when he himself would also have to step into the fight.

Reaching out, Atlas took one of the shining coins from the virtual table.

"Say your goodbyes," Bolin said with a smirk. "Because this will be the last time you’ll ever get to wish luck to those foolish lords you’re sending into battle, Atlas."

Atlas smiled faintly. "You’re making me nervous, Elder Bolin. Almost afraid."

Yet there was not a trace of hesitation in his eyes.

He flicked the coin across the table, signaling the start of the next match.

Katriel, with her ability Predator’s Memory, would be the first to enter the battle.

She had never once lost a Lord Battle. Her record was flawless.

The only question now. Could she keep it that way in this one?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 386 386 - Insects in the Sky
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Atlas won five defenses in a row in the previous category. And clearly, those were major victories. After all, their opponents were alliances with a greater number of lords. On top of that, their average levels were also higher.

Even so… in that previous category, all five lords who attacked Atlas were only rank 2. Not a single one of them had reached rank 3.

So, from this fact alone, what can actually be concluded?

Perhaps it shows that strategy in lord battles is truly complex. There are many types of attacks and defenses, and each lord has their own unique ability system. All of this creates its own layers of difficulty and depth.

However, one small conclusion can still be drawn. Maybe… focusing purely on defense does come with a lower level of difficulty compared to those who try to balance both defense and offense at the same time.

Just imagine when a lord brings their army to attack an enemy floating island. They are not only facing the enemy troops, but also the enemy's defensive structures, along with the island's environment itself, which they know nothing about beforehand.

That is why some lords, who intend to take the safer path, choose to fortify their island and strengthen their defenses instead. They focus solely on winning and collecting rank points through defensive mode alone. Exactly as Lyrassa did for five hundred years.

Atlas lifted his face as the battle was about to begin. What appeared on the massive floating screen before him were countless swirling portals forming above the enemy's island.

There were truly many variations when it came to attack strategies. Would they focus on aerial assaults, or ground attacks? Would they spread their troops to strike from multiple angles, or concentrate everything into a single point with overwhelming numbers?

Each of those strategies carried its own strengths and weaknesses, all of which depended on the condition of the opponent they were facing.

Moments later… on the massive screen, countless shapes emerged from the swirling portals. They were small in size, yet it was clear they were large insects, their bodies pitch black.

Bolin looked stunned at the sight. He should have known that this type of attack could only belong to one of the figures under Atlas's command.

Atlas had deliberately sent Krythalis to help reinforce Kate's forces.

Kryth immediately released his insect troops, which spread rapidly across the entire island. However, it seemed the enemy also had units prepared to counter aerial attacks. Soon after, dozens, even hundreds of soldiers shot upward from below, using their swords as footholds.

Bolin lifted his gaze. "You will not win through an air assault, Atlas."

Soon after, the sky was greeted by darkening clouds. In just moments, tornado winds formed across several areas, while thunder roared through the air. This was a clear sign that Krythalis would soon be surrounded by hundreds of enemy troops.

However… something else happened. From the portals swirling in the sky, figures suddenly burst forth. Most of them were standing atop their flying swords as they shot forward.

Bolin lifted his face once more and looked toward Atlas. Atlas simply responded with a smirk. The troops riding those flying swords were units once raised by Bolin himself, now serving under Atlas's command.

Their numbers were indeed smaller. But Atlas's goal was never to fight or dominate the aerial battlefield. They were meant to act as bait, stalling the enemy's flying forces. By using Krythalis's power to rapidly map the island and control the skies, they were practically untouchable up there.

Because… moments after that… a massive portal on the ground suddenly erupted as hundreds of troops poured out, launching an attack from a single point.

Kate, in her massive beast form, led the assault. She surged forward at the very front, bringing hundreds of soldiers with her. Their goal was clear. Crush the enemy from one side, then slowly push inward toward the central area.

Bolin was clearly unsettled by what he saw. Because in truth, the strategy used by Atlas and Kate was extremely risky.

Sending such a large force to attack from a single angle was dangerous. If the enemy possessed high explosive traps, powerful cannons, or any wide area abilities, then Kate and her army would be in serious trouble.

However, that was exactly why Atlas had lent Krythalis to the cat girl.

With Krythalis's ability to scan the area rapidly using his army of insects, Atlas could execute this strategy with confidence.

Bringing hundreds of troops to strike from one side of the island created overwhelming offensive power due to their focused numbers. Meanwhile, even though the enemy was on the defensive, their forces were scattered. Most of them were still occupied holding back the aerial assault.

"You have carried out an incredibly foolish strategy. You have no idea how many of your people you have sent to their deaths, Atlas!" Bolin said firmly.

Atlas merely smirked in response to Bolin's sharp glare.

"At this point, I think you should stop talking nonsense, Elder Bolin, and start taking this seriously. I have already won far too many times. And this time, I am confident my troops will win again," Atlas replied, his smirk thinning but never fading.

Bolin clenched his fist. It was becoming painfully clear that Atlas's strategy was working extremely well.

Krythalis and the aerial forces dominated the skies, disrupting the enemy's focus and spreading chaos without engaging in direct combat. Meanwhile, Kate on the ground struck with overwhelming force, crashing through everything in her path and destroying whatever stood before her.

With Krythalis on their team, it felt as though they had gained near-perfect control over the enemy's island.

Until… sometime later, the battle finally came to an end, and Kate successfully secured the first victory of the current category.

Kate even managed to send out their assassins, successfully locating the enemy Lord with Krythalis's help. And because the opposing Lord was a support type with very limited personal defense, they were easily defeated.

Atlas then calmly took another shiny coin and tossed it onto the virtual table, landing it right in front of Bolin.

The assault continued without pause, without any break at all. Next up was the turn of a lord who was the original leader of this alliance.

Luna.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 387 387 - Luna, Thalia, Lumea
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Based on statistics alone, Luna and her army, along with her system ability, were exceptional. Even though it was only S Rank, it was capable of covering her forces in massive numbers.

Luna was a lord who ranked third overall in total statistics within Atlas's alliance. Her troops were that versatile and that mature in combat.

Some time earlier, Luna had suffered a major defeat from an attack by a lord under Bolin's alliance. However, that loss happened because the enemy brought forces and support far greater than what Luna could handle at the time.
