He reached into his inventory and pulled something out, holding it calmly in his palm. A small capsule.

Without hesitation, Atlas placed it into his mouth and swallowed it.

A relaxed smile appeared on his face.

It was a Rank A item. A Stamina Recovery Elixir. Fortunately, Atlas had not wasted these items carelessly. Moments like this were exactly why he had saved them.

He spun his spear once more as stamina surged back into his body. Strength flowed through him, and he could feel that he was ready to fight at the same level as before.

His mana would still take time to recover, but it was enough. More than enough to continue clashing with the lord standing before him.

Morvain stared at him, his expression changing the instant he understood what had happened.

"Oh? Morvain," Atlas said lightly. "Why do you look so panicked? Is something wrong?"

"You!" Morvain roared, his voice shaking with fury. "You damn bastard! Do you think this changes anything? I will crush you! I will tear you apart, break you piece by piece, and make you regret ever standing here!"

Atlas’s smile did not fade.

If Morvain truly possessed three SS Rank abilities, then Atlas had something else entirely. An SSS Rank ability that granted him access to countless powerful items.

Consider what he had consumed just one small advantage. One that could change the entire battle.

Atlas lowered his stance and leveled his spear forward.

"So," he said calmly, "shall we continue? You are not tired yet, are you?"

Morvain roared in fury, his shout brutal and feral. His body flushed deep red as it began to swell in size. Muscles bulked unnaturally, veins standing out as his frame grew larger. His eyes turned fully crimson, burning with rage.

They clashed again in the next instant.

Blow after blow collided at terrifying speed. The impacts came so fast that the air itself seemed to shudder.

They stood almost in one place, trading strikes endlessly, neither of them showing even the slightest wound. At times, they leapt to the side, adjusting their positions to keep the fight flowing and maintain their rhythm.

Atlas kept charging in from different angles, attacking without pause. Whenever his mana recovered enough, he unleashed hundreds of lightning bolts into the sky, calling them down in violent storms that crashed toward his enemy.

The battle dragged on.

Atlas clenched his teeth. He knew that every hit he took was also being transferred to Lyrassa.

This had to end. It had to end now.

Then, after what felt like an endless exchange, the final strike happened.

Atlas thrust forward.

The Wavebreaker Spear pierced straight through Morvain’s chest, clean and precise.

For a brief moment, everything went still.

Golden lightning continued to crackle and roar around them, filling the air with light and thunder. The spear was clearly lodged through Morvain’s body.

Yet Morvain remained standing.

His eyes were wide, empty, unfocused.

"How can you endure all of this," he muttered weakly, "without sacrificing anything..."

"Someone who does not value the lives of their subordinates will never understand," Atlas replied calmly. "Not even if I explain it to you."

Atlas pulled his spear back in a single sharp motion.

Morvain swayed for a moment, still standing.

Then a clean slash followed.

The man’s eyes widened in shock as his head was severed from his body. It flew through the air, hit the ground, and rolled to a stop.

His body remained standing for a brief second longer, headless and silent, before finally beginning to fall.

The Wavebreaker Spear vanished from Atlas’s grasp as a chain of system messages appeared before his eyes.

At the same time, the massive domain that had imprisoned them began to crack and shatter, breaking apart piece by piece.

In the end, Morvain’s defeat came down to one thing.

He lost the battle of stamina and mana.

He could no longer continue fighting, while Atlas still could.

That was the deciding factor.

But...

What about Lyrassa?

Was she alright?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 400 - Verdant Highborn
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

A great victory for Gacha Haven.

Atlas had successfully slain the enemy lord, and with that final blow came the right to claim everything on the island as his own.

Soon after, Atlas and the entire Gacha Haven force were transported back to their own island.

Several system messages appeared before him. They were related to rank points and the process of island merging. Atlas barely glanced at them. He passed the authority to handle those matters to his second in command, a right he had already granted to Edrik.

There was something far more important. Something Atlas could no longer delay.

"Where is Lyrassa?"

Atlas broke into a run, heading toward Luminaria Grove. The area was surrounded by young trees, with gardens stretching neatly to the left and right.

Then he saw someone standing beneath the shade of the trees ahead.

Someone he had not seen for the past two months.

"Lyrassa..."

The name slipped from his lips as a quiet murmur. He could clearly see that she was holding something in her arms.

Atlas ran faster, stopping only when they stood face to face. The woman looked at him and smiled. It was the same smile he remembered so clearly.

"Lyrassa," he said again, stepping closer. He wrapped an arm around her gently from the side, careful not to disturb what she was holding.

"I was gone for so long," she said softly.

"I am glad you came back," he added.

Then Atlas lowered his gaze to what she was holding.

A baby.

The child had light blond hair, similar to Lyrassa’s, and a calm, peaceful expression. Atlas’s breath caught slightly.

"He is..." Atlas began.

"Yes, my lord," Lyrassa replied softly. "Our baby."

Atlas carefully took the baby into his arms. He supported the child’s head with his right arm, gently rocking him as the baby slept peacefully, unaware of the chaos and sacrifice that had brought this moment to life.

For the first time since the battle ended, Atlas truly felt at peace.

"He looks so... peaceful," Atlas said softly.

Lyrassa nodded gently.

"Will he grow quickly, like Vienne did?" Atlas asked.

Lyrassa looked at him for a moment, then let out a small laugh.

"He will grow normally," she said. "But, compared to humans, he will grow faster."

Atlas smiled and chuckled quietly. "I suppose I really need to throw away any human standards when it comes to them," he said.

He stayed there, watching the baby’s face. After a short while, the child slowly opened his eyes, revealing a pair of green eyes that looked calm and pure.

The baby let out a sound. It was soft and gentle. A baby’s voice, yet it carried a melody that sounded strangely beautiful to Atlas’s ears.

"His voice is very beautiful," Atlas said quietly.

"Yes," Lyrassa replied with a warm smile. "He is beautiful. I know he will grow into someone brave and kind, just like his father."

Atlas fell silent at her words.

She hoped their child would be like him?

He grew quiet again, his thoughts drifting. He did not want either Vienne or this child to be dragged into the world of lords. A world filled with endless wars and constant threats.

Could they not grow up normally? Without being surrounded by battle, without having to carry the weight of conflict from such a young age?

As those thoughts crossed his mind, system messages suddenly appeared before Atlas’s eyes.

[A new life has been born under your lineage.]

[Race Identified: Verdant Highborn (Unique Hybrid)]

[Lineage: Crimson Storm Sovereign (Atlas) × The Floral Healer (Lyrassa)]

[Innate Talents Unlocked:]

[Sovereign Seed (SSS) - Bloom Manifestation (S) - Calm Mind of the Grove (A)]

[Sovereign Seed: A dormant core containing both storm and nature authority, capable of evolving into a personal domain in the future.]

[Bloom Manifestation: Can manifest vines, roots, or floral constructs instinctively when threatened.]

[Calm Mind of the Grove: Mental state remains stable under stress, fear, or pain, greatly reducing panic and emotional corruption.]

"Verdant Highborn?"

Atlas paused, carefully reading the race name.

Lyrassa then explained calmly. "It is a higher form of human race. It stands on the same level as a High Human, equal in status, but with a far stronger affinity toward nature and spirits. Their bodies resonate naturally with life, growth, and balance. In simple terms, he is closer to the world itself than ordinary humans could ever be."

Atlas nodded slowly. "Look at you," he said with a light laugh, speaking to the baby. "Not only did your sister surpass her father, but you did too. You have already gone beyond the human race itself."

He looked back at the system details, studying the talents listed there. His eyes paused for a moment.

There it was.

A talent ranked SSS.

At a glance, Atlas could tell that the baby possessed a core that carried both Storm and Nature at the same time. And another talent caught his attention.

Calm Mind of the Grove.

It felt like the complete opposite of Vienne.

If one embodied chaos, then this child embodied tranquility.

Atlas smiled faintly, a hint of anticipation in his eyes.

He could not wait to see how they would interact in the future.

"I want you to give him a name, my lord," Lyrassa said softly.

Atlas looked back at her and fell silent for a moment. Truthfully, he had been thinking about that ever since he held the baby in his arms. But... he did not have many options.

Storm?

Nature?

"He will most likely have abilities similar to mine," Atlas said slowly, "combined with yours as well."

Overwhelming damage, paired with protection for allies. A terrifying yet beautiful combination. Power meant to destroy, and power meant to preserve.

"We need a name that sounds firm and powerful," Atlas continued, letting out a small laugh. "But it also has to carry meaning. Something that truly represents strength. That is not an easy choice."

Lyrassa smiled and answered in a calm voice.

"A name is not just a sound," she said gently. "It is a prayer. A hope. It carries the wishes of the one who gives it. From a father, a name is a promise. A quiet blessing, spoken only once, but meant to last for a lifetime."

Atlas nodded slowly, then spoke a name softly under his breath.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 401 - Caelwyn Blackthorn
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Caelwyn," Atlas said softly. "Caelwyn Blackthorn." He made his decision at last, choosing from all the names that had crossed his mind.

"Cael," he added with a small smile. "We can call him Cael. What do you think?"

"Yes, my lord," Lyrassa replied warmly. "It is a beautiful name. Cael."

At the same time, the baby reacted. A soft, gentle laugh escaped his lips, his green eyes still calm and peaceful.

Then something changed.

A faint green aura began to rise from Caelwyn’s small body. It was subtle, yet clearly visible, spreading outward and softly enveloping Atlas and Lyrassa in a gentle, tranquil glow.

[Lineage Synchronization Initiated.]

[Core Harmony Established.]

[Elemental Affinity Update:]

[– Air Element Affinity: Medium → High]

[– Fire Element Affinity: Medium → High]

[– Light Element Affinity: Low → Medium]

[Sync Progress Established:]

[– Parent ↔ Child resonance created.]

[– Mutual growth feedback enabled.]

[– Shared recovery effect activated when in proximity.]

[System Note:]

[This child represents a rare equilibrium between destruction and preservation.]

[Growth path classified as Non-Standard. Observation recommended.]

More system messages appeared before Atlas. He glanced through them briefly and realized just how great the blessing was that came with Cael’s birth. Even his Light element affinity had increased, rising from Low to Medium. That alone was remarkable, especially since Light was one of the hardest elements to improve.

Atlas gently rocked the baby again. Cael laughed softly, his expression calm and content.

"You like that name, Cael?" Atlas said quietly.

"You also have an older sister here," he continued with a faint smile. "You must be eager to meet her, right?"

At that moment, Atlas finally noticed his surroundings.

More and more figures were approaching from every direction. Some emerged from between the trees, others walked in from the main road. Before he realized it, many familiar faces were already nearby.

Edrik stood among them, along with the others. Mira waved enthusiastically at him, her smile bright and full of excitement.

Atlas turned toward them and spoke clearly.

"Caelwyn Blackthorn," he announced, "welcome the newest member of Gacha Haven."

For a brief second, there was silence.

Then, voices rose together.

"Welcome to Gacha Haven!"

"Welcome, Caelwyn!"

"May you grow strong and safe!"

"Welcome, little lord!"

Laughter and warm smiles followed, filling the grove with life and celebration.

From the direction where the elite subordinates were standing, they slowly stepped aside. The other forces followed, shifting and opening a clear path. Then, from within that space, someone came running.

A young girl, about five years old, with red hair braided behind her back and dressed in leather with light armor, ran forward with a bright, joyful expression. Behind her, Morganna walked calmly, following at an unhurried pace.

"Papa! Papa!" Vienne shouted with pure excitement.

Atlas lowered himself into a crouch and gently turned the baby toward his older sister, whose eyes were shining with anticipation.

Vienne leaned closer, her eyes wide and sparkling with happiness.

"Papa, what is his name?" she asked eagerly.

"Cael. Caelwyn," Atlas said. "Cael, this is your sister, Morravienne."

"Cael..." Vienne repeated, smiling brightly. "I like it. Cael... that is such a cute name."

She laughed, then leaned in and rubbed her cheek gently against the baby’s cheek.

"Cael, grow up quickly, okay? Later we will run along the cliffs, jump from there, it will be super fun. And we will fly high, and fight monsters too, that is really fun. I can fight with a sword. You should pick a weapon later too. But not a sword, let me have that. I am stronger," she said proudly, then tilted her head in thought. "But Cael, what kind of weapon do you think you will like?"

Vienne spoke with such overflowing enthusiasm that Atlas could not help but laugh.

He wrapped his left arm around the girl while still holding Cael securely in his right.

In that moment, Atlas felt the depth of the love in his heart as both children rested in his arms. The calm and strength that filled him were unlike anything he had felt before.

This was love. Pure, unconditional love. The kind that made Atlas willing to give everything, to sacrifice everything, for them both.

If Vienne had already pushed Atlas to grow stronger and keep moving forward, then Cael’s presence pushed him even further. The power he felt inside himself grew deeper and more resolute.

Atlas knew he would continue to grow stronger and overcome every challenge that awaited him in the future.

Holding Vienne in his left arm and Cael in his right, Atlas finally turned to face everyone in Gacha Haven, who were cheering and calling out the baby’s name.

Others began to approach as well. Mira came closer, followed by Serenith, who looked perfectly fine. The sight of her erased Atlas’s lingering worry, knowing she had likely fallen from a great height earlier.

Elyndra and the others joined in, gathering around to share the joy of that day.

Yet one person remained apart.

Morganna.

The vampire queen stood there without any expression. She glanced briefly toward Atlas, then turned away and walked off, leaving without a word.

Atlas simply nodded softly. He understood her nature well enough.

After Atlas’s great victory in the battle, it was followed by the birth of Cael.

A triumph upon a blessing.

Still, Atlas knew one thing clearly.

Even though he had won decisively in terms of score, the war was not over.

There was still one final category remaining. A stage where every lord involved in the war would engage in an all out confrontation.

The enemy side still had more than one Rank 3 lord. And one thing was certain.

Bolin, the leader of the opposing alliance, would also step onto the battlefield in the final clash.

And one thing was absolutely clear.

Atlas had to be ready.

Because when the final battle came, he would be the one standing face to face against Bolin.

"Let’s celebrate this moment with some gacha pulls tonight, and a small party as well," he said afterward.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 402 - Earthbound Colossus
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Atlas stood once more atop the ceremonial plaza. The sight before him made him pause for a moment.

The population of Gacha Haven had grown far beyond what he remembered. It had multiplied several times over. They had now surpassed one thousand residents.

From the final victory against Morvain alone, Atlas had gained three hundred and forty combatants, along with seventy five workers.

It turned out that after the battle ended and Morvain was slain, the dense forest on that island began to change. Some of the trees released those who had been trapped within them.

It was a truly horrific method of warfare. The enemy lord had sacrificed his own people, turning them into living shields. No matter the justification, war always demanded sacrifices. But this was different.

Those people had been imprisoned, forced to receive damage meant for others. They had no way to defend themselves. No way to fight back.

From Atlas’s side, if not for Lyrassa, who endured an overwhelming amount of damage on her own, the worst possible outcome would have become reality.

Atlas might have lost her.

It was a cruel way to fight. A despicable one.

Fortunately, Lyrassa had been under a powerful blessing after giving birth to Cael. Her regeneration had increased many times over compared to her normal state, allowing her to survive what should have been impossible.

Atlas let out a quiet breath and checked his inventory again.

Standard Tickets.

He decided he would only use those. Atlas had deliberately saved his Exclusive Tickets, especially since once the current Lord Battle event ended, a new exclusive character banner would surely appear.

He wanted to be prepared.

And he was.

Atlas had seventy Standard Tickets.

That was a considerable amount.

Previously, he had stopped pulling after obtaining a Rank S character, Krythalis. That meant he was no longer in a safe guaranteed state for another Rank S pull.

Even so, Atlas had no intention of using all his tickets. He decided he would spend only forty.

For now, that would be enough.

Atlas stood at the front, with Lyrassa just a short distance behind him, Cael resting gently in her arms. Vienne was there as well, unable to stay still, hopping around in excitement every few seconds, clearly overwhelmed with joy at having a new little brother.

Atlas raised ten tickets into the air.

"Today, we are celebrating many things at once," Atlas said loudly. "The birth of Cael. The arrival of more combatants to our side. More workers who will strengthen Gacha Haven. And our temporary victory in the Dominion Convergence."

With a grin, he tossed the tickets upward. "Let’s make a small celebration out of this."

"FIRE!!!" came a thunderous shout from the crowd.

The ten tickets spun rapidly in the air above them.

How long had it been since Atlas last did this? It felt like a very long time. He had deliberately delayed pulling as much as possible. He knew that the higher his level, the more advanced his class, and the greater his rank as a Lord, the better the quality of rewards the gacha would yield.

But today was special.

Today deserved it.

The tickets in the air began to change color, one by one.

PURPLE!

"FIRE!" the crowd roared.

An incredible start.

BROWN. BROWN. BROWN. PURPLE!

"FIRE!!!" The voices echoed even louder across the plaza. There were far more people now than before, and their excitement was impossible to miss.

When all ten tickets finally revealed their results, Atlas let out a small breath.

Three Rank A pulls.

On the very first attempt.

That was impressive luck.

Atlas smiled faintly. "Let’s see what the gacha has in store for us tonight," he said quietly.

The night was still long.

Atlas quickly opened the seven brown tickets first, not wasting a second. He stepped forward toward the first Rank A result floating in the air. He reached out and touched the glowing ticket, then deliberately stepped back.

The ticket began to shake violently.

It crumpled inward, as if something invisible was gripping it tightly. Then it stretched, twisted, and unfurled, rolling outward until it transformed into a scroll suspended in midair. A golden aura radiated from it.

[You have received 1 Unique Skill Scroll: Earthbound Colossus]

[Skill Description:]

[The user channels Earth element energy to manifest multiple massive earth hands that rise from the ground and crush enemies across a wide area.]

[The number of manifested earth hands can reach up to 100, depending on the user’s affinity with the Earth element and the advancement level of the skill.]

[Each earth hand possesses overwhelming crushing force and area control, capable of suppressing and annihilating large scale enemy formations.]

Atlas narrowed his eyes slightly as he read the description.

He briefly explained the skill to the others. "Unfortunately for you all, this one suits me perfectly. Unless someone here has a higher affinity with Earth and its advanced forms than I do?"

A wave of disappointed groans followed, though Atlas knew they were only joking.

Still, this skill truly was a perfect match for him. His Earth affinity was already high, and on top of that, he also possessed affinity with Magnetic Earth. This skill would allow him to use its full potential.

Even at this point, Atlas already had many skills with a massive area of effect. But having more than one option, especially with different elemental variations, always carried great value.

It gave him flexibility.

The ability to exploit elemental advantages and weaknesses depending on the enemy he faced.

And in battles between lords, that kind of versatility could decide everything.

Atlas immediately moved on to the next item. He touched the ticket, and it burst into flames almost instantly.

Fire?

The ticket burned away completely, replaced by a small vial floating in the air. Flames flickered gently inside the glass.

"A potion?"

Curious, Atlas reached out and took it. The vial was small, there was only one, and yet it was classified as a Rank A item.

That alone made it suspicious.

Atlas focused, waiting for the system details to reveal exactly what kind of potion this was.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 403 - Emberheart Essence Vial
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[You have received Rank-A Item: Emberheart Essence Vial]

[A condensed flame essence refined from pure elemental fire. Upon use, this vial permanently unlocks Fire element affinity up to High affinity quality for the user.]

A potion that could unlock Fire elemental affinity?

The value of such an item was beyond extraordinary.

Atlas explained the item to everyone, and the reaction was immediate and intense.

Once someone possessed an elemental affinity, they could train and deepen their mastery of it over time. But very few people could unlock multiple affinities in the first place.

Someone like Atlas was an exception. Most of his elemental affinities came from his bonds with Morganna, Lyrassa, and Elyndra.

For others, however, being able to gain an elemental affinity simply by drinking this potion would be nothing short of miraculous.

"I will entrust this to Edrik," Atlas said calmly. "Let the council discuss who would be the best person to use it. Everyone here understands that mastering more than one element is not an easy task."

Atlas knew that firsthand.

Having many elemental affinities did not automatically make someone stronger. In fact, it could slow true mastery. Even Atlas himself had yet to fully deepen every elemental affinity he possessed.

Atlas immediately opened the next ticket.

This time, what appeared was a weapon.

[You have received 1 Rank-A Weapon: Whispering Kusarigama (Unique Grade)]

A duplicate.

Specifically, a duplicate weapon meant for Kurogasa.

At Atlas’s signal, the ninja rat appeared on the stage without a sound. Atlas handed the weapon over and instructed Kurogasa to use it to upgrade his existing one.

A Unique grade weapon carried tremendous value. And in this case, there was no reason to keep it in storage. No one else could properly wield a weapon like this except Kurogasa himself.

For him, this was not just an upgrade.

It was a clear step toward becoming even deadlier in the shadows.

Atlas did not waste any time and immediately threw out the next batch. Ten Standard Tickets.

The tickets spun in the air, rotating rapidly until their colors began to shift. Only one of them turned purple.

Just one Rank A pull this time.

Atlas was not surprised. He never expected a great pull every ten attempts.

He stepped back slightly and opened the glowing purple ticket. The light expanded, then faded, revealing several small vials floating in the air. Each vial contained a swirling silver liquid that moved wildly inside the glass.

[You have received 20 Rank-A Items: Aegisflux Elixir]

[Upon consumption, grants the user increased resistance to all elemental attacks for the next 10 minutes.

This effect does not negate damage entirely, but significantly reduces elemental damage received.]

"Elemental resistance," Atlas muttered.

It was not absolute immunity, but defensive items like this were always valuable. Especially in large scale battles or prolonged confrontations between lords.

And twenty vials at once? That was an excellent haul.

Atlas nodded to himself, already thinking about distribution and usage scenarios.

Then, without hesitation, he tossed another batch into the air.

Another ten tickets spun upward, lights flickering as they rotated.

One by one, their colors changed.

Only one purple again.

Another single Rank A pull.

Atlas quickly opened the nine brown tickets, leaving only one behind.

Moments like this were always filled with anticipation. Even though they were both Rank A pulls, the results could be vastly different.

Just compare it. Twenty potion vials that could be consumed in a short time versus a Rank A character. The value gap could be enormous.

Still, Atlas knew the system’s grading could be... questionable at times. Not that he was complaining.

He reached out and opened the next Rank A pull.

The moment he touched the ticket, a massive surge of fire erupted in front of him.

The flames gathered and compressed, swirling together at the center of the stage. Then something long emerged from within the blaze.

A sword.

A massive one.

[You have received a Rank-A Weapon: Dragonfang Cleaver (Epic Grade)]

Atlas stared at the weapon, his eyes narrowing slightly. This was Karian’s greatsword.

The moment Karian recognized it, he ran onto the stage with clear excitement on his face. Atlas simply handed the weapon over to him.

While there were a few others who could technically wield a greatsword like this, Atlas knew one thing for certain.

Karian would never allow anyone else to use this weapon. Most likely, he would use it to upgrade his existing greatsword. And that was exactly how Atlas wanted it.

The night was still long, but Atlas committed to himself that this would be the last batch. He would only open ten more Standard Tickets tonight. At the very least, he had already gained something significant. A Unique grade skill that he could use personally.

That alone made tonight worthwhile.

He moved forward and tossed the final ten Standard Tickets into the air.

BROWN. BROWN. BROWN.

Atlas watched closely, his eyes sharp, hoping something big would appear. Everyone around him held their breath.

BROWN. BROWN. BROWN.

Seriously? Only one Rank A pull again?

BROWN. BROWN. PURPLE!

"FIREEEE!" Everyone shouted in excitement.

Then...

The last ticket began to tremble longer than the others.

Atlas’s eyes narrowed.

No way!

Its color shifted, glowing brighter and brighter, until it turned into a blazing red.

A deafening roar of cheers exploded across Gacha Haven.

Forty pulls was not a small number, but it was still only halfway to a guaranteed Rank S pull. Even so, Atlas knew this.

Whatever was coming next would be something extraordinary.

He quickly opened the remaining normal pulls, leaving only two floating tickets in the air.

Without hesitation, Atlas stepped forward and opened the first one.

The purple ticket.

[You have received 20 Rank-A Items: Stamina Recovery Elixir]

So far, these elixirs were the ones that had helped Atlas the most. They had carried him through leveling sessions and battles. Even his most recent victory had been made possible because of these elixirs.

Previously, he had only received ten from a single pull. But, this pull yielded twice that amount.

Atlas exhaled slowly. He would be far more careful with these elixirs from now on.

Then his gaze shifted.

The Rank S pull.

Atlas felt a flicker of excitement. He was genuinely curious about what would appear this time.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 404 - Every Shadow will be Erased
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Atlas took a slow breath before opening the highlight of the night, the Rank S pull.

He stepped back slightly as the ticket hovering in the air began to tremble visibly. The bright red color shifted, slowly turning into a vivid green.

"Green?"

A strange sense of hope stirred inside him. Could this be what he was thinking?

The green energy spread outward, flowing into the surroundings. From the floor beneath Atlas’s feet, grass suddenly began to grow at a rapid pace, followed by small flowers blooming all around.

"Is this a duplicate of Lyrassa?"

The swirling energy at the center gathered into a single point, moving faster and faster, until at last, a form emerged.

A crystal sphere appeared, floating in the air, wrapped in a soft green aura, with petal-like shapes resembling flower petals surrounding it.

"A weapon?" he said quietly.

[You have received Rank-S Weapon: Spirit Petal Codex (Legendary Grade)]

"Spirit Petal Codex?" Atlas murmured the name softly, and at once, someone came to mind when he looked at the item.

He turned around and saw Lyrassa standing there, holding Cael in her arms.

"Your signature weapon?"

Lyrassa smiled and gave a gentle nod. "My lord, that’s my bonded relic, the companion that has stayed with me for more than three hundred years. It answered your call for help."

Atlas nodded quietly, then glanced through the skill information bound to the Codex.

[Petalburst Cast: Unleashes a volley of spirit petals that seek out enemies on their own. Each petal strikes with light but consistent damage.]

[Spiritlink Root: Creates a spiritual bond between nearby allies. Any excess healing received by one ally flows through the link, distributing recovery evenly among all connected targets.

[Verdure Ascension: Greatly increasing regeneration and movement speed for the entire team. The battlefield itself responds, amplifying recovery effects for the duration.]

The relic clearly elevated Lyrassa’s abilities, expanding both her damage output and her healing influence across a wider range. It was a weapon of remarkable value.

Atlas nodded once more and spoke to everyone present. "Tonight has granted an extraordinary item to Lyrassa, who has also brought Cael safely into the world today. This is a blessing worth celebrating."

The night’s celebration ended with loud cheers echoing throughout all of Gacha Haven.

A truly incredible gacha pull session, wasn’t it? More rank S pulls paired with their signature relics would always be welcome news.

**

There were still at least five battles remaining before the current Lord Battle season concluded, and all of them would be full-scale confrontations.

Atlas found himself once again on the small floating island where he and Bolin usually faced each other to announce matchups and finalize battle arrangements. The next battle was scheduled to begin later that very day.

The five lords selected for this final category were Atlas himself, bearing an SSS-Rank system ability, and four others, each possessing SS-Rank system abilities.

[1. Atlas, SSS-Rank (Ultimate Gacha)]

[2. Arveline, SS-Rank (King’s Rebirth)]

[3. Thryssia, SS-Rank (Law of Dominion)]

[4. Lumea, SS-Rank (Spectral Legion)]

[5. Nysera, SS-Rank (Eclipse Dominion)]

These five were considered the strongest lords available, each commanding a powerful army that Atlas could rely on in the upcoming battles.

However, there was a clear problem.

All five lords in this category had already revealed their abilities in the earlier rounds. That meant the opposing side had ample time to study them and understand both their systems and combat styles.

Worse still, because Atlas was currently leading in score, Bolin was granted the right to decide which lord on his side would challenge which lord from Atlas’s lineup.

This placed Bolin in a highly advantageous position. He could freely assign lords with system abilities and combat strengths specifically chosen to counter any lord Atlas fielded in this round.

Worse still, three of the lords in Bolin’s lineup had already reached Rank Three. Atlas had also received intelligence suggesting that the remaining two were strong enough to rank up to Rank Three at any time.

In short, this would be a battle against lords whose armies all averaged above level one hundred and fifty.

This was truly a situation stacked entirely against him. Every condition favored the enemy. Atlas was fighting from a position of complete disadvantage.

Bolin spoke less than usual this time. Instead, he calmly reached out and picked up the shining coin resting on the virtual table between them.

Bolin said coldly. "This is not a game anymore. This is the real battle. Your forces will be crushed, your lords will fall, and what you call hope will die right here. I will make sure your defeat is absolute."

Atlas looked at him without flinching.

"I am honestly impressed you still have the energy to spew such meaningless nonsense," Atlas replied flatly.

Bolin merely smirked, then flicked the coin across the table toward Atlas. It slid to a stop beside Nysera’s coin.

From the lord Bolin had chosen, Atlas immediately understood that this time, the man was not holding back at all.

"So, that lord chooses to fight in the dark?" Bolin said with a mocking tone. "Hiding behind shadows like a coward. Fitting. When the light fades, so will his courage."

On the massive floating screen in front of Atlas, two distant islands began drifting toward one another. As they closed in, a war zone rapidly formed between them.

The battlefield was enormous. Land rose as if pulled from the clouds themselves, shaping into wide, flat terrain. Natural features emerged across it, forests, elevated ridges, and even a large lake.

This would be a full-scale, all-out war on neutral ground.

There would be no surrender here. The battle would end only with the death of one lord.

"You really think the Light Element is something special, don’t you?" Bolin said coldly.

"Let me shatter that illusion. Light means nothing to me. I call it as easily as breathing. If I desire it, I can even pull the sun itself into this battlefield."

A chilling smile slowly formed on his face.

"When the light floods this battlefield, every shadow will be erased."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 405 - Even the Eclipse Trembled
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Atlas stood with Luna and Tessa as the battle between Nysera and the opposing force finally began.

Nysera, dressed in her assassin outfit, stood on one side at the edge of a cliff. In front of her stretched a vast open field, followed by forests and even mountain ranges beyond. It was an enormous battlefield, far wider than anyone could have expected.

The enemy had begun to reveal themselves in the distant horizon. Their army, numbering in the hundreds, maybe even more than a thousand, slowly showed their shapes one by one, clearly ready to attack whatever stood in their way.

The screen focused on Nysera’s face, her expression firm and unshaken, her long black hair tied neatly behind her head. Then, as if the world itself heard the command of this lord, a massive black shadow suddenly spread across the ground.

Atlas noticed the area around him growing darker.

He glanced up at the sky and saw part of the sun beginning to disappear.

Eclipse Dominion had begun to show its power. This was a system ability that allowed the lord to cover the battlefield in darkness, weakening the enemy’s energy flow while strengthening forces aligned with shadow or night.

Normally, it was supposed to be a domain that only enclosed a limited area. But what was happening now was something entirely different.

The bright daytime sky from moments ago suddenly dimmed at an alarming speed. Even the sun looked as if it had sunk halfway, slowly being covered by what seemed to be the moon.

There was no way a system ability could truly cause something like this, right?

Was this an actual eclipse happening in real time? A system ability powerful enough to affect nature on such a scale? Was that really possible?

Luna stood beside Atlas and leaned in closer, whispering to him. "It really is incredible that we can witness a real eclipse like this, all because of a system ability," she said in a soft voice.

"She said this is her ultimate ability," Atlas continued. What that meant was that Nysera had sacrificed something to make this happen.

"The price she had to pay was extremely heavy. And it was not paid by her alone, but by every soldier under her command."

"Nysera," Luna went on, "and everyone involved in this war will not be able to use their abilities for the next thirty days."

It was a truly devastating price to pay.

This was truly a massive gamble. The enemy they were facing was also a rank-two lord, but one who commanded an army with an average level higher than Nysera’s forces.

On top of that, she had suffered a major defeat in the previous battle. Even though she had mostly recovered by now, thanks to reinforcements from Atlas and the others, the number of troops she could bring this time reached around a thousand soldiers.

Not long after, Atlas shifted his perception. He stopped relying on his eyes and switched to detecting his surroundings using his other senses. The world around them had fallen into complete darkness. Even the massive floating screen in front of them was now entirely black, showing nothing at all.

"Nysera has successfully activated her ability," Luna said quietly.

After a moment passed, a light appeared across from him. Bolin’s figure emerged, faint light wrapping around his body, making him visible in the darkness.

Bolin clapped his hands, the sound clear and deliberate. The applause was slow and low. "Truly impressive. You really did not hold back this time."

Atlas, even while submerged in the darkness, responded calmly. "There is no way it is this easy, right? Do not tell me you came here unprepared. In complete darkness, Nysera and her army will dominate the battlefield entirely."

"Hah!" Bolin laughed again. His satisfied and arrogant expression was clearly visible, even in the pitch-black surroundings. "I like your confidence, Atlas. I really do. But of course, we did not come into this war without preparation. We would not be foolish enough to challenge her power without a proper answer."

And just as he said that, something happened.

A light appeared.

It was blindingly bright.

Atlas instinctively looked toward the edge of the floating island, where the intense light had emerged, right from the center of the war zone.

The light was overwhelming, clearly coming from something massive, though its shape was not yet visible.

Then, on the huge screen in front of them, something enormous could be seen forming above the battlefield.

It was a gigantic ball of fire.

It appeared suddenly, rapidly expanding, growing larger with each passing moment.

The complete darkness that had covered everything was pierced by what looked like a miniature sun. In an instant, the battlefield that had been swallowed by shadow was illuminated again, its surface clearly visible.

Atlas had to narrow his eyes from the sheer brightness. And more than that, the heat was intense. Even for Atlas, someone who was used to burning himself, the temperature felt far higher than anything he expected.

Everyone stared with their mouths slightly open. Nysera had brought the presence of an Eclipse into this war, fully covering the sun and plunging the battlefield into complete darkness. And yet, the enemy suddenly answered by bringing their own sun.

Now the war zone was completely bright again. The miniature sun that had appeared so suddenly began to scorch everything beneath it. Trees and anything else within the battlefield started to wither and melt at an alarming rate.

Atlas could feel a thin layer of sweat forming on his forehead.

"I think the battle is ready to begin, Atlas," Bolin said.

Atlas watched closely. Nysera and her army remained in position, not making a single move. Meanwhile, the miniature sun began to rotate. At first it spun slowly, then faster and faster.

From within the miniature sun, massive fireballs were launched at terrifying speed. In just a moment, hundreds, no, thousands of blazing spheres spread across the sky, as if the heavens themselves were about to rain down colossal fire.

Every pair of eyes widened in shock as the countless fireballs streaked toward Nysera and her army.

"Ha ha ha ha!" Bolin laughed loudly, his voice filled with triumph.

"Look at this. This is the difference between us. You thought darkness alone would decide the war. You thought you had already won the moment the sun disappeared. But now, witness it clearly. Even in your precious night, my light will burn everything you stand for. This battlefield, this war, and your so-called advantage, all of it will be reduced to ashes!"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 406 - We Have Many Pets to Feed
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Atlas could clearly see it when hundreds of massive fireballs spread out toward the position where Nysera and her troops were gathered.

It all happened so fast, as the giant fireballs slammed down on them with brutal force.

Explosion after explosion erupted with deafening power. At their position, all they could do was scatter and raise their defenses as best they could.

Thunderous blasts and towering flames shot into the sky, striking again and again, and Nysera’s forces were truly torn apart by the impact. They could only endure and try to avoid as much damage as possible.

At the same time, in this situation, they could not move closer to the enemy to launch any kind of counterattack. They were far too busy just trying to survive.

Luna, standing beside Atlas, showed it clearly on her face. She could not hide the fear and panic anymore. On the other hand, Bolin was openly laughing as he watched the strikes grow more violent and relentless.

"Atlas?" Luna said softly after that.

But there was no response from Atlas.

What happened next was that the miniature sun began to move, drifting closer to where Nysera’s troops were positioned.

The fireballs kept spreading and crashing into the area around them. Trees fell one after another, and loud explosions followed in rapid succession.

Bolin laughed with clear satisfaction. "Do you see this? Look at how you cannot handle it at all, Atlas. The troops do not even need to step onto the battlefield. This cute little sun is more than enough to take care of everything."

However, as those explosions continued, there was one thing that went unnoticed, or perhaps forgotten by some.

The real eclipse that had covered the world around them began to slowly fade away. The real sun started to emerge, little by little, until it was almost fully visible.

It became clear when the shadows around them separated and sharpened again, and the light in that place grew brighter with every passing moment.

Seen from the massive screen, Nysera’s troops had already spread out at high speed. They flooded across the battlefield, slipping past the incoming fireballs aimed at them. Some of them shot into the air, immediately trying to clash with the opposing side.

Their forces were slowly being worn down by attacks from the sky, attacks that did not even require the enemy troops to fight directly.

This could be Bolin’s first victory, or at least that was what he believed. Of course, that was what filled his mind.

Until suddenly, the man’s eyes widened in shock.

Anyone would have been stunned.

There was something floating in the air. Someone. A figure completely wrapped in shadows, with dark tendrils writhing wildly from his back like living tentacles. It was a man, hovering silently above the battlefield.

"Edrik," Luna murmured under her breath.

It was Edrik, and he looked nothing like the man they had known before.

Edrik thrust his hand to the right, and in an instant, a tear split open in reality above the sky. It was as if a dimension had been ripped apart, revealing a clear black vortex spinning within it.

Then something completely unexpected happened. The miniature sun began to slowly pull toward that rift.

Bolin stared in disbelief at the sight.

It was clear that Edrik was struggling to keep the rift open. He clenched his teeth hard, his body hovering motionless as he poured everything into holding it in place.

At that moment, hundreds of fireballs shot straight toward Edrik. But Nysera’s flying troops were already there, blocking every single attack. They raised protective barriers, and some even used their own bodies as shields.

Atlas muttered quietly at that moment, and only Luna could hear him.

"One of the other abilities of Eclipse Dominion is that Nysera can focus her dark power onto a single person. That is why Edrik can do something this extreme. He is trying to pull that sun into his dimensional space."

Luna swallowed hard when she heard that. "But Edrik looks like he is suffering badly up there."

"He is being empowered multiple times right now. His strength is multiplied over and over. And if that miniature sun can be swallowed, then the battle will shift back to our side."

"Attack him! Attack that foolish man!" Bolin shouted loudly after that, his voice filled with frustration and desperation.

What happened next was chaos. Hundreds of massive fireballs were hurled wildly in every direction with brutal force. Explosion after explosion tore through the air.

The miniature sun looked like it was struggling to resist as the rift tried to pull it in.

Edrik hovered there, his clothes clearly torn apart, his body visibly slashed and wounded. He was undeniably being burned by the intense heat of the miniature sun he was trying to erase.

Nysera’s troops who were struck by the attacks fell rapidly as well, all because they were trying to protect Edrik at any cost.

It felt like it went on for a very long time, yet it ended just as suddenly.

The miniature sun vanished from the sky, leaving only Edrik floating there alone, his body severely injured. Even so, his hair still looked perfectly neat and polished.

Bolin stared in shock at what he had just witnessed.

Right after that, Nysera’s forces surged forward onto the battlefield, launching a rapid assault. The opposing troops moved just as quickly to respond.

Edrik was still surrounded by an overwhelming spread of shadow energy.

Everything happened in an instant when the ground suddenly grew dark. It was as if shadows rose from the land itself and rushed violently toward the enemy.

"What is happening this time?" Luna murmured softly.

Those massive shadow tendrils spread across the ground, sweeping through the area at terrifying speed. What followed was horrifying.

The shadow tendrils caught the enemy troops, trapped them, and within moments, they vanished, swallowed completely by the darkness.

The enemy troops vanished one by one across the ground, disappearing at an alarmingly fast rate.

"Where did Edrik take those people?" Luna asked quietly once again.

Atlas only gave a faint, calm smile. "We have many pets to feed."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 407 - Harvesting the Fallen
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

What followed after the miniature sun was swallowed by the rift, along with dozens of enemy troops, was pure war on a massive scale.

Across the war zone, Nysera’s forces charged forward with full force. Explosion after explosion erupted in countless areas, and direct clashes broke out everywhere. Skills were unleashed without restraint, and within moments, dozens of lives were lost across the battlefield.

It was the kind of sight that always cut deep into the heart. Watching people fight, struggle, and fall in war for the same single purpose was never easy. And here, Atlas carried the greatest responsibility, because this devastating outcome was born from a decision he had made.

Even Luna, who was already used to being in the middle of war, lowered her gaze slightly as casualty after casualty fell before her eyes.

Then, some time later, Edrik’s figure could be seen collapsing onto the ground, with several people rushing in to save him.

"Edrik is in critical condition," Luna said.

Edrik truly was in a terrible state. This was the worst condition Atlas had ever seen him in. In truth, Edrik was an assassin, someone who usually fought fast, finished his enemies quickly, and escaped just as swiftly using his countless escape abilities.

"Edrik was the one who offered to take that position," Atlas said calmly. "He knew that Nysera’s ability would heavily boost someone with a Dark Element affinity. There were many options on my list, including Kurogasa, Raze, or even Dullorak. But after careful consideration, Edrik’s ability was the most suitable to handle this war."

After some time passed, the battle finally showed its result. Once again, Atlas’s side emerged victorious. Nysera had succeeded in wiping out the enemy forces, though the losses on both sides were severe.

Nysera lost almost forty percent of her total troops, while the enemy lost nearly seventy percent of everything they had.

At the very least, Nysera would be able to recover herself and her island with the spoils of war gained this time. New resources for her to absorb, and new forces she could take in afterward.

Atlas stood facing Bolin once more after the war had ended. The old man said nothing at all. Had his confidence finally been completely shattered? Or was there something else?

"This war is not over yet, Atlas!" the man finally said.

Atlas understood that well, because the truth was simple. In the final war, a direct clash between Atlas and Bolin was something that could never be avoided.

**

Atlas, Luna and Nysera were together inside a Nimbus Carriage at that moment. The carriage raced through the air at high speed, surrounded by thunderous sounds and bursts of lightning exploding around it.

The carriage cut through the sky toward an island cloaked in darkness and endless lightning in the distance. It was Shattered Ember, still trapped within a state of unceasing storms.

After arriving, they used a teleportation device. Moments later, they found themselves inside a cave, with mana crystals embedded along the walls.

Nysera followed Atlas through the narrow stone corridor for some time, until a wider light appeared ahead. Soon, a massive cavern chamber opened up before them.

There were several other people present inside the chamber. But what immediately caught their attention was the cave itself, filled with monstrous spiders that covered the entire area.

Something new had appeared here as well. Large spider silk cocoons hung all around the chamber, gently swaying, some of them still moving.

These were the enemy troops that Edrik had taken earlier and teleported to this place. He knew that these spider beasts would multiply far faster once they were fed souls from intelligent creatures.

And humans, above all else, were the most nourishing prey for them.

For a brief moment, Atlas felt something strange stirring inside him as he watched all of this. Turning humans into food for these monsters.

Could it really get any worse than this? He had never imagined in his life that he would use his enemies as nourishment for the monsters he raised, monsters he planned to harvest for their web threads to create sets of armor.

"Would you still want to accept the Soulbinding Armor from me, knowing that this is how the spiders get their food?" he asked.

Nysera did not answer. A few moments later, it was Luna who spoke.

"We are forced to live in this world," Luna said calmly. "If it were not them, it could be us in that position. I think there is no way to avoid it."

Still, this situation was a harsh reminder of just how brutal this war truly was, a war that no longer recognized anything called humanity. Human lives were nothing more than statistics here. What mattered most was victory.

And the higher Atlas climbed in rank as a lord, the more he felt himself drifting away from the principles he once wanted to build in Gacha Haven. He had believed his troops were not just weapons, but a family he could lead forward together.

"What about Edrik?" Atlas asked after a moment.

Nysera answered him shortly after. "Edrik is in a fairly severe condition. But he is receiving treatment right now. He should recover, at the fastest, in about two weeks," the girl explained.

There was one special privilege that Atlas gained for winning the battle in the all out confrontation category. He was allowed to transfer resources and troops between lords during this phase.

However, there was also a clear disadvantage. The opposing side had the right to decide who would face whom in the next battle.

This created a real dilemma. Atlas had to carefully allocate key individuals to the right islands, because he could not be certain who would be pulled into the next fight.

It was even possible that Atlas himself would be chosen, especially if Bolin had grown impatient and decided to challenge him directly.

Still, everything was approaching the final stage. The outcome was becoming clearer with every step.

Then, the next battle was announced.

Arveline, an SS Rank with the King’s Rebirth system ability, would face Bolin’s first Rank 3 lord.

Was Bolin moving too hastily here? Going straight after the second strongest potential force within Atlas’s alliance?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 408 - The First Death
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Atlas clenched his fist tightly, holding back the surge of anger inside him. Just minutes after the battle began, a massive clash erupted between Arveline’s forces and the enemy, brutal and overwhelming from the very start.

But what Atlas and Luna witnessed this time felt different, as if the pain struck them directly.

Arveline stood there with ten spears piercing her body from all directions. Her face was twisted in agony. Blood soaked into her armor and mixed with her bright red hair, staining everything in a deep, violent crimson.

Moments later, flashes of light spread across the entire island, clear signs that Arveline had lost the battle.

Atlas turned his gaze toward Bolin, who wore a mocking smirk as he looked back at him. "This is only the beginning, Atlas."

However, Bolin’s expression soon shifted.

Arveline’s ability ensured that she could never truly be killed.

The battle was still won by the lord from the opposing alliance, but it was not a complete victory. They had failed to kill Arveline. She had already returned to her island and was now in a recovery phase.

Some time later, Atlas and Luna were inside a room where Arveline, the girl with blazing red hair, lay asleep on a bed. Several healers stood nearby, working to restore her condition.

"Ah, damn it, I died. That was stupid," the girl said loudly as her eyes slowly opened.

Atlas gave a faint smile at her condition. "Twenty percent of your troops were killed, and another fifteen percent were absorbed by the enemy," he said calmly.

"Yes, damn it, I hate that so much. This was my first death. And honestly, getting stabbed by spears from every direction is not pleasant at all. But it was surprisingly intense, in a way I did not expect," she added, clicking her tongue in annoyance.

Atlas let out a quiet sigh after hearing her words. "Get some rest, Arveline. Your task is done for now."

Was this Atlas’s first defeat?

Arveline would need time to recover from this loss. In truth, the wounds, the pain, and the experience of death itself would surely leave their own scars on her mind.

At least, that was what she had once said about her troops, those who had died and later been brought back to life.

**

Only three battles remained, and there was still one Rank 3 lord whose opponent had yet to be decided. The choice came down to Tessa or Lumea.

In the end, after careful consideration, it was decided that Tessa would take the next battle.

Atlas was given the chance to determine how the next battle would unfold. Because of that, he and Luna soon returned to the same place, watching as the war began to unfold before their eyes.

"The enemy’s level is equal to Tessa and her troops," Luna commented as the battle progressed.

Atlas nodded slowly in agreement. Because of that, Tessa had no choice but to use another absolute rule to control the battlefield.

Even so, no matter how it was viewed, the opponent was still a Rank 3 lord. A level difference of even ten was already a massive gap. And although the enemy’s level was not higher than the one hundred seventies, their average level was still extremely high.

This was what eventually caused the battle to last only a few minutes before it ended.

Tessa surrendered.

Atlas clenched his fist tightly once more when he heard Bolin’s loud laughter, clearly enjoying how his side now seemed to be winning battle after battle.

"Next battle, it’s our turn, Atlas. Are you ready?" Bolin threatened loudly, his voice carrying a sharp edge.

**

Eventually, Atlas found himself once again inside the Noble Meeting Hall, together with the remaining lords.

"We can end this war if I challenge Bolin in the next battle," he stated his intention without hesitation.

"Only Bolin remains as a Rank 3 lord. There is still one more lord who could be tricky, but we can handle that if we allocate a large amount of resources to Lumea in the final battle. And I am confident their alliance will fall into chaos if we manage to defeat their alliance leader as quickly as possible," he said.

No one answered right away after Atlas laid out his decision.

In truth, whether he was ready or not, Atlas had made this decision from the very beginning. He would face Bolin in this war. That clash would decide the final outcome of the great conflict.

Even though Atlas had failed to achieve a perfect score, having suffered two defeats along the way.

No matter what, this war had to be brought to an end soon. Even more so because of the growing questions and suspicions they had uncovered after repeated analysis and deeper investigation.

"Information about Bolin in the past suggests that he once had a different system ability. That could be something we need to consider carefully," Luna said after that.

Atlas nodded slowly.

"Edrik has some analysis on that. There are lords who possess more than one system ability, like Morvain, whom I defeated before. He could switch his system ability before each battle," he explained.

He then continued, "Based on the information we gathered, Bolin has been recorded as having shown at least four different system abilities. At least, that is what we know so far."

Atlas nodded again and went on. "The last ability we know of is that he could create a special sword that can float and be bound to his army. But that does not sound particularly extraordinary. The sword has no outstanding features beyond being able to fly. It is nothing more impressive than Rhaen’s Aetherlift Dominion."

Atlas fell silent for a moment, even though, unlike usual, Edrik was not standing behind him this time.

"But we already have all the information on every system ability Bolin possesses, right, Atlas?" Luna continued.

"Yes," Atlas replied once more. "However, I have a suspicion that his system ability works in a different way from Morvain’s. We might be facing a completely new system ability possessed by this lord. And we will only find that out in the next battle. Let’s see whether my guess is right or not."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 409 - The Sky Will Be Your Grave
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Atlas stepped to the edge of Gacha Haven Island as the war zone began to form before his eyes. A vast expanse stretched far ahead toward the enemy island, which looked even larger than his own.

The war zone took shape as mountains rose rapidly, fitting together like pieces of a puzzle. Trees spread at incredible speed, forming dense forests, while lakes appeared and rivers carved their paths across the land.

In just moments, it was as if an entirely new landmass had been laid out between the two islands, ready to host a massive war.

Atlas stood still as all of his elite subordinates appeared one by one at his sides. Edrik took position to his right. Morganna stood on his left, followed by Lyrasa. Then more figures emerged, Kurogasa, Mira, Karian, and others, until they formed a solid line at the front.

Behind them, the main army of one thousand full combatants lined up in perfect formation. The sheer number of them was staggering.

This was the first and largest war Atlas would ever fight in an all out confrontation.

The battle would begin in exactly thirty minutes, a span of time that now felt dangerously short. Soon, the fate of more than a thousand people on this island would be decided, along with the future of the entire Vanguard Dominion Alliance.

Atlas stood there in his full armor, dark black with glowing blue lines running across several sections. His expression was cold and focused, his sharp eyes locked ahead. He lowered his body slightly, not quite bowing, more like preparing to leap.

Then the ground beneath him cracked.

In the next instant, an explosion of golden lightning erupted around him as his body shot upward at extreme speed.

Within seconds, Atlas was already floating high in the air, surrounded by a raging water aura and violent lightning bursting around his form.

Another small blast followed beneath his feet, from his boots, like a rocket igniting midair. Atlas accelerated again, tearing through the sky and piercing the clouds as he rushed toward the center of the war zone.

Moments later, he came to a stop and hovered calmly in the air once more. The golden lightning around his body slowly faded and went still.

From this height, the vast landscape of the war zone looked small beneath him. The same was true for Bolin’s floating island, visible at the far end of the battlefield.

Between them stood a semi transparent barrier, hovering silently in front of Atlas. It would only fully open once the battle officially began.

Atlas waited there alone, gazing into the distance where he could clearly see Bolin’s forces standing neatly at the edge of their island. They were lined up with discipline, just like his own troops.

Then it happened.

All of the distant soldiers, each wearing red cloaks layered over either light or heavy armor depending on their roles, suddenly launched themselves into the air.

Hundreds, perhaps close to a thousand troops, shot upward at once. They filled the sky in a single surge, like a massive swarm bursting forward and claiming the heavens in an instant.

Right after that, they moved in perfect unison. And within moments, the familiar figure of the old man who had faced Atlas time and time again was now standing directly before him, balanced atop his flying sword.

It was obvious that Bolin was deliberately showing off the scale of the army he had brought this time. Not just their numbers, but their levels as well.

"Nervous?" Bolin spoke, stroking his white beard with a smug smile. "Are you scared, Atlas? You should be scared!" he declared loudly.

"My army has an average level of one hundred and eighty. Average!" he shouted, his voice booming across the sky. "You have already seen how my system ability works, have you not? You have seen how all of my troops can fly freely through the air!"

His eyes burned with arrogance as he leaned forward slightly. "I will crush you with ease. You and your pathetic forces that can only crawl on the ground, staring up helplessly as the sky becomes your grave. You never belonged up here, Atlas. This battlefield was never meant for insects like you."

Even after all the information Atlas had gathered about Bolin, the old man was still trying to emphasize that his system ability was these flying swords.

Atlas could admit that Bolin was indeed impressive in how he utilized that special ability. Even if he was likely able to use other abilities now, the one tied to flying swords still remained bound to those who had already been linked to it.

In fact, some of those people were now standing among Atlas’s own ranks.

Golden lightning erupted around Atlas, electricity crawling over his body as it wrapped around him. His black hair lifted slightly as the energy surged.

"This will not be easy," Atlas said, his expression completely flat, revealing nothing. "It will not be easy. I did not come to challenge this battle without preparing myself for defeat."

He then gave a faint smirk. "I have lost. Twice," he said, his eyes fixed on Bolin. "It seems I was too confident, and it ended with those two losses."

Bolin flew closer after hearing Atlas’s words, until his face was almost pressed against the protective barrier.

"Enough with your boasting?" Bolin said coldly.

"I am only saying this," Atlas continued. "I do not underestimate this war at all, Senior Bolin. You have my respect. I have prepared something big, something very big, and I am not even sure you will be able to stay calm once you realize what it is."

"Now then, why don’t we just see," Bolin replied. "Who will end up with a shocked expression when their neck is cut off after this battle begins."

"A very frightening threat, honestly. But I will finish this great war today," Atlas said calmly. "Now, do not hold back in showing your superior system ability. Remind me again, what is your rank? I ask humbly, since you are planning to kill me anyway."

Bolin laughed and stared sharply at Atlas. "Have you ever heard of someone with an SSS Rank ability before, Atlas?"

"That sounds impressive," Atlas replied.

"You are standing in front of that person right now," Bolin said.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 410 - The Reaper of the Sky
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

SSS Rank ability?

Atlas smirked when he heard that bold claim. Of course, someone with an ability ranked that high would not normally reveal it so easily. Still, it was perfectly in character for Bolin to do so. The man was simply that confident in his power and strength.

On the opposite side, Bolin flew backward, creating some distance. He raised both of his hands and made a sharp motion. In response, countless massive magic circles formed across the sky. Those circles began to spin rapidly, and moments later, explosion after explosion rang out as enormous shapes started to emerge.

Dragon heads.

A deafening roar tore through the heavens as dozens of dragons appeared. Azure dragons, with massive heads and pairs of branching horns, burst forth from the magic circles.

Those ethereal dragons twisted and coiled through the sky, merging seamlessly with Bolin’s flying army, hundreds of troops spreading out across the airspace in front of Atlas.

From below, from the position where Atlas’s forces stood far in the distance, the scene was overwhelming.

Atlas hovered alone at great height, standing atop the semi transparent magnetic platform. Alone.

Before him were hundreds, perhaps close to a thousand enemies, all ready to crush him at once.

Anyone who witnessed this sight would struggle to measure the resolve, strength, and sheer courage it took for him to stand there without retreating.

And instead of breaking morale, the sight only ignited the fighting spirit of those watching from below.

Today, they were ready to give everything they had.

"An ability that steals the abilities of those he defeats," Atlas murmured quietly.

With a sharp gaze, he stared at Bolin, who floated not far from him.

The analysis and suspicions shared by Atlas and his elite subordinates seemed to be correct. Bolin could take the system abilities of any lord he defeated, or more precisely, any lord he killed.

How many system abilities did he possess now? No one knew. But judging by how carefully and strategically he used his powers, there had to be limits.

"Look at how vast my authority is, Atlas!" Bolin roared loudly across the sky.

Atlas stared ahead with a sharp, icy gaze. At the same moment, something strange emerged from his left eye. It moved fast, like black fabric twisting wildly through the air. That shadow cloth swept around and settled behind him, forming a robe that fluttered and snapped in the wind.

At the same time, a surge of blue light and rushing water burst outward. A spirit beast appeared not far from Atlas. Zefyros roared loudly, lightning exploding around its form.

The Arclume Drakeling then shot forward and spun around Atlas. Its body gradually turned into pure energy, wrapping itself around the armor Atlas was wearing. When it finished, his armor had changed, now layered in deep ocean blue with sharp, fin like edges glowing in gold.

A full helmet closed over Atlas’s head.

But it did not stop there.

Wild red and black energy surged next, wrapping tightly around his body and adding another layer to his armor. From his forehead, a pair of crimson energy horns emerged.

The Demonic Reaper had now been activated.

Atlas raised his right hand to the side, and the Legendary Wavebreaker Spear appeared instantly, accompanied by bursts of golden lightning crackling around it.

And still, it continued.

One by one, weapons began to appear behind Atlas.

Swords. Spears. Blades of many shapes and lengths.

One, two, three, four, five. The number kept growing until there were dozens, all floating neatly in formation behind him.

The arrangement of weapons formed the shape of wings spreading from his back.

Over the past period of time, Atlas had been constantly training his Magnetic Earth ability, channeling his mana into thirty two weapons of at least Epic grade. As a result, he could now control all of them at once through his power.

Those long weapons hovered perfectly in place, moving like a pair of massive wings behind him. They were not physically attached to him, yet the sight was undeniably majestic.

"You came this close just to show off your toys, or what?" Bolin shouted loudly from across the sky.

"That armor sure looks way too flashy."

Flashy? This was the darkest form Luna had ever given to anyone.

"You are not seriously planning to stay this close when the battle starts, are you?" Bolin shouted again.

The countdown toward the real battle continued to tick down, growing ever closer. Yet Atlas showed not even the slightest sign that he intended to retreat or hide behind his troops.

"I know this will be an easy war," Bolin shouted once more. "But I have no intention of watching my opponent throw his life away so cheaply."

"At least give me a chance to show just how strong my army really is!" he continued.

Time kept moving forward. Still, Atlas remained hovering high above, golden lightning constantly bursting and crackling around his body.

On the far side, at the edge of Gacha Haven’s main island, the troops were fully prepared. Weapons were drawn across the ranks.

Those who could fly launched themselves into the air, hovering in place, ready to surge forward the moment the order to attack was given.

The elite subordinates were also prepared. Even so, they looked the most relaxed among them all, some not even holding their weapons yet.

Off to one side, Milo, with his messy hair and black suit, nervously shuffled a deck of cards in his hands.

The countdown kept racing forward, and the battle was truly about to begin. At the same time, the sky grew darker as thick clouds swallowed the sun whole.

A deep rumble of thunder rolled across the heavens soon after.

Hundreds of Bolin’s troops showed no intention of holding back. The moment the barrier opened, they would launch a full assault, rushing straight toward the target standing before them.

Atlas lifted his head slightly, his voice booming across the darkened sky.

"Come," Atlas declared. "Bring everything you have. Your numbers, your pride, your so called power. I will stand here and take it all. And today, this sky will remember who truly rules it."

The countdown hit zero.

The protective barrier in front of him shattered instantly, breaking apart like glass scattered into the air.

And in that very moment, the hundreds of enemy troops surged forward at once, unleashing their attacks together, all aimed directly at Atlas.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 411 - The Lightning Warden
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Atlas remained standing upright on the floating platform as time itself seemed to slow down. Hundreds of enemy troops rushed toward him at terrifying speed, their movements blurring together as they closed the distance.

So many attacks at once. Were they truly planning to go all out on him from the very start?

[Legendary – Seraphic Breaker: Dawnfall Lance (Conditional Active Skill) activated.]

In that fleeting instant, two figures suddenly appeared behind Atlas. Two women.

One of them was a woman with long blonde hair, her movements sharp and precise as both of her hands moved in rapid motion. Within seconds, a massive magic circle shaped like overlapping flower petals formed beneath Atlas’s feet.

Like a colossal blooming blossom made of pure energy, the petals rose and wrapped around his body, enclosing him in layers of radiant light.

Beside her was Elyndra, her seraph wings fully spread, glowing brilliantly against the darkened sky.

At the same time, the storm filled heavens split apart. The thick black clouds were torn open, and from that opening, a Lance of Dawn began to descend.

It was a colossal spear of condensed light, forged from blinding radiance.

Dozens, then hundreds of blades made of pure light manifested from every direction, hovering in the air like an execution array, all poised to strike anyone who dared enter its range.

[A dual soul technique that manifests the Lance of Dawn. Light based actions charge the spear. Once fully charged, it is hurled or used in a high speed impalement, detonating in a burst of purified Light.]

The ability continued to charge each time Atlas or Elyndra used a light based spell.

Come closer.

The more of them that gathered in one place, the more devastating the impact of his spell would become.

Come on.

As they drew nearer, crowding the same stretch of sky, Atlas smirked behind his fully closed helmet, his sharp gaze burning with anticipation.

Everything happened in an instant. So fast that it felt as if every event unfolded at the same time.

Because at that very moment, Atlas saw it clearly. A single figure suddenly appeared in the midst of the hundreds of enemy troops.

Edrik.

He appeared out of nowhere, hovering in the air, causing everyone around him to freeze in shock, their movements disrupted for a split second.

Edrik moved with terrifying speed, maneuvering through the air as his dagger slashed outward, as if he were cutting directly through reality itself.

Cracks of dimensional rifts formed instantly, spreading through the air in jagged lines.

A massive explosion followed, throwing everyone nearby off balance and sending bodies flying as control was lost midair.

Edrik vanished just as quickly as he had appeared.

And in the very next instant.

A massive fireball suddenly formed in the center of the sky.

It was the miniature sun that Edrik had previously stored within his dimensional space. He released it once more, this time unleashing it directly upon the enemy troops.

A weapon born from their own power, now turned against them.

Hundreds, even thousands of fireballs filled the sky in the blink of an eye.

The setup was complete.

Wrapped in golden lightning, Atlas launched himself forward at terrifying speed. In that brief instant, he appeared right in the middle of hundreds of enemy troops.

They could only see him for a split second, with no time to react to anything at all.

[Unique – Stormcall Domain (Active Skill) activated.]

[Legendary – Crimson Reaper Dominion (Active Skill) activated.]

[Unleashes a large area execution field where spectral crimson reapers manifest and hunt every enemy within range.]

Violent thunderous impacts erupted instantly. In the same moment, hundreds of enemies within the area felt something latch onto them from behind.

Spectral reapers manifested, clinging to their bodies, slicing them apart with relentless speed.

Atlas surged forward again, moving as one with the lightning.

The war had begun.

Explosion after explosion. Slash after slash.

All of Gacha Haven’s elite forces flooded the sky soon after. Morganna, Serenith, Krythalis, Dullorak, and hundreds of other troops joined the assault.

Screams of pain and roaring detonations filled the heavens, the air saturated with nonstop attacks.

The massive miniature sun now turned back on Bolin’s own forces, tearing through their ranks.

No matter how strong or how numerous Bolin’s army was, the opening setup unleashed by Atlas was devastating.

At the very least, it would wipe out two hundred to three hundred of their troops in those first moments alone.

Atlas fought with absolute brutality. He moved like living lightning, appearing wherever he willed. Every swing of his weapon unleashed thunderous explosions, each strike carrying devastating power.

His thirty two swords and spears shot wildly in every direction, piercing and slashing anyone in their path.

There was no escaping them. Whenever one of those weapons was deflected, it would immediately redirect, flying off in another direction to strike a different target.

Atlas kept his focus locked on Bolin, who was still positioned in the distance, actively casting his skills.
