He surged forward, cutting down five enemies at once in a single pass. A massive explosion followed, yet Atlas did not slow down in the slightest.

Everything happened at blistering speed.

In that same fleeting moment, he caught sight of Morganna not far from him, tearing through enemies around Atlas with ruthless efficiency. Her presence carved open the battlefield, giving Atlas even more space to accelerate and break through the densely packed enemy lines.

With each second, Atlas pushed deeper, tearing a path through Bolin’s massive army, unstoppable and relentless.

After some time, Atlas finally broke through the enemy ranks completely and shot straight toward Bolin in the distance.

Atlas tightened his grip on his spear, preparing to strike. Above him, a massive reaper manifested, its enormous wings spreading wide across the sky, following him.

Bolin stared back with a fierce expression, while twelve massive azure dragons writhed wildly behind him, coiling through the air as if ready to deliver the harshest punishment imaginable.

Dozens, then hundreds of shadow tendrils burst forth from Atlas’s body. They lashed out at terrifying speed, surging forward like predators, rushing to seize Bolin from afar.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 412 - The More, the Better
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Hundreds of shadow tendrils shot out from Atlas, racing through the air toward Bolin in the distance.

But the old man moved with incredible speed, darting away and slipping past the reach of every single tendril.

Atlas burst forward like a flash of lightning in pursuit. Even so, Bolin’s movement was just as fast. Riding his flying sword, the old man weaved through the sky with terrifying agility.

Atlas swung his right hand to the side. Instantly, the thirty two swords and spears following him shot forward, tearing through the air toward Bolin at extreme speed.

The sharp weapons closed in, but every strike was blocked by layers of magic shields cast by the old man, each blow deflected and sent flying away.

Faster.

A burst of golden lightning exploded outward as Atlas accelerated again, finally catching up. He swung his spear straight at Bolin.

Bolin smirked and laughed. "Not that easy, Atlas."

The old man countered with refined martial movements, deflecting Atlas’s attacks with precise motions, blocking every strike head on.

Then he shot away once more.

Atlas chased after him. The two streaked across the sky, moving so fast they were little more than flashes of light.

Above them, the ethereal azure dragons unleashed cannon-like blasts, all firing toward Atlas at once.

Atlas dodged at impossible speed, his movements like lightning slicing through the air, avoiding every single attack.

He was not fighting just one enemy anymore.

In a split second, Atlas appeared directly in front of a gaping azure dragon. He thrust his spear forward, slicing cleanly through its upper jaw.

The dragon vanished instantly, dissipating into nothing before Atlas’s eyes.

Atlas surged forward again. He leapt swiftly between magnetic step platforms, his body zigzagging through the sky at extreme speed, evading every incoming attack with sharp, precise movement.

In a flash, he was face to face with Bolin.

"Not bad," Bolin said. "You are quite entertaining."

Atlas struck immediately, swinging his spear in a powerful thrust sweep.

At the same time, all thirty two swords and spears shot forward together, converging on Bolin from every angle.

But the old man moved beyond expectation. His body shifted with unbelievable precision, deflecting every single strike as if he were creating afterimages of himself, each movement cleaner and faster than the last.

Bolin suddenly lunged toward Atlas and unleashed a rapid punch. His arm split into multiple afterimages from sheer speed.

Atlas blocked with his spear, shadow energy wrapping tightly around the weapon.

The impact sent Atlas flying backward. Yet in the next instant, he reappeared behind Bolin.

The old man stiffened in surprise as Atlas seemed to multiply.

Bolin spun in place, throwing punch after punch. Each successful hit caused Atlas to split again, more duplicates appearing in the air.

Then all of them rushed toward Bolin at once.

Bolin twisted midair and formed a spinning magic sphere around himself. The sphere rotated at incredible speed, releasing a violent force that blasted the duplicates away in every direction.

But one Atlas remained.

That single figure drove his spear forward with flawless precision, piercing straight through Bolin’s heart.

Blood burst from the old man’s mouth. The strike had shattered his mana core. Atlas could feel it clearly.

This was a clean kill.

"Is it over?" Atlas said, a faint smirk forming.

"Not that easy, kid," Bolin replied.

His body dissolved instantly, vanishing from the spear. A moment later, he reappeared far away, floating in the air without his flying sword.

It was clear now. This was one of Bolin’s system abilities.

An ability that allowed him to evade death itself, or at least bypass it entirely.

Whatever the exact mechanism was, one thing was certain.

This was a truly terrifying ability.

"What do you think, Atlas? Are you already getting tired?" the man spoke with a booming voice.

Atlas spun his spear once and took his stance again. This was only the opening, he knew that. And he still had many tools left to use against this man.

He took out two potions from his inventory, one to recover his mana, the other to fully restore his stamina. He could do this many more times if needed.

"Oh? So you are already running out of stamina after doing those cute little moves?" Bolin mocked, flying closer.

"Well, I already killed you once," Atlas replied flatly. "And I did not even break a sweat."

"Killing me once is not enough, Atlas," a voice answered.

But that voice did not come from the Bolin standing in front of him.

Atlas glanced to the right as another Bolin appeared there, floating calmly in the air.

"Maybe you need to kill me four times," another voice echoed, as a third Bolin appeared far to the left.

"Or maybe five times."

"What about six times?"

Then more figures kept appearing without pause. One after another, until there were twelve Bolins in total, all flying and surrounding Atlas from every direction. Behind each of them, an Azure Dragon writhed and coiled through the air.

"However, I will admit this, Atlas," all of the Bolins spoke at the same time. "I am quite entertained. At the very least, this has been a rather enjoyable warm up."

He laughed again, his voice overlapping from every direction. "So, what do you say if we begin the second round? Do you want us to come at you one by one, or shall all twelve of us attack you at the same time?"

A surge of golden lightning roared louder around Atlas, while waves of shadow and boiling blood energy churned violently around his body. He gripped his spear tightly, his stance unwavering.

"Come at me, all of you," he said calmly. "The more, the better."

As his words fell, thunder exploded across the sky, and the clouds darkened even further.

All twelve Bolins rushed forward at once.

[Unique – Stormcall Domain (Active Skill) activated.]

[Legendary – Crimson Reaper Dominion (Active Skill) activated.]

In the same instant, crimson reapers manifested again, each one locking onto a Bolin and the Azure Dragons behind them. Golden lightning tore through the sky, crashing down brutally as it surged straight toward the incoming enemies.

Spectral blades swung. Thunder shattered the air. The battlefield turned into pure chaos.

"I will not fall to cheap tricks like this, Atlas!" Bolin roared, his voice echoing from all directions as the clash erupted head on.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 413 - Twelve vs. Four
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Atlas placed his focus at its absolute peak. Everything sharpened. Every sense within him fully awakened, as if every cell in his body had become an eye, able to perceive in all directions at once.

At this point, Atlas was no longer relying on his vision. His eyes could not keep up with the brutal speed of this battle anymore.

He closed his eyes.

He spun his spear and clenched his teeth as three Bolins charged at him simultaneously from different directions.

Atlas rotated his spear and blocked. A heavy impact exploded outward. He twisted his body immediately after, narrowly evading another attack from the side.

His movements were impossibly smooth. Every rapid strike from Bolin missed by the smallest margin, each violent gust of force brushing past his body.

Atlas leaned left as a crushing punch grazed the right side of his head, clipping his ear.

He shot upward into the sky, his body surging with lightning, and opened his eyes once more.

With a sharp motion, Atlas commanded dozens of swords and spears to spin around him at extreme speed. He then lunged toward one of the Bolins positioned to the side.

Suddenly, a surge of green energy wrapped around Atlas’s body.

A massive tree formed above him in an instant, towering and radiant, casting brilliant light across the battlefield. Its leaves shot outward, slicing through the air toward the ongoing fight.

"Lyrassa?"

In the next moment, Lyrassa twisted gracefully through the air and appeared behind Atlas.

"I saw that you might need my help, my lord," she said calmly.

"With pleasure. Twelve versus two still sounds reasonable," Atlas replied.

But then, shadow energy began to swirl violently nearby as another presence emerged.

A woman with long black hair appeared, her bat-like shadow wings spreading wide behind her, a long scythe resting firmly in her grasp.

"What about three versus twelve?" she said with a faint smile.

Morganna had joined the battle.

Atlas gave a faint smile when he saw Morganna join the fight.

Without wasting a single word, Morganna shot forward at extreme speed, her scythe flashing as she carved through one side of the battlefield. Almost instantly, four Bolins surrounded her, attacking at once.

Above them, the Azure Dragons were struck by razor sharp leaves raining down from the sky, while Lyrassa darted through the air, evading every attack aimed at her with fluid grace.

Atlas surged forward again, attacking with brutal force as he collided head on with his enemies.

Massive explosions erupted. Thunderous impacts echoed endlessly through the sky.

The battle raged on in complete chaos. Forms were no longer clearly visible. Everything moved too fast. Blinding flashes of light, violent detonations, and deafening shockwaves filled the battlefield without pause.

Then, at one critical moment, Morganna unleashed a flurry of savage strikes. Her hand movements were completely invisible, her attacks landing relentlessly.

The Bolin in front of her raised both arms, desperately blocking as he was cut again and again.

"Kill this one!" Morganna shouted.

Other Bolin duplicates rushed toward her, but thick green vines suddenly wrapped around them, binding their movements and stopping them in place.

And in that very moment, something brilliant and overwhelming streaked in from the distance.

The Lance of Dawn.

Elyndra descended at incredible speed, guiding the massive lance of radiant light straight toward them.

Atlas lunged forward at the same time, striking toward Bolin. The old man blocked, only to be assaulted from two sides at once.

In that razor thin instant, the massive Lance of Dawn pierced forward, driving straight toward Bolin.

He raised his hands, catching the tip of the lance with raw force.

Morganna continued cutting down the other Bolins who tried to close in.

Lyrassa, from above, controlled the battlefield, restricting their movements even further.

Atlas flashed forward and swung.

Both of Bolin’s arms were severed in a single clean motion.

In that same split second, the Lance of Dawn drove fully into Bolin’s body. Even its tip alone was enough to leave him frozen in midair, unable to move.

Atlas slammed his spear to the side and stood upright beside Bolin’s rigid form, his eyes wide and his mouth hanging open in shock.

Morganna, Lyrassa, and Elyndra hovered nearby, surrounding the scene in silence.

Atlas kept his gaze averted, never once turning to face Bolin directly.

"Say it," he said calmly. "This is truly the end, isn’t it? Show me more of those cheap tricks from your so-called SSS Rank ability."

"H how..." Bolin spoke, blood pouring heavily and soaking into his white beard. His face had gone pale.

"How did you grow this fast," he whispered, his voice trembling.

"Thanks to you," Atlas replied. "You were the push that forced us to grow this quickly." He smirked faintly.

At the same time, Atlas’s energy began to fade. The shadow power dispersed, the golden lightning died down, and the helmet dissolved, revealing his face and black hair once more.

He returned to his normal armor. Even the spear vanished from his hand. Still, he remained standing atop the transparent platform in the air.

"I cannot accept this. I cannot lose like this," Bolin said again, his voice growing weaker with each word.

At that very moment, a series of system messages appeared before Atlas, confirming his victory in this battle.

The leader of the opposing alliance had been defeated.

Bolin’s body began to fracture, pixel-like energy breaking away from him as his form rapidly unraveled.

"I will not die like this..." his voice echoed faintly.

Then, the old man’s figure vanished completely.

Atlas let out a relieved smile and turned around, meeting Lyrassa’s gaze as she smiled back at him. Elyndra hovered nearby, her expression carrying a trace of concern, while Morganna floated a short distance away, facing the opposite direction in silence.

"We win," Atlas said quietly.

Lyrassa nodded in response.

Atlas then looked into the distance, where the massive miniature sun was still raining fireballs across the battlefield. Moments later, fractures of a rift formed again in the air, forcibly pulling the miniature sun back into its hiding place.

"Just how many more times can Edrik use that mini sun?" Atlas muttered.

After a brief pause, he exhaled slowly.

"Let’s take a rest," Atlas said.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 414 - Frozen Lotus
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Atlas returned to Gacha Haven at once and received the full update on the aftermath of the great war they had just won.

From the victory over Bolin, he gained hundreds of new troops, more than 400 in total. They all stood in neat formation across the open field as Atlas and Edrik faced them together.

This was a massive increase in strength, since every one of them had already passed level 170, well above the average.

However, that day also carried another truth that weighed heavily on him. This might have been the greatest loss Atlas had ever suffered. A total of 367 combatants from his side had fallen in the war.

"Were any of them team leaders or key figures, Edrik?" Atlas asked again, seeking confirmation.

"No, my lord. All team leaders survived," Edrik replied calmly. "But many of those who fell were people who have been with us since the early days."

Atlas nodded slowly. "Make sure we honor them properly. And if they left families behind, ensure their lives are taken care of."

Atlas understood that his rapid progress came with its own drawbacks. The people who followed him from the beginning could not all keep up with the speed and level growth of Gacha Haven.

Those who fell behind would struggle to meet the growing demands, and their risk of defeat in battle would only increase.

Even though Atlas had done everything he could to arm them well and push their levels higher, they still needed time to adjust. Battles at higher levels always required real experience and long hours in the field.

This was never going to be easy. He had known that from the very start.

Still, there was one more major gain from this war. He obtained 73K Gold from Bolin.

Wow. That man really did carry a lot of valuable items.

The territory of Gacha Haven had also doubled in size. Atlas now had far more space to work with for defense. At the same time, it meant much more land that needed to be covered and protected.

Even so, Atlas’s task in the current Lord battle season was finally complete. Only one battle remained, one that should take place soon. And this time, victory was guaranteed to be in the hands of his alliance.

Without wasting any time, Atlas soon found himself once again on the mini floating land where he usually faced Bolin. This time, however, there should have been only one enemy lord left, the last one from the opposing side who still had the right to launch an attack.

A man stood there, wearing a robe of white mixed with icy blue tones. He had long black hair tied behind his back, and his eyes carried a gaze that was both sharp and calm at the same time.

The man walked forward, and Atlas responded immediately, stepping closer as well. Soon, they stood facing each other, not too far apart, though still separated by the transparent barrier between them.

The man then brought his hands together in front of his chest and bowed lightly toward Atlas, a greeting that carried a distinct Eastern style.

"Han Feng of the Frozen Lotus Land," the man said as he introduced himself.

Atlas replied in the same manner, introducing his name and his island in return.

At the very least, this man did not show the same arrogance as the alliance leader Atlas had already defeated.

"I have already defeated your alliance leader. There is no point in continuing this battle. It will only bring more trouble for you," Atlas said calmly after that.

The man smiled faintly and bowed once more. "Indeed, Lord Atlas. I understand very clearly what you are trying to say. However, with all due respect, I am the second in command of the alliance," he said.

"I can see that, considering how Bolin deliberately held you back until the very end," Atlas replied.

"I have a proposal for you, if you are willing to hear it," the man continued.

"Tell me."

"Before anything else, I have already opened access to my island. You or anyone from Gacha Haven may visit and inspect everything there. Troops, buildings, facilities, all of it is open. I will personally make sure that you or your people receive a short tour to understand exactly what my island holds."

Atlas fell silent for a moment, then glanced over his shoulder where Edrik stood behind him. Edrik nodded slowly, clearly understanding what Atlas meant.

After that, Edrik vanished from the spot and immediately moved to accept the invitation extended by the lord before them.

Atlas remained there for some time, doing nothing and simply waiting while Edrik, Kurogasa, and several others visited the enemy island. Their task was to observe everything the opposing lord intended to show, and uncover anything that might matter.

After a while, Atlas began receiving reports from Edrik.

[Edrik: "My lord, this lord is extremely strong. His troops are high level across the board. His defenses are complete, covering land and air without gaps. The island is fully self-sustaining. It can provide food, training facilities, beast units, and all essential resources on its own."]

[Atlas: "Conclusion, Edrik?"]

[Edrik: "In terms of total power, he is almost equal to Bolin. Possibly even stronger."]

Atlas nodded slowly after hearing that answer.

He had the option to reallocate resources and forces from every lord within his alliance and move them all to Lumea for the next battle. However, it was clear that doing so would still result in heavy losses on both sides.

Unless Atlas was willing to deploy all of his Elite Subordinates, victory would not be fully guaranteed.

But even then, this would still be a massive war. Worse, they had no knowledge at all about the system abilities possessed by the lord standing before him now.

"Now tell me," Atlas said calmly, fixing his gaze forward, "what kind of proposal do you want to make?"

The man in front of Atlas showed a faint smile and bowed lightly once again. Then he spoke.

What he said next made Atlas freeze for a brief moment, his expression shifting in clear disbelief.

What?!
